name: ACI Central (Philippine-Australian Arts, Culture and Innovation Central)
address: Shop 4/3-9 Warby St, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.

name: Addison Road Community Organisation
address: 1/142 Addison Rd, Marrickville NSW 2204, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews: It was my first time visiting the Addison Road Organisation and it won't be my only one! I enjoyed walking around and exploring the area. It was much larger than I thought it would be. I liked all the different parts of the grounds, such as the community garden, bbq and picnic area. There is a large patch of grass where various sports can be played. Some small, older buildings are there which are used by artists.

Great organisation with cheap groceries and free bakery products. Veg is also free, but in short supply and often quality is very poor. Many of the volunteers are lovely, but some can be alittle abrupt at times, or seem bored or disinterested. One pushed past me with her trolley without saying excuse me, and caused older lady with a walking stick have to move out of the way awkwardly. A sense of superiority I feel at times. Having said that most are really lovely.

Gorgeous community focused safe inclusive welcoming hub of kindness,  genuine care and generous support for those in need. This is what positive daily impact looks like, this is what humanity looks like!

Food, furniture and lots more.

The Addison Rd Community Centre kind of springs up on you, it's surrounded by houses and the driveway might have you thinking there's not much there. Once you're inside, it opens up to a very large area with multiple shops and other community buildings. The focus is on reused and recycled goods, and the prices are suitably lowered.

I always start with this place whenever I need some new furniture, homewares or other items. You can easily find gifts or arts and crafts supplies in either the Bower repair cafe or the Reverse Garbage centre. There's also plenty of parking and some open spaces with picnic tables.

The Street Food Night Market is great. If your coming with kids, it is a great spot to have a picnic or to set up at one of the tables. It's a super casual vibe. A lovely atmosphere for having some amazing global street food. They have food from everywhere! Filipino bbq, Fijian Indian, Persian sweets, Thai and Syrian food, just to name a few. It's all sooooo good. Def worth stopping in, especially if your looking for a good feed and a relaxing place to chill before heading out for a night on the town.

name: Adhika Cafe
address: 59 Bayswater Rd, Rushcutters Bay NSW 2011, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Finally tried Adhika and it definitely did not disappoint. The place is cozy and nostalgic. It instantly reminded me of my lola’s house, where breakfast always feels comforting and familiar.

I love the creative twists they’ve done on Filipino breakfast classics. Every dish has character and feels thoughtfully put together, not just “instagrammable” but also full of flavour. I had the chilli egg longganisa with bicho, so so delicious, with lots of textures that complemented each other perfectly. The ensaymada was so good, must- try! Love my oat mocha too! Perfect!

You can really feel the passion behind the food. Each plate looks like a piece of art, and the staff and owners are incredibly warm and very friendly. They don’t just serve great food and coffee, they serve it with genuine smiles and conversation. Such a lovely neighbourhood spot.

Second time visiting here and always has the best food and service!!
The sandwich is savory and sweet and full of flavor! The meat has flavor to it self and the pickles definitely add the texture, with the bun super fluffy!!

The ube cake is a must try!! It is pretty dense (not sickening at all) super moisture, not too sweet with natural ube flavor to it since it uses real ube paste without nasties. Definitely going back again!!

Adhika is the kind of place you stumble into once and immediately know you are coming back. We arrived in Sydney needing coffee and ended up finding a true hidden gem. Adhika means “dream or vision,” and this Filipino cafe lives up to the name in every way.

On our first visit I ordered the Chilli Egg Bicho, and bicho… let me tell you, this was the best breakfast I have ever eaten. Not the best on this trip, not the best this year, the best breakfast I have ever had. My friend ordered the Beef Tapa and loved every bite. We both had cappuccinos, and even those were exceptional. The bean matters, and they clearly know what they are doing.

Today was my last day in Australia, and I came back for that same home cooked feeling and familiar warmth. I ordered the Chilli Egg Bicho again and it was just as incredible. The Bicho bread is unreal, soft and chewy and flavorful in a way that makes you stop mid-sentence. The bread is locally sourced about 40 km away from Starlight Bakery in Doonside, a Filipino bakery worth visiting if you are out that way. If you go, get yourself a Bicho. No hesitation.

Today’s service was from Giannina and she was fabulous in every way, warm, attentive, and exactly the kind of person who makes a great meal feel even better.

Long story short and short story long, go. Get some food. Drink the coffee. Enjoy the vibe. Then leave your own review so more people find this beautiful little spot. Adhika is a standout and one of my favorite finds in Sydney.

Really lovely café with delicious food and such friendly service. Everything was fresh, well-seasoned and beautifully presented. The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, and it feels like the kind of place you could happily linger for a while. I dined in and enjoyed every dish I ordered — great flavours and generous portions. Would definitely come back.

I recently visited Adhika Cafe, and I was honestly impressed.

The sandwich was made with fresh ingredients and had the perfect texture and flavor combination. But the highlight for me was the ube cake soft and full of rich ube flavor.

It’s rare to find a place that does everything this well. Definitely a must-visit 🥰
Specially the staff very friendly and funny.

name: Alpha Mart
address: 92 Cahors Rd, Padstow NSW 2211, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: Alpha Mart is consistently my go-to spot. The store is always clean, well-organized, and stocked with the essentials. I’ve noticed their produce is fresher than most convenience stores in the area, and the staff is genuinely friendly. Whether it’s a quick morning coffee or a late-night snack run, it’s always a seamless experience. Highly recommend!
Visited this shop to get my Indonesian grocery. They have ready cooked meal, and some warm snacks on Saturday such is bakwan risol, lumpia etc sooooo yummy!!!

Great Indonesian store, has the best rendang paste, with it anyone can make great rendang at home, in a few simple steps. Really tidy store with good prices and friendly helpful staff, was after a spicy hot chilli sauce, their recommendation was excellent.
Good place to go for Indonesian groceries. Good selection and good service

When i looked at the previous review, someone said they did not open on time.
The picture was taken on 6th Dec 2020 at 11:30 and guess what . The shop is still not opened.

Would be nice if you put sign that you are not going to open today.

I understand if the shop is late to open in Indo but this is Australia. I can understand this shop is one lady show as she is doing everything while her husband is busy with his real estate company

If you are not committed to run this business, please do not waste time to manage it, better to close it.

Why i said to close? Limited items in the shop, price is above any other indo grocery store. For example 1 box mie goreng was 21 bucks and  the following month was increases to 23 bucks.
If the price is compared to White Loftus, they will be laughing by White Loftus owner  lol.
Normallt i shop at White Loftus but because of Covid,  am looking for the closer. The closest indonesian grocery for padstow,  revesby and panania is alpha mart . That is why they think they can get more profit.  Lol.

name: Asuka Japanese Kitchen (Rouse Hill)
address: Tenancy 1/32 Civic Way, Rouse Hill NSW 2155, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Nice staff.

Ordered Salmon Teriyaki bowl, paid $22. Decent taste but if I am paying $22, I want more than half a cherry tomato for my 5 leafs salad.

Asuka Japanese is a hidden gem in Rouse Hill!

We had the Tonkatsu Ramen and the Japanese curry (prawn) and both were stand-out dishes. The Japanese curry sauce was delicate and delicious. The prawns were cooked perfectly. I think a lot of people order chicken katsu curry with it so I would definitely be keen on trying that next time.

I am actually not a ramen fan as I find the base too rich but due to the reviews referencing it, we ordered it. I’m so glad that we did because it’s the best ramen I’ve ever tasted so far (not that I’ve tasted a lot but still!!!) The balance is great and the service size is also good (not too overwhelming as normal ramen bowls).

The restaurant is airconditioned so it really helps on a warm summer day. ⭐️

Jill and Carl served us and their customer service is top-notch. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

We’re so happy to have discovered this restaurant and we will definitely be back!

P.S. Ate there again today but unfortunately, the Tonkatsu is out of stock. We ordered the chicken katsu curry and the spicy pork udon. While both were yummy, I loved the spicy pork udon more. Absolutely delicious. Staff were courteous and had good customer service as well. Kirsten was really great!

We didn't expect much going here because it is a bit secluded (tucked away at the corner of the aparment complex). We wee're greatly delighted by the Japanese flavours. We finished both our orders. Only a few people dine here, it's more aimed towards doordash and ubereats audience. The staff, we found out, are all Filipino and they are all lovely. They were playing Filipino songs.

The interior is a bit tacky but decent. The initial table we chose was sticky so we changed tables.

This is a hidden gem in the Rouse Hill area. Servings is not that generous compared to other Australian restaurants.

Overall the experience is pretty good. The broth has strong taste. I am used to with tonkotsu pork has been grilled but the one I got seem to be missed . The chicken katsu is fried well and crispy. Last but not least I ordered for dining and the serving came in take away containers

The restaurant is near my home, and the food is usually delicious, so I've ordered from there many times. I ordered again today, but the vegetable tempura was in terrible condition, completely burnt, leaving only a bitter taste. I ended up giving up on eating it. Last time, there was even a piece of plastic in the food. I hope you'll pay more attention to the quality and cleanliness of the food in the future.

name: Atin Ito Filipino Finest Cuisine
address: Shop 2/7/9 Lake Ave, Cringila NSW 2502, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
You could spend a long time exploring the menu at Atin Ito Filipino Finest Cuisine, it’s extensive. Former ABC Sydney radio host, Simon Marnie, recommended the dried fish platter ($37.50) that features five different types of fried dried fish. His recommendation is how we came to find ourselves in Cringila, on a hill overlooking the Port Kembla steelworks. In contrast to the hard industrial backdrop, the restaurant has a homely vibe. Bamboo window decals and garlands of plastic flowers create intimacy, with fishing basket lights and potted green palms completing the effect. The warmth of Pinoy hospitality is legendary, and this restaurant is no exception to the rule. We’re quickly seated with staff pointing out highlights, like spaghetti topped with chopped hot dogs that closely resembles Jolly spaghetti, a dish put out by Jollibee, a famous Filipino chain.

We’re drawn to bicol express ($19), pork stewed in coconut milk with chilli peppers, shrimp paste and plenty of garlic, eaten over golden turmeric and annatto infused java rice ($4.50/bowl). From the extensive vegetable selections, adobong sitaw ($19) presents a generous amount of snake beans in a tangy adobo sauce that combines soy sauce, vinegar and more garlic. Bagnet Pinkabet ($25.95) is mostly vegetable too—a colourful collection of okra, pumpkin, eggplant and snake beans simmered in a tomato and shrimp paste based sauce—֪֪topped with a neat row of crunchy pork belly.

Pinoy people definitely like their sweets—the country has a history of sugar production—so sugar appears everywhere, from drinks to desserts to savoury dishes, like adobo. We try banana Q ($6), a saba banana (sweet plantain) stuck on a stick under toffee-like caramelised brown sugar. The same slightly fibrous bananas are sliced in banana con yelo ($10) a drink of sweetened condensed milk and banana poured over crushed ice. Sago drink ($7) is slightly less sweet, with sago pearls and gulaman jelly (agar-based) flavoured with vanilla. Trays of biko ($10) belie the price: this sweet glutinous rice cake cooked with coconut milk and brown sugar will feed a family! We ended up with enough food to feed us both again for dinner. Luckily containers ($0.50/each) were happily supplied.

Lechon kawali is still tough and not crispy as per advertised.
Palabok is so so
Chicken inasal is like chicken bbq
Pork bbq skewers is okay
Not finest filipino cuisine

First time trying Filipino food and it did not disappoint.
We tried the Halo-Halo, qwek-qwek, Bicol express and Crispy pata with white rice and wow, it was so flavourful and fresh. My husband is very picky but he was eager to try it and loved it

The owners came out and introduced themselves, they are both lovely people full of joy and love. They make you feel welcome and comfortable

Will come back soon!

Authentic From Filipino street food kwek kwek , To silog’s’, to main dishes, to Soups , to inasal and   to Fiesta Favorite - whole Lechon Cebu or Belly Lechon! Desserts? Halo-halo, leche flan and turon , they have it!! 😋

Am in Wollongong just for 3 days but I came by this Filipino restaurant and stepped in ... Not sure how long the lunch specials will last coz I tried the pork tocino Roger recommended. It was awesome. Haven't really had much Filip food before but  my taste buds say this is pretty authentic!

name: Australia Philippine Chamber of Commerce
address: Wills Glen, St Clair NSW 2759, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.

name: Australian Filipino Healthcare Alliance
address: 135 Macquarie St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.

name: B.A.R. Cafe and Resto
address: 27/27-33 Oaks Ave, Dee Why NSW 2099, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: One of the best Filipino food you can find in Sydney. Relatively smaller portions than others but decent price and definitely delicious! Garlic rice was bomb and so was pork 🤤

The best Filipino food! Been to the Phillipines twice, and the food here is authentic and cooked with love.

But don't take my word for it. B.A.R. already has a cult following from the Filipino community in the Northern Beaches. You know a place is good when all the Filipino Titas/Aunties come here to eat and chat. ;)

From crispy pata (crispy pork knuckle) to breakfast silog - pictured is the bansilog (milkfish silog) - to sinigang (tamarind soup) B.A.R. does it scrumptiously well.

With a dedicated barista, baker and chef, it's the perfect stop for a coffee and siu pao (pork bun), sweet bun or a delicious lunch.

Great coffee, welcoming service, reasonable prices, great quality, delicious flavours - B.A.R. is serving Filipino soul food for the whole community. If you've never tried it before, this is the place to get your taste of the Phillipines.

Salamat po, Thank you, B.A.R. Cheers to the love of good food and much success to you.
Delicious food!! My family and I loved it. I need to come back for more bread....

One of the better rooftop bars in Sydney close to the CBD. Worth the short walk. Great atmosphere and cocktails. Nice views of the city. It gets busy in Summer but they can usually handle walk-ins during off-peak times for 2-3 ppl. If there are more of you it pays to call and reserve a table.
Extraordinary. A good example of a specific Kind of filipino Restaurant. Delicious food with Excellent Service. You will think about returning if you wish to have Filipino Cuisine.

name: Baker's Avenue
address: Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Honestly the food here was probably a three but they were pretty friendly here so the service brought it up. Came here not knowing what to get and asked for their recommendations and they told me to try the two I ended up choosing. I respect the small business, they’re not quite breadtop quality in terms of quality and quantity but I don’t see many other filo places like this one.

Well priced bakeries that are freshly baked. Staffs aren’t too friendly but can’t expect anything much from an affordable family business. Bakes are generally alright, you get what you pay for. Not exactly that tasty or made with quality ingredients, they’re okay. We tried the ube cheese pandesal, pandan cheese pandesal , chicken empanada, ube bun, mornay, ham and cheese roll and ube muffin. The ube/pandan flavourings were not strong, they tasted like plain bread/ muffin, the only difference was the addition of food colouring. Chicken empanada tasted too sweet for a savoury food.

This bakery specialises in Filipino cakes and pastries, our favourite is the ensaymada nicely coated with butter sugar and cheese, but the ones with flavours are somewhat inconsistent with their fillings, some have a little filling some have hardly anything at all.other than that , the bread itself is soft and fluffy.we might try their other offerings in the future.

FRESHLY BAKED ,HUGE, CHEAP . Spanish bread can be improved by making it custardy and spread filling all over the dough coz when i tried, the custard all in one spot and does not taste like spanish bread. Not a big fan of empanada filling pastry is good but pls use chunks of meat pls . Otherbreads are nice and rrmunded me of childhood pandesal and ensaymadas ...they have all kinds of pandesals ensaymadas, ube hopia. Since ur pandesal is ur star pls make more throughout day. For the price  i think overall taste and quality is good. Its a busy bakery i think staff were helpful.

The reason i give only one star kabayan is empanada is one of my favourite. Nang kinain ko taste panis yong pork. Pls dont sale kung ilan days na tapos reheat uli, it couse food poisoning. Lucky I didn't give to my children.

name: Bambu Cafe Desserts Canley Heights
address: 226 Canley Vale Rd, Canley Heights NSW 2166, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Bambu Café Canley Heights is a total vibe! 💕 They’ve got so many drink options—everything from refreshing teas to creative coffee blends—and the ice cream and bingsu are a must-try! 🍧☕ The atmosphere is super cozy, and the staff are always friendly and welcoming. Perfect spot to chill with friends or treat yourself to something sweet! 😋✨
Really good local spot for Che and other desserts. Staff are extremely friendly and plenty of seating. Everything was tasty and made to order. Also small playground nearby for kids

A modern and spacious cafe offering lots of Vietnamese drinks, coffee, desserts, ice cream and a small food menu. We enjoyed their coffee, halo halo but food was average. Food that we ordered, fish and chips and cheesy garlic bread. It's a nice cafe to try especially if you're looking for authentic drinks and desserts from Vietnam. Good customer service.

Went on a Saturday night. Got pretty packed as you would expect. Got the avocado/durian shake, the lychee soda and the mixed dessert. Avocado & durian shake tasted great, the halo halo was ok, felt that more toppings were needed. The lychee soda was nice and refreshing although a smidgen sweet. Customer service was good and all food items came out in a decent time (considering how busy it was). Menu items were extensive so theres definitely something for everyone.  On par with Cafe Nho in my opinion, however way more menu options.

Shop is outstanding because of bright colourful led signs Bit of surprising when you want to change from normal millk to light/skim milk there is extra 80c 😁😁. Don’t see this in any other coffee shops. Truely Avocado coffee just completely loss the taste.

name: Bar Una Más
address: 130a Beach St, Coogee NSW 2034, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: How have we lived here and never before visited  with a very reasonable $65 tasting menu serving Spanish tapas that are exquisitely prepared and promptly served. Bread, steak, kingfish Crudo, and even cheesecake. A lovely view over Coogee beach makes for a lovely atmosphere

We booked last minute and sat at the bar. The bartender and staff were ultra friendly and lovely. Food was absolutely incredible! But the cheesecake!! I dream about this cheesecake, it was to die for. I drool thinking about it.

The only thing is we were a bit disappointed in was the sangria. It was 3 little mouthfuls for the price. But we just switched to wine, which was amazing!

Overall had a great experience and can’t wait to visit again!!

To begin:
The venue is quite small and was packed. Nevertheless the music was turned up so loud that I had to shout to my partner who was sitting 6" from me. This, apart from being unpleasant led to the rest of the problems with this place.

Whilst the wait staff were polite and professional, the noise level (presumably) led to a great deal of confusion with the ordering.

Out of the 6 dishes we ordered, 4 were evidently misheard and substituted with different dishes. For example a calamari dish was substituted with garlic prawns, and empanadas never arrived. Same for the drinks; a whisky sour became an Amaretto sour. Annoying but not fatal.

Then the wine. The wine list was full, with some good wines. What really, really annoyed me was the house wine being falsely labelled. We ordered an Italian Red, Chianti from the wine list. When the bottle arrived, it had a screw closure (not really used in Italy, so I looked closer). House wine made for Merivale.

As for the patronage, the Mutton was dressed as Lamb and the Lamb dressed as strippers (I'm told I'm being disrespectful to strippers).

"Una Mas" means "one more" no thanks.
Loved the great variety of tapas and the beautiful view of Coogee Beach. The staff were really attentive, but since it gets quite busy and the tables are close together, it can be quite noisy.

This is a lovely tapas restaurant. The food was seriously good - from the appetizers to the mains. In particular the flatbread and the swordfish. We had a wonderful meal. I didn’t photograph the desserts but my strawberry sorbet with French toast was simply melt in the mouth good, and the lemon/olive oil sorbet was equally good. Definitely a place to return to.

name: Barong Tagalog Filipiniana
address: Shop 3 Station Arcade, 24 Main St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: Barong Tagalog Filipiniana is run by kind people who really care about finding you the right outfit for the occasion. Highly recommend for anyone looking for Filipino clothing 🤗

name: Barong at Bestida Australia
address: 274 Queen St, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews:
Came initially to just have a look at the range and came out with one barong!
The customer service is incredible and the staff was very patient and helpful. It's so good that i was able to find what i was looking for and be able to represent my culture at my wedding.

We visited the shop one week before our wedding and were able to find the perfect suit and gown. The owners are lovely and accommodating, offering free coffee and creating a warm, welcoming atmosphere that truly reflects Filipino hospitality.

Good quality barong compared to the one I got in the Philippines in the local market, it looks great with my undershirt and my wife loves it too! It fits my size and they even steam my barong to remove creases only for 5$. Great job! I love how the shop is organized too.

I needed a barong for my citizenship ceremony and reached out to them through their Facebook page. They responded promptly, assisting me in finding the available items in my size. Initially, I had reservations about receiving it on time. However, once I placed the order and requested same-day delivery, they fulfilled my request. The exceptional quality and perfect fit of the barong even earned praise from the mayor of Northern Beaches.
Barong arrived really quickly and looks great. Communication was really good regarding an website-related issue. Worked well resolving. Excited to wear this barong at my friend's wedding.

name: Bay Predo’s Lechon Cebu
address: 11/1 Sappho Rd, Warwick Farm NSW 2170, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
One man band...love it. .food are great and also in fair price. We enjoyed the sisig, pancit and longanisa.

Huge Thank you for allowing us (family of two) to dine late Sunday evening. (9:45pm). Had a good chat with Jon D Jon...woohoo

Laid back atmosphere, reminds me of a small beach town diner. Friendly service and great food.
Food was promptly served after the order was taken. Enjoyed the lechon and skewers. Fantastic flavour.
Will be back to try other dishes.
super lovely restaurant with great food and welcoming staff! would recommend for anyone looking for a yummy feed

Bay Predo Lechon Restaurant is a must-visit for Filipino cuisine lovers. Their lechon is incredibly flavorful and crispy, truly a standout. The salad options are diverse with large portions, perfect for sharing. Fish dishes are also well-prepared and delicious. Great food, generous servings, and satisfying taste all in one place!

almost every day they’ve live music which is awesome.

Fabulous little hidden jem. Delicious food, beautiful staff. Live bands on Saturday night. Really fabulous for the whole family. Kids running around safely having a ball, parents chatting and enjoying the space. Surprising and delightful. Thanks Bay Predo’s.

name: Bayani Imports Pty Ltd
address: 18 Eddie Rd, Minchinbury NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
The best supplier we have 😊
The team are very accommodating and very helpful very friendly as well 😊😊

I had a great experience with Bayani Imports, a supplier of Filipino products. Their selection is impressive, offering a wide range of authentic goods that truly bring a taste of the Philippines closer to home. From pantry staples to specialty items, everything I purchased was fresh, well-packaged, and high quality.

name: Bm Asian Food Import Pty Ltd
address: 11 Wiltshire St, Bow Bowing NSW 2566, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
First impressions didn't expect much with a halo halo order and thought I would be getting a small skimpy halo halo like im normally used to getting at other places BUT! SHE GAVE ME A MONSTER HUGE HALO HALO. I'm over the moon with just how big it was and  all the mixes included.

So delicious and fresh. Perfect for a hot day and a little treat.

Best one I've had in Syd!
Best Halo Halo in Sydney!

name: Burger Point Marsden Park
address: Shop 24/9 Hollinsworth Rd, Marsden Park NSW 2765, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
decided to try this place after seeing all the hype on TikTok. I ordered the double patty burger and a 6-piece meal. The food was good  nothing extraordinary, but enjoyable overall. The atmosphere was outstanding, and there’s plenty of parking available. I’d definitely recommend this place for casual dining, though I did find the prices to be a bit on the higher side.
Good food, great vibes, and a nice spot for a relaxed meal.
Solid burger and chicken spot. Gives off major Pinoy vibes 🇵🇭. If you like Jollibee, you will love this place, it comes real close to the real thing. Sarap!
Food was nothing special, the sauce was too salty . The burger was oki . Taste Could be improved
Fabulous burgers we had the Boss burger with added bacon. Great friendly service, definitely recommend to dine here.

Tried the Marvin Glaze at Burger Point — absolutely awesome! 🔥
The double patty was juicy and packed with flavor, and that glaze tied everything together perfectly. One of the best burgers I’ve had in a while. Highly recommended! 🍔✨

name: Burger Point Norwest
address: Shop 20/4 Century Cct, Norwest NSW 2153, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Burger Point is seriously one of my comfort food spots. Their sauces?? Elite. I don’t know what they put in them but everything just tastes better because of it.

I’m still not over the Dirkty Skinny Fries — that was my absolute fave and I wish they’d bring it back 😭 It was SO good.

Their spaghetti and chicken combo is also a winner for me. Super satisfying and always hits the spot when I’m craving something indulgent. Honestly, every bite just brings me back home in the Philippines. ❤️

The staff are also amazing — super friendly and always make you feel welcome. It just makes the whole experience even better.

Always a solid choice when I want something tasty and saucy. 10/10 would keep coming back.

Their chicken is crispy, crunchy and tasty - comparable to the Philippines' Jollibee chickenjoy. Their spaghetti is a bit on the salty side. I would not recommend it. Burgers are also good! Serving time took close to 20 minutes!

⭐ 1/5 - Extremely disappointing experience at Burger Point Norwest (29 November 2025, 8:33pm)

We came to pick up a simple takeaway order and somehow ended up leaving annoyed, unimpressed, and questioning why basic customer service seems optional here.

When we arrived at 8:33pm, we stood directly in front of the cashier. She made direct eye contact with us, ignored us, and continued standing there without a greeting or acknowledgement. After an awkward wait, we actually prompted her with a “hello, how are you” - to which she didn’t respond at all. She simply stared at us, waiting for more information. We then had to clarify that we were there to pick up a takeaway order. That alone set the tone for the rest of the visit.

After the cashier pointed to the order, we collected it and checked it outside and noticed that despite paying for a large fries, we were given a small. We walked back in and raised the issue with the cashier again, who then escalated it to the person cooking at the back.

The explanation we received from both of them was honestly unbelievable. We were told that they do sell two sizes of fries, but both the small and large come in the exact same container. The cashier first claimed that a large should come as “two small boxes,” until we pointed out we only received one - at which point she quickly retracted that. The final justification was that a “large” is just the same container with “more packed in.”

This logic is absurd. It’s the equivalent of selling a “large drink” in a small cup and insisting it’s large because you filled it higher. No reputable food venue tries to pass this off as a legitimate portion upgrade.

When we questioned the inconsistency, the response was simply: “No refunds or exchanges.” That alone speaks volumes about how little customer satisfaction matters here.

To top it all off, the fries we did receive were stale, dry, and clearly not fresh.

Burger Point Norwest needs a serious reset on customer service, honesty in portioning, and general professionalism. After tonight’s experience, we won’t be returning.
Good food and great service. I would definitely recommend it to my friends and family. 👍🧡

Food quality and quantity was atrocious. The Hot Hatch burger tenders were so small two of them barely took up half the bun combined. Not crunchy, but I mostly tasted bread in the end. The Boss Burger ingredients were similarly stingy and not worthr the price at all. The patty was tiny as well as the bacon slice. The cheesy chips were a small portion (I think smaller than usual) and were pretty cold and I only live 4 minutes away.

Would love to be contacted as the only contact for the Burger Point brand is the store phone number...pretty poor.

name: CONCORD BAKEHOUSE Concord
address: Unit 1/102 Majors Bay Rd, Concord NSW 2137, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I had read about the grumpy woman behind the counter and was prepared to get my pies and run. Out she came and grumpy she did look. But very quickly we were chatting and laughing and in a very blunt Philippine manner sold me 2 almond croissants I didn't want. The pies were great and the sausage rolls...thank you Buddha for leading me to a shop that makes sausage rolls with flaky pastry and real meat rather those rolls you cant tell the doughy pastry apart from the doughy meat filling and need to have extra tomato sauce just to be able to swallow them.
Atmosphere gets 5 stars because I love how Asians just don't care and present their shops like they are straight out of the 70's.

Shop: Concord Bakehouse
Filling Amount: 4/5
Bread: Crisp crust and soft inner.
Price: $6.5 classic pork, $8.95 Crackling Roast Pork (sampled today)
Taste: 4/5
Total: 4/5

I’ve tried the traditional pork banh mi here in the past, but today I’m checking out the “Crackling Roast Pork”, an option that has been added since I first visited here last year. The roll came with mayo, pate, red onion, carrot, cucumber, coriander and shallot. The roast pork and bbq pork are kept in a heated section of the display cabinet, so the meat on my roll was still warm. I must’ve come at the right time as my bread roll was also still warm, presumably coming out of the oven not too long ago. Banh mi was a good size and well packed with ingredients. The roast pork has been seasoned well and there was quite a bit of it in the roll.

Was a hit and miss also rude service  the pork roll was dry I asked is it crispy and fresh  reply was yes . While making the sandwich this lady was so rude I should I’ve cancelled it then and there ended up throwing it. As the previous week it was tasty not wasting money anymore  and getting served dry meat and rude service .

I have been here a few times to grab a pie and on one previous occasion bought a Banh mi.

All the prices and products are displayed on screens above the counter and the BBQ pork Bahn mi was priced at $9.50

So I ordered one, the lady made it and said $10.50. I said no the screen says $9.50 she said that’s the old price. Well sorry that’s the legal price you MUST charge by law.

I paid it but I will never shop there again.

If a small business can afford to put in screens and software to advertise there products then they can afford to ensure pricing is up to date and legally compliant
Iced viet coffee was 90% ice.

name: COP International Ministries Australia Ltd.
address: The Hub, 29/128 Station Rd, Seven Hills NSW 2147, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews:
I have been attending COP for quite some time now. I can wholeheartedly say it's an uplifting experience.

What also stood out most to me is the genuine care that the pastors have for each member. They are committed to our spiritual growth and offer continuous guidance, always respecting where we are in our journey. The church also fosters a strong sense of community, with many opportunities to serve God and connect with others. The teachings are deeply rooted in the Bible, providing a solid foundation for anyone looking to grow in their faith. As an example, tithing is taught once a year as an encouragement to the members to grow your relationship with God, but it is never enforced, allowing us to give from our hearts rather than obligation.

Overall, I will say that COP is a place where you can deepen your understanding of God's goodness and the teachings of Christ. It is a church where you can truly grow in faith and fellowship. Thank you!

It was through this church that I came to know Jesus and deepened my relationship with God. I have been attending this church since 2022. The teachings are fully rooted in the Bible, and the worship services is filled with the Holy Spirit, inspiring, and overflowing with the presence of the Lord. The ministry goes beyond Sunday services. They also have Friday services, weekly Bible studies, morning prayers, overnight prayer meetings, daily Bible devotions, and even kids’ musical. The community is incredibly welcoming, and the food is always delicious. Praise God for this amazing church!!!

I’ve been attending COP since I was little in the Philippines, and I was so happy to find COP Sydney when I moved to Australia for work. It’s truly a place of worship where God’s presence is felt, and the people are warm and welcoming. All teachings are deeply rooted in the Bible, which has strengthened my faith even more. Now, my husband and I worship here every Sunday, and we are grateful to be part of this spiritual family.🩷

I praise God for this Church. As an international student, I never felt homesick and alone. I was even able to find a family in Christ. God has created a meaningful purpose of putting me here. It will always be my greatest decision to follow Him. Back in my country 2 years ago, I just asked Him that to know Him more. He not only answered my prayer to study in Sydney but gave me a new life, a better life and Jesus helped me grow by putting me in this church, using me to serve in the ministry. Till today I am still in awe of what God has done for me. 🙏🏻🥹 Thank you Jesus! You deserve all the Praise and the Glory forever.

A very welcoming church. Great Praise & Worship and Bible based teaching. In addition to the Sunday service,  COP, which stands for Cathedral of Praise, has morning prayers on Weds to Fri and a Fri night Heart ❤️ Service at 7.30pm. If you are seeking a family oriented church,  please check it out. God is good.....all the time.

name: Casa Filipina Bakeshop & Restaurant
address: 54 Oxford Rd, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We have been a regular customer since they opened their restaurant in Ingleburn. The food is just delicious and reminds me of Philippines.

We ordered their special pancit bihon for friends gathering. Everyone loves it and said the pancit tasted great.

Thank you for your Filipino dishes!

My first time in the shop, got myself the Pandan Shake and some desserts. Never had filipino desserts before, changed my life. The place was nice, the staff were really friendly and enthusiastic and the vibes were great. Definitely coming back to try the other shakes 👍

A True Filipino Gem in Southwest Sydney!

Casa Filipina Bakeshop and Restaurant is an absolute gem in the southwest! It was our first time dining here, and we were celebrating our friends’ 50th birthday—what a blast. From the food to the service to the atmosphere, everything was spot on.

They served
pansit malabon (palabok), crispy pata kare-kare, sisig, fresh lumpia, pork BBQ, lumpia shanghai, and fried chicken—each dish tasted authentically Filipino and clearly prepared with care.

I was fortunate to be seated next to Oly, one of the owners, who shared amazing stories about how the restaurant started. He even explained how they prepare their dishes the traditional way—especially my favourites, the sizzling sisig and dinakdakan. To top it all off, he personally prepared brewed coffee for us during dessert!

My wife and daughter also asked me to buy from their bakeshop—sapin-sapin, yema cake, and leche flan—and all of them tasted awesomely Filipino.

I hate to compare, but here we go: Casa Filipina beats the usual places we go to around Blacktown—Mama Lor, Sir Manong’s, and the A-Team. I truly hope they expand and open a branch in the northwest of Sydney!

Highly recommended. Will definitely be back!

This was my first time trying Filipino food, so I don't have anything to compare it to. However, the meat was quite flavorful, though slightly on the dry side. Some items at the counter had names but no descriptions, which made me hesitant to try them. That said, I’d definitely return to explore more items on the menu.

Not only was the rice tasty, the tocino deliciously sweet, and the egg just perfect... I watched the staff assist an elderly customer with extreme patience and care. Well done. Also, it's good that they didn't mind my mixed up TagCano. I was trying to exercise use of Tagalog and Ilocano and it seemed to be good enough here. I feel encouraged now. Thank you. My wife and I love this place!

name: Catholic Church of St Paul of the Cross and the Immaculate Conception
address: 532 New Canterbury Rd, Dulwich Hill NSW 2203, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: A lovely church. Its dark brick exterior is imposing and austere but the interior is light and airy. The picture depicting the stations of the cross are interesting. The acoustics are great. The gardens outside are a little wild and have statues in amongst the greenery. There is a school hall next to the building which services the school across the road. A lovely place for some quiet reflection.

Dean Louis is the best. Friendly staff. Good choirs. Sometimes got cardinal there. If u mentioned free bread, lot strangers just took it without follow the mass. Or some just 15min mass, took the bread, go home. Lot annoying kids now, parent couldn't control it.  Few intention just 2get free bread, not 2pray, let alone meet God. Get in, get out.
Beautiful church with a loft area for discreet live streaming. Thank you for working with us over the years to assist families live stream their loved ones services.
Beautiful church and people here. They are accommodating, providing a children play area and hold both English and Filipino mass. Perfect sacred place to be married.

I love this Church. This Church provides free Bread at the 930am Saturday Church Service. A previous priest who resided at the Church was an Avid Gardener. Around the Church there is an excisite Devotional Garden. There is a Unisex Toilet also.

name: Charcoal & Crisp Lechon Sydney
address: 16 Falcon St, Crows Nest NSW 2065, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
CHARCOAL AND CRISP IN CROWS NEST

If you’re craving authentic Filipino food in Sydney, Charcoal and Crisp Lechon in Crows Nest is an absolute must-visit. Everything we tried was bursting with flavor and nostalgia, but the lechon was the undisputed star of the show — the skin impossibly crispy and flavorful, the meat juicy and tender.

Another standout was the lechon sisig, served sizzling with an egg on top. Perfectly seasoned, rich, and comforting — it’s the kind of dish that makes you want to come back for more.

We also loved the watermelon sinigang with prawns — a playful twist on a classic, both refreshing and tangy — as well as the fried pompano and ensaladang talong, which balanced the richness of the meats beautifully.

And don’t skip dessert. The ube cake here is divine: light, moist, not overly sweet, and packed with that signature purple yam flavor Filipinos know and love.

The venue itself is warm and inviting, with a casual yet vibrant ambiance that makes you feel right at home. If you’re a Filipino food lover — or just curious about discovering new flavors — Charcoal and Crisp in Crows Nest deserves a spot at the top of your list.

(3.5 stars)
Fat rolls of pork belly are on display on the kitchen pass at Charcoal & Crisp Lechon. The namesake golden-skinned, spit-roasted pork—lechon—is the focus of the short menu at this Crows Nest newcomer. The pork is cooked Manila-style, in a simple blend of salt, pepper and spice, then chopped and served with chips (or rice) and salad ($22.50). The fatty richness is balanced by a vinegar-heavy dipping sauce that employs the pig’s liver. Naturally sweet watermelon juice ($8) helps your palate recover from the salt.

And you will need to recover, especially if you also hit up the lechon sisig ($19.50). Here the chopped pork belly is turned onto a sizzling, cast-iron plate with mild green chilli, onion, and a raw egg that sets right before your eyes. Even a liberal squeeze of calamansi—the favoured citrus of Pinoy people—isn’t enough to save you from a powerful thirst. I suggest combining it with ensaladang talong ($15) and using the grilled eggplant salad’s fresh tomatoes and washed Spanish onion to further calm things down.

The pork also makes its way into lechon kare-kare ($19.50) in crisp cubes and big sheets of crackling. With the bagoong (sautéed shrimp paste) passed separately, I found this peanut-based curry a little bland and disappointing, though my salt-drenched palate appreciated the inclusion of juicy green beans and bok choy. Perhaps I just favour the Cebu-style version I first had in Enmore, where lemongrass and a metric tonne of garlic were stuffed inside the roast meat, and the kare-kare was balanced with bagoong by the kitchen. To make a booking here you have to pay a ten buck deposit. It’s refundable against whatever you eat, or the cost of bringing a bottle of wine (think: acidity) to enjoy with your eats.

The best lechon I’ve had in Sydney. The skin is super crispy and the meat is really tasty. The ensaladang talong was really good, especially the bagoong. The staff are very friendly, the price is fair, and the portions are worth it. I’ll definitely be coming back!! ❤️👌

We had such a great experience here! The restaurant serves amazing Filipino food — the lechon is so crispy and tasty, the pompano is delicious, and the prawn watermelon sinigang is a must-try, packed with fresh prawns and heaps of veggies. We also ordered the Feast for 2, and it was definitely worth it.

Not to mention, the ube dessert was really good too — not overly sweet, but you can really taste the ube halaya.

A big shout-out to Hazel as well — she was super accommodating and provided excellent customer service, which made our visit even better. I’d happily rate this place a solid 4.9/5, leaving just a little room for even more improvement. Highly recommended!

The Lechon is good but the Lumpiang Shanghai, Sisig, Kare Kare, and Ube cake are our go to dishes.
Hoping to one day wash all this down with a beer but don’t let that put you off, they sell out of food for a reason.

name: Chefs Buffet Broadway (Korean BBQ)
address: Shop LG02A Broadway Sydney, 1 Bay St, Glebe NSW 2037, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Easily one of the best Korean BBQ buffets in Sydney based on the price, variety, quality and not least of all the warmth and friendliness of the staff here. You can see they work like a well oiled and happy team and the result is a fantastic customer experience. The meat quality was very good for the lunch buffet price. The cooked dishes especially the Japchae were very good and tasted more like made to order ones.

Despite not having marinated LA ribs, they made up for this with an instant noodle bar and Korean fish cake soup (Eomukguk), Korean Fried Chiken and Dopoki section.

The dessert bar was also unique in that you could try a variety of commercially available Korean ice cream sticks as well as Korean cookies and fresh fruit.

The layout was well designed and reminded me of having street food in Seoul. There was only one negative but that was more the fault of shopping mall design in that there were no attached bathrooms and if you needed to use one, you had to exit the restaurant and take the escalators to the Broadway Shopping Centre.

Enjoyed this experience tremendously and I will be bringing friends back here to try it.

Overall, a solid experience.

The meat selection was a little on the smaller side compared to other Korean BBQ spots, but what they did have was good quality.

The side dishes were a highlight — really fresh and constantly replenished, which isn’t always the case at KBBQ places.

The cold bar had plenty of variety, so there were lots of options to choose from.

The atmosphere was nice and comfortable, and the service was friendly and attentive throughout.

Not the biggest selection, but still an enjoyable night overall.

HELLOOOO??? OMG I REALLY IMPRESSED by this place 🥹🥹 so many choices i cant even decide which one i want! 5 STARS OF EVERYTHING! and omg when i finished cook food they change the cooking plate straight away what a thoughtful service 😭😭😭. FOOD ALSO DELICIOUS, EVERYONE IS FRIENDLY. ITS ONLY $46 FOR ALL YOU CAN EAT. I love you ❤️‍🔥

My Fiancée and I enjoyed a delicious Korean Bbq at Chefs Buffet today, and we had such an amazing time. We received extraordinary service from Soyoung and Tasya. They were so accommodating, friendly and made our experience extra special.

Don't even get me started on the food. There was so much to choose from and everything we chose were fresh, flavourful and absolutely delicious. The cocktails were very refreshing and tasty. This is by far our favorite Korean Bbq restaurant and we will DEFINITELY be back again. We highly recommend them 🍖🥓💕.

Have more selection of food compared to other K Buffet places. Food being re fill quite quickly too so that's a bonus.

Food reasonably fresh and meat was ok some are fresh some are tasted a bit old. Price is somewhat reasonable, asked over text if allowed for individual payment but on the day was asked to pay all together which was a bit frustrating.

Some of the staff are great (the older korean lady and one of Filipino lady very attentive to our needs) and some are just meh.

They change our grill often but mind you it wasn't as busy.

No toilet available but they have plenty of wet tissues, clear gloves available.

Free water but need to pay for other drinks.

90 mins limit seating.

name: Chinggy's Express
address: 76 Atchison Rd, Macquarie Fields NSW 2564, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Food was delicious and affordable. Will definitely comeback for more.

Liempo (grilled pork) was tender,  the filipino burger steak was really good and dont forget to try the lumpia (pork spring rolls), really really good.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Absolutely lovely Filipino food with a bonus spread of Aussie classics! I keep coming back for the schnitzel, pork chop, palabok, chips, chicken, spaghetti—and honestly, so much more. Every dish feels like comfort food with heart. The flavors are spot on, the portions are generous, and there’s something for everyone. A true gem if you’re craving a mix of nostalgia and delicious variety.

Yummy Filipino palabok and Souvlaki!!! She makes the best palabok ever and we had to drive to satisfy our cravings! We tried the souvlaki and it was so yummy too! Burger steak is better than jollibee and the spaghetti and chicken is also sooo good!! Very generous serving too! 100 percent recommended!

Absolutely loved this food truck!
The food was fresh, flavorful, and served hot. You can really tell they put a lot of care into what they do. Super friendly service, The loaded fries were a standout! Will definitely be coming back for more. Highly recommend if you’re in the area!

Best food in town and open late!
We’re locals and absolutely love this place. Personally I have tried the cheeseburgers, loaded fries and lamb skewers — the food is always fresh, full of flavour and made with love. The menu also has a great variety of different options, and we now find ourselves coming back weekly.. The staff are always friendly and welcoming. Great service!
So glad to have Chinggy’s Express nearby as it’s our new favourite spot. Highly recommend checking them out!

name: Church Of Christ Non-Denominational
address: 504 George St, South Windsor NSW 2756, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews:
I have been attending to this church since I moved from the Philippines. They are an encouragement to me also to my family. Very friendly and funny (happy) congregation! I am so blessed that I have them!! They teach lessons based on the Bible and just from the Bible.
They also do youth groups every month which is really awesome! I love the singing fellowship as well!

You guys are more than welcome to join us and have some fellowship!

A friendly and welcoming church, lots of families and very child friendly as well . There's also Sunday school for children, youth class and adult catered lessons. They teavh according to the Bible and only from the Bible, no creeds or man-added ideas. Very sound.

Traditional Worship to keep the solemnity and sanctity of worship.Maranatha!

Cheers!
Great church of friendly Christians. Worth checking out.
Good  place for worship. Worship in spirit and in truth

name: Cocoa Vanilla Cakes
address: Inside Calli’s Grill, Shop 5a/22 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.

name: Coffee Creative
address: 133 Mitchell Rd, Alexandria NSW 2015, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews:
A neighbourhood Family business. You feel like you walked into someone's home, not a shop. Everyone working there was friendly and engaging. Coffee-wise: FW had a combo of subtle flavours slowly oozing out - 4.25/5. The Cappo was creamy n smooth- 4.25/5.
Food: veg Empanada (similar to a pastie) with slight chili sauce was ok. The truffle n mushroom toastie was better with a burst of umami. The lemon cake had a slight citrus tang & quite rich. What we ordered was definitely filling for 2.
Their dog, next to us, did not make a sound during our entire time there. We had walked half an hour in the light rain to find this cafe. It was worth it and we definitely want to return.

Loved that we were invited by friends to try out this cafe in Alexandria. Enjoyed our coffee and empanadas. Ash, the barista, is friendly and really makes coffee magical. Small place but totally a creative spot. Street parking available, also there's Jacaranda trees in full bloom nearby.
Visited today for the first time. Ashley was so friendly and nice, and we had some small talk. He makes people happier. The store's vibe was so cool.

We were in the area looking for a coffee and saw this place had amazing reviews. They did not disappoint - quite possibly one of the best coffees I’ve had in Sydney?! The place itself had so much warmth and character. Glad we found this little gem and look forward to our next visit!

This was my first stop in Sydney, and no other place was like this one. Family owned by a lovely Filipino family, their coffee and homemade food were amazing, and I loved the ambiance and community happening there.

name: Consulate General of the Philippines
address: Unit 1824/33 Wentworth Ave, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews:
I would like to express my sincere appreciation to the Philippine Consulate for the excellent service we received during my child’s passport renewal.
Special mention to Rowena (Weng) for being incredibly helpful, professional, and accommodating.🥰 She guided us through the entire process and even assisted me with photocopying the documents😊 which I truly appreciated. Everything was smooth and stress-free. The processing was amazingly fast, it only took about 15 minutes and we were completely dond😇
Thank you for your efficiency and dedication to serving the Filipino community. Your outstanding service is truly commendable and very much appreciated.😇🤩

Extremely disappointing experience at the Philippine Consulate General in Sydney.

You would think that an office representing the Philippines overseas would at least have clear instructions and professional staff. Apparently that is too much to expect.

The website provides almost no useful guidance. Important information like cut off times, required mailing envelopes, correct forms to download, and other requirements are either missing or unclear. So people show up thinking they prepared everything, only to be told they downloaded the wrong forms or are missing something that was never even mentioned online. It feels less like a government service and more like a guessing game.

And then there is the customer service.

One bald, dark skinned male staff member at the counter was incredibly sarcastic and dismissive when answering questions. Instead of helping applicants understand the process, he seemed more interested in making sarcastic remarks and acting irritated that people were asking for clarification.

There was also an older lady staff member who behaved in a similarly rude and condescending way. Rather than guiding people, the tone was impatient and unfriendly, as if applicants were bothering them simply for being there.

This is a consulate. People come here for official documents and services. They should not have to deal with attitude, sarcasm, or staff who clearly do not want to assist the public.

Between the lack of transparency, the confusing instructions, the surprise requirements like envelopes and photocopies, and the extremely unprofessional behaviour from staff, the whole experience leaves a terrible impression.

If this office represents the Philippines abroad, then the standards need serious improvement. Professionalism, transparency, and basic respect should not be this difficult to provide.

Worst customer service experience I have had in a long time.

I had a truly wonderful experience at the Consulate General of the Philippines in Sydney. The service was very pleasant, welcoming, and efficient. Everything was completed quickly and completely stress-free. All the staff were friendly, professional, and accommodating. Mr. Many Galvez kindly helped me organise everything I needed for my Philippine passport renewal, making the entire process smooth and easy. Ms. Rowena Maria was also very supportive and helpful. They are genuinely amazing staff, and I am very grateful for their assistance. Overall, I am extremely satisfied with the service and highly appreciate their excellent work. 😊

The male staff were quite rude and are very passive aggressive. This makes it very difficult to interact with them. One staff member gave up on taking my photo and said that if it gets rejected that I’d have to pay for it instead when they’re supposed to make ways. We pay for the service to WORK not for us to come back because it didnt work the first time. Do better

I recently processed my SPAs, report of birth, and dual citizenship application, and I couldn't be more pleased with the service I received. The staff were incredibly helpful and respectful throughout the entire process. They were very responsive to my messages and even took the initiative to call me with updates on my application. Their attention to detail and commitment to customer service truly stood out. Thank you Ma'am and Sir ❤️

name: Cucina de Manila
address: 28/30 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
This restaurant is a must-try for Filipino dishes. We ordered and tasted almost everything on the menu but everything is cooked so perfectly, that it made it hard for us to choose which dish is the top 1😂🙈

But for me, the best of bestest dish of all is their Crispy Pata. I’ve already tasted this almost 3 times when I stayed 3 weeks in Sydney. And as a Filipina traveller, I suggest going to this place, because it’s rare to find food here in Sydney that is worth your money and will satisfy your cravings. Crispy Pata is also the best food for gatherings and celebrations with family and friends. And, by the way, this resto is near the Featherdale Wildlife Park, we went here again after our fun and tiring walk in the Wildlife Park.

We also tried The Crispy Dinuguan, we ordered this because the “crispy” intrigued us, and it did not disappoint. Another best one from their menu, is the Grilled Pork Belly, sooooo tasty that it would make you order it again for dinner.

And, as a Bicolana, Laing is a must-try too, it’s so fulfilling and tasty. Pork Sisig is sooooooo yummy also, the sisig plate is full of meat and flavor.

As for their Kare-Kare, Bangus, Pinakbet, and Palabok, I don’t usually order these in the Philippines, because they did not taste good, but I never imagined that I would like their cooking for these dishes, especially being able to taste them in AU, since it’s hard to create a tasty flavor for these dishes. Dabest! Will surely come back again when we travel back to Sydney. Oh, and their Sagu’t Gulaman and Calamansi beverages are a must-drink also, especially in the summer heat of AU.

Overall, their dishes made us Filipinos feel like we’re “home away from home”. To all Filipino and Filipino food-lovers out there, support and try out this resto. It’s so worth it! The staff is very accommodating and helpful, just a tip also, they’re sometimes booked with a lot of orders, dine-in, and boodle fight bookings, so please be patient 💖😋😋✨

This the best Filipino food in Sydney indeed! After our tour in Featherdale zoo, we head directly to this restaurant as it is the closest we can find.

Their Crispy Pata is crispy on the outside, tender in the inside. We also ordered Kare-Kare, Pinakbet, Dinuguan. All of them are perfectly cooked.

The staffs are very welcoming and attentive. Will definitely go back for more!

Ordered palabok xl tray and crispy pata for Christmas. We have had crispy pata before and it was always perfect and the best anywhere we’ve been but this time it was overcooked and was so dry and tough. The palabok was recommended by a friend but unfortunately, it didn’t have enough sauce for the xl tray, the toppings were just eggs, lemon and spring onions - no prawns, tofu, maybe just a bit of mince (I’m not even sure). My sister in law had to cook extra sauce or else it will be just wasted. I’m not sure if they changed chef or owners but definitely no one in our party will order from them.

Classic Filipino dishes on the menu and everything is tasty. This is my fave crispy pata in Sydney, hands down. Food delicious. Service fast and friendly. Space is clean and walls are decorated with Manila scenes.

The only downside is the facility - need to improve toilet facilities in Filipino restaurants.

Food is okay.
Prices are above the normal price.
When we arrived, some dishes on thr menu were not available. The tissues are limited. Had to ask for it.
The Ube shake did not taste like Ube it taste more like vanilla. I was very disappointed. No halo halo available.
Any soup were big on liquid, not many vegetables or meat. You will be swimming with soup. Great for people who loves soup. Bring your rice with you. Expensive to buy rice too.
My experience is good but I was expecting better.

name: Cusina De Pedro
address: Eskdale St, Minchinbury NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Useless website. No menu. No published prices. There's a button to click to order but what it does is to scroll down to the bottom of the screen to tell to you to email your order.  How can one order if there are no prices? It is illegal to trade without price labels being displayed.
The food are so delicious and affordable. I buy from them regularly.
No regret. My all time favourie
Nice food

name: Da Jia Hao Asian Supermarket
address: 10 Howard Ave, Dee Why NSW 2099, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
-First shop I found on the beaches that sells the marumiya furikake pouches
-They have a ginger cat 🐈
-I did look at expiry dates and wasn't  able to discover out of dates. But I honestly can't  read half of the products anyway becuase they are written in Filipino, Thai etc. lol.

I was happy. Decent selection. Some good prices. The cashier  was.. like.12?
Owner couldn't even tell me if the place was open

Bad place - everything that is supposed to be frozen is melted. I bought some 'frozen' abalone here and found out that it was already melted and smelling like literal rotten fish. Some things are also expired. If I could give 0 stars I would

It is nice to have an Asian grocery in the area for years. But I got couple of expired products from them make me really concerned… please do check the stuffs on your selves regularly…. They are expired like weeks ago but still selling as normal price ….
Good selection of products at a good price right in a central location and the staff are always friendly and sometimes there's a cute cat !

name: Daniel's Cucina - Kariong
address: Inside Kariong Tavern, 10 Langford Dr, Kariong NSW 2250, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: It is located inside thae Kariong Tavern itself. The food is so good, been craving for a good filo food for a while now, hard to find a good and affordable one here in the Central coast. But Daniel's Cusina is a gem. Taste so Filo the staff was very nice and welcoming. My husband is so happy with his sisig and Crispy pata. Definitely gonna come back here and indulge with the food again...

Cucina Daniel is the real deal when it comes to Filipino comfort food. The dishes are affordable but seriously full of flavour,  they don’t cut corners on taste at all. The service was fast too; we were all famished and the food came out quickly. Definitely a must-try, especially the crispy pasta (couldn’t take the photos as it was too yum and finished by the second!), spring rolls  and dinuguan. Honestly, I can’t say enough good things about it. Definitely worth checking out!

A bit slow to get started despite the advertised opening time, but well worth the wait. We got the sisig and crispy pata with a garlic rice. Clearly enough for 3 or more people. Relaxed in a pub setting, which can be variable in noise. For someone like me living in the northern Sydney suburbs, this is faster than travelling to Western Sydney for the same great taste!

Not many filipino cuisines available. Went there for the soups but there were about 8 or so items unavailable and both soups were on there.
We ended up having the sisig which was on a hot plate and looked good but wasnt as good as other filipino restaurants. Im guessing the chef may be from a different province so its made differently. Each to their own. Didnt eat it all but didnt takeaway.

The adobo was great. Perfectly seasoned. We decided to take away the leftovers.

Environment isnt ideal for families. Its in the Kariong tavern so theres outside areas that allow smoking and the smoke was coming into the food area which isnt great for non smokers and kids. There was also mould all above the seating area or it looked like mould anyway.

Spent $68 for 2 dishes and 3 serves of rice for 2 adults and 2 kids. Could have done with just 2 serves of rice though.

Crispy pata was on point! Had it for takeaway and it was still so good, super crunchy skin and soft, tender meat. Perfect with rice and sawsawan. Felt like having a proper meal back home in the Philippines. Definitely ordering again, fosho!

name: Danieli Cafe
address: The Clock Tower Centre, shop 10, 35/55 Harrington St, The Rocks NSW 2000, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews:
Sampled a very delicious hot chocolate & their special range of tea! But most appreciative of their kind service — pleasant and full of smiles. I was too absorbed on my laptop and forgot the closing hours, the staff/owner quietly shut down and I only realised after I packed up.

Would love to come back and try out the food 🫶

Came here 2 days in a row for breakfast. Very friendly & food is delicious. Hubby had the big breakfast & I had poached eggs & avocado on sourdough toast- all cooked to perfection. Coffee is the best . Definitely recommend. We sat at the outside tables - great spot to watch the world go by

We had such a great start to the year by having coffee at Danieli Cafe. We met Ron and he was such a fun guy! Food and coffee were great - we definitely felt his love for his cafe! Thank you from the Philippines! 😊

didn’t have big plans that morning. I just wanted to grab some breakfast and take it easy. I found Danieli Café on Google and from the reviews I was expecting something different. I thought it would be an outdoor café, but it turned out to be inside a shopping arcade. At first I wasn’t too convinced, but the owner welcomed me right away. He had that slightly firm, very Italian way of speaking at the beginning, but he turned out to be really friendly and nice.

The coffee was excellent, strong and full of flavor, just like a real Italian espresso. It reminded me of Borbone coffee back home. I didn’t ask what blend it was, but I’m guessing it was an Australian roast with Italian inspiration.

For breakfast I ordered a vegetarian plate, a beautiful mix of zucchini, sweet potato and tomato served on a base of hummus and pesto. It was delicious. After that I tried a “Piccolo,” which is like a macchiato or a cortado. Also very good.

Overall, it was a lovely surprise. Great food, amazing coffee, and a warm, genuine atmosphere. I’d definitely go back.
I read a previous review about the protein pancakes and they didn’t disappoint. A perfect combination of flavours and sweetness.

name: Descanso
address: Shop 4/32-72 Alice St, Newtown NSW 2042, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Tried Descanso for brunch today and we were very impressed.  A clean and tidy cafe with a great location a little bit away from the madness of King St in Newtown giving plenty of options for free street parking in the surrounding area (1 or 2 hour limit).  Fantastic menu with a range of “Aussie” food options for the non-adventurous and a delicious range of authentic Filipino breakfast and lunch selections.  Some menu options are only available on the weekend.  Service was friendly and fast while the food was absolutely delicious accompanied by fresh (bottled) juices and great coffee.  Cheesy Pandesal was a scrumptious accompaniment to our meal too.  We grabbed some extra dishes to take home with us but I’m sure we will be back again sometime soon to work our way through the menu. Highly recommended!

We really enjoyed our visit to Descanso Café 🥰
The owners, ate Regine & Kuya Wilbur are so kind and accommodating. You can really feel the heart they put into the café and how much they care about their customers ❤️
They have so many yummy Filipino breakfast dishes and filo favourites! Come check it out ✨

As someone who has been to many Filipino restaurants — and being Filipino myself who can cook these dishes at home — I’m always on the lookout for a good Filipino restaurant for those days when I’m feeling lazy to cook but still craving comfort food.

This place came highly recommended by a friend, so we were excited to give it a try. The restaurant itself looks nice, and the food presentation was on point. However, sadly, the experience was quite underwhelming, especially for the price point.

We ordered the Barkada Share Board ($52), which was advertised as good for 2–3 people. Realistically, it barely served even two. At that price, I was expecting a hearty Filipino feast with perhaps even leftovers to take home — but that wasn’t the case at all. The longganisa was disappointingly tiny (about the size of my thumb), the garlic rice lacked any real garlic flavour (which defeats the purpose of calling it garlic rice), and the portions across the board felt stingy.

We also tried the Turon for dessert. For $8, we received just two small pieces cut in half to make it look like four. Honestly, this felt like a total rip-off.

After dining, I decided to check the reviews online and noticed a concerning pattern. While the owners do respond to feedback, the tone comes across as defensive and passive-aggressive. Instead of taking on board constructive criticism, they seem to blame or argue with the customers who leave honest reviews. This kind of attitude is disappointing and shows a lack of willingness to improve the dining experience.

I wanted to love this place. The vibe and presentation had potential, but sadly, the food quality, portion size, and pricing left me feeling very dissatisfied. There are so many other Filipino restaurants in Sydney offering more authentic flavours, better portions, and respectful customer engagement.

Would I return? Probably not. Filipino food is meant to be hearty, generous, and comforting — unfortunately, this was none of that.

Tried my first Filipino restaurant in Sydney and it was a great experience! The food was delicious — I had the beef pares, which was a bit on the salty side for my taste, but a squeeze of lemon balanced it out perfectly. The flavors were rich and comforting. Service was top-notch, friendly, and attentive — couldn’t ask for better. Definito worth a visit if you’re curious about Filipino cuisine!

Love, love, love DESCANSO!! ❤️ My family and I have been enjoying their food for years - way before they moved from Glebe to Newtown. We keep coming back not just because of their delicious food… but also because of the lovely Filipinos (husband & wife team) who own and run this Cafe Restaurant - together with their awesome team.

It’s definitely one of the best places to eat at here in Sydney - especially if you want to taste some delicious, authentic Filipino cuisine. I almost forgot to mention - their lechon and sisig are “must have” dishes when you come to visit.

Make sure to try all their popular mains PLUS the delicious deserts. We love the various “***silog” dishes, the yummy adobo, kare-kare, sinigang, palabok, Filipino spaghetti and MORE! 😋 My basic photos can’t properly describe how good their food are - you must come and taste them yourself.

See you again soon, DESCANSO family! Keep up the good work. 🙌🏽❤️

name: Descanso Filipino Cafe
address: 197 Glebe Point Rd, Glebe NSW 2037, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.

name: Eastwood Market 美食广场
address: 178 Rowe St, Eastwood NSW 2122, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
Eastwood night markets.  Every Saturday From 4pm till 10pm…

Range of food stalls from different cultures from Chinese, Malaysian, Philippines and Korean.

It’s lively and vibrant with so much to offer.  A try of every food is a must and prices for some are affordable and others too pricey.

So much flavours and colours to taste and gaze upon!

Total cost spent $40 for 2 people.

Cute little Asian food market!
Feels small but actually a great selection of different cuisines!

Has icecream as well but would crave more drink stalls  🥺
The seafood pancake from the Kin Len Thai booth was really good!
Good skewers Yanbian one.

Fun and lively night market vibes! The grilled skewers were juicy and flavorful with a nice smoky aroma, the dumplings and wontons were handmade, so warm and comforting, and the egg puffs were chewy and yum. The fresh coconut drink was refreshing and perfect to end the meal. Some stalls had a bit of a wait, but overall the atmosphere made it a nice casual night out.
The night market atmosphere was lively! The grilled skewers were fragrant and juicy, and the fried dumplings and wontons were handmade, so warm and comforting.
The egg waffles were soft and sweet, and the fresh coconut water was refreshing to finish. Some stalls had a bit of a wait, but the overall atmosphere was relaxed and cheerful, perfect for a weekend stroll and a bite. 🌙🍜

name: El Pinoy Care Services
address: Unit 7/192 Queen St, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews:
Very good and accommodating staffs and understanding especially Ma’am Maria the director. Thank you for giving a kind heart clients and helping to find a jobs. Very much appreciated 🙏🙏🙏
God bless your company

The care service provided  was outstanding, with an amazing and caring people,  was exactly what I was looking for.” “I was so pleased with the service I received from el pinoy and  they  went above and beyond to help me in any way.  Thank you for all the care and support.
Very approachable admin staffs and service offered is tailored to staff and client needs. 👍🏻

name: Fairfield Showground
address: 443 Smithfield Rd, Prairiewood NSW 2176, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews:
We set up our largest softplay setup at this venue for a little one’s first birthday and it was the perfect venue for such a special celebration.

Venue was huge and plenty of parking. This is the perfect spot to host a huge party! We set up in the Waratah Room.

We can't wait to be back again for another bubs birthday party!

This is where the popular Wednesday Flea Market is.
Parking can be hard to find if you don't go early.

First time I went here and I am so glad I did.  My husband showed me all the amazing freebies you get from exhibits, shows, stalls and displays.  Amazing things you find that are cheap and useful.  So many choices, adventure, hobbies, shows and demonstrations.

Everyone is happy, friendly, helpful and engaging.

Edit....sorry supposed to be Homebush showgrounds

Fairfield Saturday Markets are Held at the Fairfield Showgrounds.

Located on Smithfield Road Prairiewood.

Open between 9am - 4pm most Saturday’s.

Quiet a Large Market with the Majority of the Stalls Undercover.

Has a Great Selection of Stalls offering something to suit most everyone.

Lots of parking available. But, it’s almost always an effort or good fortune to get one.

Has many Stalls offering Food, Beverages, Fashion, Fruit and Vegetables, Homewares, Mobile Phone Accessories, Plants, Auto Accessories, Tools, Pets, Perfumes, Nuts, Collectibles, etc.

Only downside is that since the Grand Stand area has been locked up.

There are precious few seats available. Unless you’re purchasing food or drinks.
Had a wonderful time at the Filipino Fiesta Kultura . So many great Filipino food and entertainment. 🇵🇭😍

name: Farmers Land Florist - Rouse Hill
address: 10-14 Market Ln, Rouse Hill NSW 2155, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
Hương did a phenomenal job for my friend's Valentine's bouquet! My friend's girlfriend from the Philippines asked me to arrange this on her behalf, and we're all more than satisfied with Hương's above-and-beyond efforts!

As you can see, the pink lillies and roses arrangement is stunning, and the choice of wrapping really compliments the flowers and the overall gentle pink aesthetic.

The bouquet also smelt gorgeous too — it was fresh and fragrant, which smelt exactly like a strong and beautiful floral perfume!

Hương is so kind and sweet, and you can tell she puts a lot of care and consideration into all of her floral arrangements. I'll definitely return to Hong as my go-to florist, she's amazing!

This florist is absolutely incredible. She was so sweet, seemed to love her job so much and did an amazing job in wrapping our flowers. Will definitely be coming back in the future. God bless her, the world needs more people like this.

Huong is such a beautiful person with amazing skills in flower arrangements. If you want great customer service and beautiful flowers, see Huong ASAP ❤️ She will not disappoint!!!!!
