I will continue to support her business, as her passion and dedication are clearly reflected in her beautiful floral arrangements.
Huong was amazing at delivering my flower arrangement order for funeral service. Definitely coming back for more flowers next time :)

A wonderful flower shop with a beautiful variety of fresh flowers. The quality is excellent, and the prices are very reasonable. The staff are warm, welcoming, and always ready to help. Definitely worth a visit!

name: Fiesta Avenue Blacktown
address: 14/18 Main St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Its not very often and very reluctant to eat in a filipino restaurant when I'm here at home in Sydney but I thought I'd give this place a try. Its our 2nd time eating here and feel comfortable coming back again and again. Located opposite Blacktown station just down the Main Street escalators. Service is great with friendly staff. Plenty of seating. Freshly cooked food is delicious plus its affordable with 2 choices and a serve of hot white rice for $15. Additional rice is $3 - 3.50. They'll have your favourite pinoy ulams like afritada, adobo, dinuguan, bicol express, kaldereta and more. And my favourite desert halo-halo at $10.
Food is a bit bland. Serving size is decent. If you're craving Filipino food, you might consider other restaurants around the area.

discovered this hidden gem today.. great for those pinoys who are craving for some home cooked food. the place is comfortable with a functional ac to cool off from the blasting summer heat. the set up is a modern carenderia with lots of condiments (one pinoy trademark).

breakfast at 10 dollars. regular meal starts at 10-12 dollars. soft drinks is sold at 2 for cans and 3 of 390 ml bottles.

pinoy dessert such as halo halo is sold at 8. Turon at 2. many more options.

definitely will be coming back.

Recently, I went to the same shop for 2-3 times. the quality of food isn’t consistent as compared to the first time I came and tried the food. I took food home and noticed that the fried fish was fully soaked with cooking oil thus making it soggy. I think they can do better. Best to go early than go there when they are about to close - food not warm and the taste isn’t as good when they are freshly cooked.
Had topsilog at $15 with garlic rice, egg, vege and pork. Very delicious. Service wasn't too great but didn't have issues unlike the other comments.

ordered tapa with egg and garlic rice for $15. Worst garlic fried rice ive ever had, it was hard. I ended up paying another $3 for half plain rice.

Ambiance is good.

name: Fil-Mart
address: SHOP 5/220 George St, Liverpool NSW 2170, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: Highly recommend Eunice and Dom's food catering!  food is 👌🏼👌🏼👌🏼 and ordering from them was smooth and took out the stress of organizing everything from setup down to the staff who was friendly and extra helpful. Can never go wrong with Eun and Dom! lovely couple and knowing Eunice back in college days until now, she has done more than best along with hubby Dom to make the event perfect! 👌🏼 Thank you once again Eunice and Dominique! 🥰xx
Not sure why this place has such high reviews. Very small store with basic Filipino groceries. Small selection of average cooked food, had 5 choices on Sunday.
Filipino groceries and they also have Filipino dishes cooked daily for take away. Camille is very friendly.
Their food is delectable! My favorites are the Cebu ngohiong, their fresh lumpia and their pork bbq! A+++
Another great Asian food store. The staff was very friendly. They also sell Filipino foods. Very goog priced as well.

name: Filipino Chaplaincy of the Diocese of Parramatta
address: 58 Orwell St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: There's god in every call centres in Manila

name: Filipino Fiesta
address: 9 Regent St, Kogarah NSW 2217, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Don't go here! Appalling behavior! Stay away!
Ateng pandak asked me if I wanted a tender juicy hotdog as they have new item in stock which she said are cheaper than other filo store nearby. I politely decline and said "No, thanks. I don't eat hotdogs". She blurted and said "Baka di mo lang afford".
Dafuq?!! Pandak! Unano!

I had rice with a beef dish it was salty but it was yummy . I went there today with my step dad and my twin sister for lunch . I had a can of zero sugar coke for my drink.  I also had dessert with was some kind of biscuit or cake with purple cream with peanuts or almonds on top it was good .

Got the sausages, eggplant and sisig which were all delicious. Everything was very flavourful, sisig wasn’t too spicy. Also checked out the desserts and got the green sago and jelly one which was really good, not too sweet. Also tried the cheese chiffon cake which was very fluffy topped with savoury cheese, $4 for one. Service was quick and friendly too.
One stop-shop for Pinoy food & grocery. The owner is lovely and the store is very clean. Food is delicious but pricey.

I haven’t tried much from here aside from the baked goods, but I always pick up a Spanish bread on Sundays after my grocery run, it’s a must!
Highly recommend any of their pastries or breads, everything I’ve had has been delicious.
The owners are always lovely and provide such warm customer service.

name: Filipino Imports
address: 113 Queen St, North Strathfield NSW 2137, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.

address: Unit 1/28 Crawford Rd, Brighton-Le-Sands NSW 2216, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.

name: Filipino native cuisine
address: Woods St, Riverstone NSW 2765, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.

name: Filo Lasa Blacktown
address: Level 1, Shop 1025/17 Patrick St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Serving size is a bit small for the price. Plates are an extra 20c.

The sisig was the only dish I enjoyed. Though it can be a bit oily for some, the flavour is good. Good thing ends there. Kaldereta flavour was good, but the beef was a bit tough. Garlic rice should be renamed to butter rice with garlic bits on top and not even lukewarm. The bbq (chicken and pork) were cold and tough as well.

Service was just normal.

$12 BBQ pork skewer with garlic rice and coconut water. Great value for the portion. The pork was fatty in the best way, juicy, flavorful, and still thick and meaty.

Easily some of the best Filipino BBQ I’ve had.

The spot is in a busy food court near the escalators and elevators, so there is always a lot of foot traffic, but that is just the nature of the location.

Definitely my go to whenever I am in the area.
I wanted Filipino food and Filo Lasa in Blacktown hit the spot. The food gave me a taste of home. I highly recommend the turon!

Most take away shops fill up the containers that the food spills. At Filo Lasa, the container is only 80% filled. When I brought it up, the server defensively responded by saying that they weighed the food. And that it should be 400 grams. The serving is also poor even when you dine in.

Great food but measly quantity. Ladies were giving Caucasian customers large quantities and friendly smiles but gave me smaller portions and a cold attitude. Maybe ill bring a white friend next time lol. Staff will treat me like a King.

Response: thanks for acknowledging but your staff needs to treat customers equally. I've been here more than 5 times now and never had a positive interaction with your staff. They're cold and look annoyed to serve you but yesterday was different when they were very happy to serve others but when I stepped in the same rude attitude came across. As I said, I love the food but it's pushing customers like me away.

name: Filo Lasa Penrith
address: Food Terrace, L 2/585 High St, Penrith NSW 2750, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Delicious filo food served with amazing garlic rice. The bbq pork is beautiful aswell. A touch on the pricey side for the serving but worth it as this food takes time to cook and isn’t slapped together in 1 minute like a Big Mac.
Mum’s favorite go-to place for palabok. Their sago’t gulaman, halo-halo, mais con yelo, and ube jam taste like home!
Jerald, Jillian and Yan are very accommodating! Excellent service! Delicious food! Nice Spaghetti and Fried Chicken! Yum!

Delicious bbq pork with garlic rice 🤤. Also loved their pork skewers, good amount of fat on it.

I'm also hooked on their bakso pandan drink,  it reminds me of chendol but in liquid form with coconut water, a lighter than expected drink. It also contains coconut bits.

The dishes that Filipinos can get wrong this place does so right!
Their pancit is flavourful, their chicken is so good, their spaghetti is just like jollibee, my 8 year old loves their dinaguan, and their drinks are not overly sweet. They are all so friendly and sometimes it may take a while but you can see they are doing their best to get the orders out. Reasonably priced too! Surprised it does not have a higher rating.

name: Filo Pantry Mini Mart
address: McFarlane St, Merrylands NSW 2160, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: For all your Filipino snacks, spices, food and sweet cravings,  visit Filo Pantry. They also have the cheapest but quality coffee and hot choco drinks. Shop is only 200 meters from Merrylands Station.
You feel right at home with the friendly service and a great selection of products that give you a little boost if you’re feeling a little home sick.
Thanks boys great service
if you cook and appreciate good ingredients, I love this place
Good and friendly service

name: Filo Pride Asian Grocery, Eatery and Catering
address: 211 Marrickville Rd, Marrickville NSW 2204, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I have tried several eateries/cafes around Sydney, Lidcombe, Kogarah, Rockdale, Newtown. This place by far is my favourite! Food is pretty much on point. I tried the Dinuguan and Palabok, I was not disappointed it took me back to my roots and felt so happy. The place was neat and tidy with many Filipino groceries on offer and their customer service was excellent. We will definitely be coming back. Salamat Po!

We loved all their Filipino dishes when we went for the buffet. For $25/person, it was very much worth it. The flavours were spot on and transports you back to the Philippines with their authentic Filipino traditional flavours. We also like shopping for basic Filipino pantry items. Everyone from their team is very friendly and customer service was exceptional. Will definitely be back and highly recommend this shop.

The staff are friendly, accommodating and always have a welcoming smile to greet you as you enter their establishment.  They showcase authentic delicious Filipino cuisine which you have the option to eat all you can style.  Their per dish meal is also available if you are not that hungry.  Their wide variety of Filipino desserts are available as well.  They also have the basic Filipino ingredients they sell if you want to cook your own food or restock your Filipino pantry.  This is an all in one stop to satisfy your Filipino cravings.

Great place to get my filo essentials. The lady serving me was so lovely & friendly.

There were a few filo ladies talking on the high tables having a chat with the staff which I thought was nice touch.

There is a wide selection of takeaway food options which I’ll hopefully get to try next time if I haven’t already eaten lunch.

Good selection of snacks, desserts & filo pantry essentials.

I visited this Filipino grocery in Marrickville during Marrickville Music festival. They sell hot foods and on the weekends & they also sell Halo Halo! I tried the pork & chicken skewers and they were tasty. I’m keen to try their other foods next time!

name: Filoship Connect Seventh-day Adventist Church
address: Unit 4/11 Stoddart Rd, Prospect NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: My church that I attend, a Filipino Adventist community that ranges from babies to older persons. Every last Saturday of the month is usually closed, but otherwise we meet every Saturday. We usually have main service first at 930am for approx 1hr, 5min break then Sabbath school at 1130am-1230pm, then potluck lunch. We have an Adventurers Club, Pathfinders Club, active youth team, passionate leadership team, and friendly members. Please send a message to us if you have any questions, prayer requests or any other needs that we can help meet. We hope to connect to other Filipinos in the community, and anyone and everyone are welcome. Main service is in English, and Sabbath school classes are all English except 2 adult classes- Tagalog and Bisaya.
Youthful and active church. Excellent potlucks!

name: Filo’s Casa Cucina
address: 155 Georges River Rd, Croydon Park NSW 2133, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Absolutely hands-down one of the best meals I’ve had. Everything was fresh, full of flavour, and cooked perfectly authentic Filo foods. Portions were generous without being heavy, and you can tell real care went into every dish. Service was friendly and relaxed, which just made the whole experience better. Already thinking about coming back  highly recommend.
Came in today for a feed with my family had great time friendly staff great service experience 👍 👌 really recommended coming here !!! for all your flio food needs take away as well 💯🙂

Guys sobraaaang sarap! If you're craving authentic, heart-hugging Filipino food, this place is it. I've been dreaming about tocino because it's my absolute favourite, and honestly... this is the best tocino I've had in Sydney. No competition. And the rest of their food? Slay. Every. Single. Dish.

I'm already planning my next visit and bringing the whole fam because good food is meant to be shared.

A huge shoutout to Chester and Chef Diane~ you made us feel so at home. Your warmth and hospitality went above and beyond, and we felt genuinely cared for.
I had a chance to eat here , I loved the ambiance of the restaurant, service was good , the quality of the food is great too .

The first thing I noticed when I walked in was the atmosphere — warm, welcoming, and instantly comforting. It’s the kind of place where you can relax and feel right at home. The staff were friendly and attentive, which made the whole experience even better.

The servings were huge and definitely worth the price. The food was exceptional. The tinola tasted so authentic that it felt like enjoying a homemade meal back in the Philippines — rich, comforting, and full of flavour.

The daing na bangus served with mango was another highlight. The combination added a refreshing touch that made the dish even more enjoyable.

The menu also has a great variety to choose from, which makes it even more exciting. I’m already looking forward to coming back and trying their chicharon bulaklak — it’s definitely on my list for next time.

Overall, I had a wonderful experience. Great food, generous portions, and such a cozy atmosphere. If you’re craving genuine Filipino comfort food with plenty of options, this place is absolutely worth visiting. I’ll definitely be returning!

name: Frango Drummoyne
address: Shop 1 187/189 Lyons Rd, Drummoyne NSW 2047, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: No car parking available Drumoyne. looks change of ownership from the very opening all workers from overseas i wonder why?you order your meal never ready you send your family member to please ask if ready yet so to understand they say one minute then nothing.  Always the weekend this issue not nice service you only a number. Bad management cold food and ridiculous all people customers waiting like school line up. Just a shambles.  Hygiene not look good.  Better place is concord Frango amazing i ever seen.  Organise and beautiful workers all happy and who ever the Owner should be given a five star medal 🏅.    Bravo and express service there all sit down service never dirty.  We'll organise is the key.  Not oh yeah bad never bad manners at concord nsw beautiful customers as well LOTS mix nationality especially Aussies that expect humble in return.    Not just a number.     Thats what makes it happy not just yeah get in the line.

Not as great as the reviews say (honestly Ogalos chips & burgers r much better), but decent portions.  Nice big wash basin at the back, is needed for messy hands!  I took a photo of their beaut massive flame griller.  Unfortunately the chicken burger patty looks great in the $11 Original Burger, but is not as crispy or tender as say Ogalos.  Again, with the $15 Ciabatta steak roll the meat was decent but a little bit too chewy.  See my photos of both.  Their famous chilli dressing with the mayo & even their chicken salt, r also not as good as tasty as Ogalo or Oporto.  The fries & half BBQ chicken ($16 combo with can of drink not bad as chips alone are $9.90) were v generous, the skin was nice & the drumsticks too, not too much bones on the breast meat but slightly dry as breat meat tends to be.  I'm unsure of this place has different cooks or management to say 2023 or even 2024 reviews that I saw (they are Indian I think?  Efficient & business like) but defo not the best portuguese fast food in Sydney, but I rate it above average mainly due to portion size.  Not bad but I wouldn't go out of my way to eat here, a friend who is a local said they prefer the Chargrill Charlies across the same main road.

There was one comment about the chips being crispy on the outside but soft on the inside - and I agree 101%. So much better paired with their chili sauce! The chicken is so so good. When I hear charcoal chicken, this is the quality and taste I am expecting. Reminds me of the Philippines!

We’ve been to this place 4x now and we’ve only been in the area for a week. Would recommend.

I just have to tell you about this amazing local spot in Sydney! First off, the chicken was out of this world - perfectly seasoned and cooked to perfection. But the burger was also a real winner with all the right ingredients, and the sauce was spot on.

But let's talk about the chips - oh my goodness, they're a game changer! Crispy on the outside, fluffy on the inside, and seasoned to perfection. And get this - even though there was a dinner rush, I was able to score a table without any wait time. The staff were so friendly and efficient, making my experience all the more enjoyable.

Overall, this local gem is a must-visit for anyone who loves good food and great service. The restaurant has a relaxed atmosphere and it's clear that the team is passionate about what they do. I'm already planning my next visit to try the rice and pork belly - I have no doubt it will be just as delicious as everything else on the menu!

Absolutely amazing place to eat!! Everything was made fresh, and the staff were so friendly and nice. 2 Lovely young girls were so kind and welcoming to me as one of them took my order. I will definitely be coming back!!

name: Fresh Food Market Rooty Hill
address: 56 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews: I always love to come here even we are quite far. But it's just everything  is here the stuff that Filipinos  needs in their kitchen  and also with a reasonable  prices and a lot of offers and we can get a bargain price in their selective  items.

This morning, the butcher at the meat shop was clearly in a bad mood, frowning as if she had no interest in working. It really put a damper on my day. Before us, an elderly woman was trying to buy something, but it seemed like they were reluctant to serve her. The man working even snapped at her. Such behavior is unacceptable and will definitely hurt the shop's reputation. Customers shouldn't have to deal with that kind of treatment, especially from staff who are supposed to be professional and courteous.

DONT WORK PLEASE!!!!!

I love doing my grocery shopping here. Everything is so cheap compared to Coles/woolies. Fruits and veggies are really cheap and fresh. Their meat section is really good too. I only shop here maybe once a month and buy a lot. My only issue is they don’t have trolleys that you can take to your car so you pretty much have to carry everything. Otherwise still a good place to shop.

don't buy the bulalo meat on the meat area paid $43.91 and this bones weighs like a kilo so i paid $15.99 for this bones! 😑

be careful!
Cheaper price but poor quality. Rotten egg plant wrapped in plastic bag had tiny booklice bugs in it. And btw isn’t it is illegal to sell rotten vegetables.

name: Granville Community Baptist Church
address: 104 South St, Granville NSW 2142, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews:
Lovely Filipino church gathering on Saturday afternoons, on the last Saturday of the month, when there aren't Covid restrictions that is(!). They have a few games, a message and usually lots of good food afterwards.

Great family feel.
Great family Church!

name: Greenknowe Cafe
address: 8 Greenknowe Ave, Elizabeth Bay NSW 2011, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: A Filo inspired menu that is modern take of favourite Pinoy  dishes. We started with the Adobo spring rolls with the vinegarette/ketchup sauce on the side as the usual condiment (sawsawan). We then had Nasi Goreng in brown rice and a full bowl of Batangas Lomi. We are happy customers and will be back again. Would love to see more protein dishes in the menu like chicken and fish.

Greenknowe Café is a charming, Filo‑inspired brunch spot known for its excellent coffee, creative menu, and genuinely welcoming service. The atmosphere is cosy yet lively, with both indoor seating and a pleasant outdoor terrace that locals love. A café with personality, great flavours, and a neighbourhood feel

Neat little cafe tucked away in Potts Point. Was very busy when we arrived , but food and drinks still arrived in a timely manner. Great variety of food from bacon and egg rolls to filipino comfort food. Make sure to ask for any specials for the day ! 10/10

Best little Filipino fusion cafe tucked away on Greenknowe Ave! The pork belly in the Ultimate Tocino was perfectly cooked and I’ll pay Phil’s (Ube) Pancakes the ultimate Asian compliment: “not too sweet!” I cannot wait to come back and try their other delectables. 🤤

Ordered the Tocino and Beef Tapa and it was absolutely wonderful! Coffee was great too! Great cafe that offer a cozy environment to sit back and relax. Most importantly the service and people are really great and friendly . Will definitely come back again!

name: HALO BanhMi
address: 52 The Crescent, Fairfield NSW 2165, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: ​"As a local business owner, I always come here to support this shop. The quality is excellent and it reminds me of the great Filipino cooking we have at home! The team from Cash for cars near me are regulars here because we value quality and good service. Highly recommended to anyone looking for authentic Filipino products!"

Great Banh Mis love their own sauce they use there, just lost a star because the last 3 times I went they were closed!! And it wasnt a sunday.
I love their Banh Mis but theyre hard to get 😔

New face in town but cannot go wrong when you craving for Vietnamese Banh Mi bread rolls. Bringing the “tune” to Fairfield station that I used to walk pass as boring. Not anymore, stop by and have some breads. The sound of bread crunchy by melting in your mouth with all ingredients inside purely homemade with effort and love to share the authenticity of Vietnamese northern taste bread rolls to everyone. The pate’, viet butter made by shop fresh and soft are the specialty you looking for.
Strongly recommended 👍🏼

I bought Vietnamese coffee turns out really bad, super sweet powdery and bitter. Not gonna buy if I never drink that. Explain to me how Vietnamese coffee was like I’ve never drink before. Not even ask either to exchange or offer better option. 1 sip then trash bin. First and last time visit this outlet. I was expecting a very good Vietnamese coffee coz it’s been prepared by their own people but nah.
Tried the Vietnamese sticky rice bowl paired with fresh juice — it was delicious and generously portioned!

name: Halo Halo Cafe
address: 1/20-22 Wentworth St, Parramatta NSW 2150, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Seriously! A hidden gem don't tell anyone .... comy cushions on wicker seats ..  so clean and nice..  served in a cup and saucer with a smile ... fantastic coffee👍👍😁😁😁

Thank you Halo Halo Cafe for the generous gift. Such a lovely, yummy, perfect birthday treat! Lucky to have a great cafe close-by where we’re always treated like family. 🤩 we always look forward to coming back because of the yummy food and great service!

Such a good place to eat! My family and friends love going here anytime we have a celebration for their gorgeous brunch options. I also work next to the cafe and their takeaway options are fantastic for my work lunch breaks xx

Halo Halo Café, the cozy, beautifully decorated space creates a welcoming ambiance. Their coffee is rich and creamy, used The Grounds Coffee Roastery. The friendly staff go above and beyond to ensure a delightful experience. Whether you’re looking for a peaceful spot to relax or catch up with friends, this café is the perfect choice. Highly recommended for its great atmosphere, fantastic service, and flavorful offerings!

Very homely and friendly service, food was also delicious. They are also inspired by Vietnamese food, explaining why banh mi is on the menu. The barbecue pork banh mi was flavourful and filling. The adobo loco moco is delicious, though rice was slightly greasy.
Halo halo is one of my favourite desserts and they make it very well here, definitely justified ordering it for our brunch.
Good coffee and the mug size is huge!

name: Hanmades Bakehouse Windsor
address: Shop 3/70 George St, Windsor NSW 2754, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
great find in windsor. service was exceptional. loved that they let you try everything. so many options.
if you like ube you’re set.
the tocino banh mi was great. personally i prefer a crispy roll. but the filling was 10/10.

will be back.
Nice new cafe in the Windsor area. They have delicious Filipino Fusion Banh Mi. The Tocino Banh Mi was sooo good. Also really enjoyed their drinks topped with banana pudding. So many to try.

I placed my first order today.
A pork banh mi. Terrible!
The paper bag was wet when handed to me. A stale bread roll, soft not crunchy. A thick layer of butter. Pork & vegies not fresh. Disgusting! Do yourself a favour & DO NOT order the banh mi. I hope other items on the menu are better but i won't even go in there again!

Great new addition to windsor tried their ube cheese bread sample was great and coffee was well done but the shinning star was their red velvet cheesecake cookie 2 moist fresh cookies with a great layer of cheesecake filling a must have definitely will be back and look forward to trying more
Misleading advertising on Facebook, that the menu items are available at Windsor and Eastern creek. When I went to Windsor, they say certain items are only available at Eastern Creek.

name: Hanmade’s Bakehouse
address: 159 Rooty Hill Rd S, Eastern Creek NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews: Solid place for Filipino food and dessert. Restaurant had a great selection of desserts including their famous silvana (frozen cookie). Had their beef pares (beef stew). Beef was tender. The soup was flavourful and beef. Really went well with the garlic rice. Also their Halo Halo is great as well.

I was searching for a Filipino Baker where I can order my son's cake which is near my area and Handmade's Bakehouse comes 1st on the list.☺️
Check their website and emailed them immediately. Ms Hannah and Jessica is so responsive and very easy to deal with.
The cake taste nice but I think for a buttercream cake with just a little edible design 170$ is a bit too much. If you can consider checking the prices will be very much appreciated.
Overall experience is great.♥️

I also tried their cupcakes and the Taho taste amazing! 👌
My kids love them.

I was a bit disappointed that Halo Halo wasn’t available when I visited, since that was the main reason I stopped by. But I decided to try their mango lemonade instead, and it turned out to be a decent choice — refreshing, balanced, and easy to enjoy.

What really stood out, though, was the staff. Super friendly, welcoming, and happy to help. That kind of service always makes a difference and definitely left a good impression.

I didn’t get to try their food this time, but the menu looked promising. I’m keen to come back when Halo Halo is in stock and give their dishes a proper go — and I’m pretty sure my rating will go up once I do!

Yema Cupcake is awesome. Soft and just about right sweetness in it.

New York Cheesecake - nah. Should've just left it to New Yorkers. Didn't finsih it.

Visited Hanmades Bakehouse in Windsor and was genuinely impressed. I grabbed a bahn mi (pork roll) and it was excellent — fresh bread, generous filling, great flavour, and made properly (not skimpy on the pork). Service was friendly and efficient, and everything looked fresh and well presented. Highly recommend if you’re in the area.

name: Hannah's Beecroft
address: 5.107 Beecroft Place, Level 1/18 Hannah St, Beecroft NSW 2119, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Classy little hidden gem that was indeed "worth the climb up the stairs". Extensive Japanese-inspired menu with a decent collection of shokupan sando and lunch options. However dinner options are not available on some of the days so please check before coming for dinner. Impressive wine collection and Mountain Culture beer on tap. We were very happy with the service experience from start to finish. Pricing was fair to boot. Can definitely recommend if you're in the Beecroft/Pennant Hills area.

We were  looking forward to trying this out. We were ignored for the first 15mins we arrived and then when we politely said excuse me can we please order we were told by the waiter( who we think was the owner) “well that’s one way to get attention” .  Some  dishes were yummy; the ceviche was bland and uninspiring. The owner/waiter was morose and didn’t acknowledge us or speak to us at all. We’re not quite sure we did to offend him other than be there. Would not go again
The food is quite good, but it’s quite expensive considering you are supposed to order more dishes of tapas per person

Tucked away in the corner above the shops. Offering an intimate dinning feel with both indoor and outdoor seating. Bar and tapas style dishes paired well with their good selection of wines. Keep an eye on the specials board for a deal.

I know Hannah's has been there for a while. But we have never been, silly us, because it was great.
We went Easter Sunday morning, it is quiet and relaxing. Breakfast was very nice with 2 totally clean plates going back to the kitchen. Knowing what it was like we will be back again, lunch, dinner or drinks!!!!
For reference we had Mexican Smashed Avocado & Shokupan French Toast and coffee of course.

name: Harvey's Social
address: Doonside Rd, Arndell Park NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews: Ordered the fish and chips. Great value for $25.00. Couldn’t finish all the chips. The chips were beautiful. Crunchy, soft on the inside and well seasoned. Up there among the best chips I’ve had. The fish was also well cooked and the batter nice and crunchy and a nice serving of tartare sauce.

My all time favourite cafe, the best coffee out, amazing all day breakfast menu, food options for the kids, refreshing smoothies and just something for everyone. This Amazing little Gem is also great for dog owners, located few steps away from the Dog Park.
Amazing staff and service always an extra bonus on the morning rush to work. Thanks harveys 😀
Genuinely one of the best food I have ever tasted. The staffs are amazing and the quietness of the park is a plus!!

The staff are exceptional, I have been there so many times the staff feel like family.  There are lots of great food and drink options. They also do some great specials and occasionally have special Friday night meals e.g. Filipino. A great local small business that is worth support.

Beautiful area to relax at. Awesome food, very quick service!
As someone who loves orange juice, there's might be the best I've ever had. I had to order two haha.
Will come back again :)

name: Heebie Jeebz
address: 2/95 Harrow Rd, Glenfield NSW 2167, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Had the Angus beef burger and the breakfast burrito. Was really good, nice and fresh. Service was great and welcoming. Hopefully the community get around our small businesses more and support. Great prices, great atmosphere. Looking forward to my next trip for breakfast/lunch here again.

Absolutely loved this cafe! From the moment I walked in, the staff made me feel like a regular. Everyone was so happy, welcoming, and genuinely kind—it completely lifted my mood. The atmosphere is cozy and full of positive energy, and you can tell they really care about creating a great experience for their customers. Can’t wait to come back!

Update 2 yrs after initial review - still love this place… they still care about their customers, food is still top notch quality and I was especially excited to try their strawberry matcha latte today.. it’s pretty, tastes perfect and not too sweet. Love it! New photo added!

Love this place. We come here a few times a week. Joel and Jordan are awesome- heaps of laughs. Food is delicious and coffee is great. Love the pancit, charcoal chicken, and the fresh baked pastries are awesome. Coffee is so good! Highly recommended!

Searched on google for a place nearby Costco in Casula to stop at for lunch on our way home out of Sydney. Wanted something affordable but a bit healthier than standard fast food and stumbled across this gem! The food was made fresh and was delicious! Fantastic service! Highly recommend.

I’m extremely disappointed with my experience at this cafe.

We ordered a bacon and eggs, a whole chicken (which was supposed to come with garlic sauce, bread, and pickles), chips, and a drink. the total was $38.

When the food arrived, the chips portion was ridiculously small, and the whole chicken came with only garlic sauce — no bread or pickles. We had to ask for the bread separately, and when it finally came, IT HAD MOLD ON IT.

We were disgusted and told the staff. They took the bread away and offered to make us a chicken wrap instead. But seriously? The wrap bread is what had mold on it in the first place! Why would you recommend a wrap made with moldy bread?

No one offered us a refund or even properly apologized. The whole situation was unacceptable, and I’m still shocked by how poorly it was handled.

I won’t be coming back, and I definitely wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone.

name: Hi Dins kitchen
address: 226 Queen St, St Marys NSW 2760, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Hi Dins is our go to for Filipino Desserts and Empanadas. Their classic pulled pork empanada, creamy chicken and sundried beef will not disappoint. The golden, flaky crust had just the right amount of crunch, giving way to a savory, well-seasoned filling.

Can't fault this place - price on food is unbeatable!! The setting was nice and clean and it doesn't feel like you're just walking in another cafe/eatery - it feels welcoming and cosy. Despite the lack of parking due to a busy major street at St. Mary's, trust and believe the food will more than make up for the walk!!
Had the chance to try out a few of their dishes including the tapsilog, tocilog, lechon paksiw and pork emapmadas (the best in the west, I promise!!) and needless to say, it was beyond my expectations! (and they were high!) The flavours were spot on, nothing too salty or bland - it was perfect! I need not to say anymore - if my exclamation marks weren't enough to dress this place with praise then you're on own and YOURE MISSING OUT! *mic drop*

Cannot believe I haven't left a review yet as I have been ordering from here for years. The food is always fantastic, the prices are extremely fair and affordable. My family always asks for Empanadas when we host events.

The owners are extremely nice and accommodating. It is clear they are passionate and give great care and attention to their products.

If you come here you will not regret it.

I bought traditional pork and chicken empanadas in this place today and can definitely say that they are amazing! I brought a box of 20 to a party and everyone absolutely loved it. Would definitely recommend!
Pulled pork empanadas with quail egg was a crowd pleaser! Very warm accomodating service.

name: Hills of Zion City Church Sydney
address: 34A Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: A Filipino English-speaking church that knows no boundaries in reaching people and ushering His church to Jesus.
All attendees of this church is so friendly and I really feel the presence of our God in here.

name: Hua Yang Supermarket
address: 11 Penshurst St, Penshurst NSW 2222, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: If you need fresh cheap, vegetables, and fruit they have it. Various Filipino frozen, and canned goods, ice cream they also have it. No need to go to blacktown. I'll add photos soon
Great selection and cheap prices.
Found my Filipino goodies here!

name: Hudson George
address: 56/16 Victoria Ave, Castle Hill NSW 2154, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews:
An unexpected eating spot in HomeCo. Castle Hill, which we have passed by many times. We thought we would grab a quick lunch after shopping and we were pleasantly surprised.

The eating area is spacious with glass windows and lots of greenery.  I enjoyed the wellness bowl and my husband had the lamb souvlaki.

Our food was delicious with fresh salads and vegetables and the service was good, welcoming and friendly.
Coffee was good. The food was not bad, however, they should work on their plating. Perhaps add a salad to fill the gap on the plate?

We informed staff that we spotted around 6 cockroach in different parts of the dining area (surely there's alot more) and they just said sorry so we just wait for food to arrive. then here comes the kicker! food came and first bite was like a different taste/feel for the dish and lo and behold MOLD is spotted on my food! all staff did is a partial refund which even took a while as they chatted amongst them then gave partial. I have taken photos and have submitted proper report to local council and nsw food authority.

Me and my mum came here for breakfast and discovered the massive portion sizes and high quality food for great prices and we had a great time and I would recommend you get the Nutella thick shake because it’s delicious and my mum recommends the avocado toast

Been here a few times for brunch. Love the setup and the wide windows. Service is fab. Good cafe food but have only tried the breakfast menu. Have to check out the lunch as today found they have Filipino adobo on the menu. Will be back to try it.

name: Ingleburn RSL Club
address: 70 Chester Rd, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews: It is just another RSL. Only went there as I was in area late at night. The staff were very friendly. The two piece band Alter Ego? (I think Philippines?) were terrific. Plenty of classic tunes well sung and made sure the few patrons present were involved. Fun time.
Very tasty beef roast with efficient,  friendly staff. Unnfortunatly They didn't have apple  sauce for the Pork roast. Good selection of on tap beers. We will return soon.
Super friendly and helpful staff. Specials menus are amazing. Have had some beautiful meals here recently

We came around Thursday lunchtime. Popular place, particularly with the older crowd around this time. We had a table in the second room as it was quieter. Self-serve order and drinks. The food were good portion sizes. Most main courses were around $25 upwards.

Today during our lunch, I observed one of the serving staff, William, go out of his way to ensure an elderly lady didn’t feel left out after she had initially missed a table. He quickly guided her to a nearby table and made sure she was comfortable. It was a wonderful example of a young person showing care and respect for our elders. Throughout the service, his manner was consistently warm, attentive, and welcoming to all guests. Great job, William. ❤️👌🏾

name: Inigo's Cafe & Grill
address: 22 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We’re so happy that Inigo opened a branch here in the West, closer to our home. My husband and I dined there yesterday and were extremely satisfied with the food, service, and the clean, elegant ambiance.
We ordered the pork barbecue skewers, crispy beef kare-kare, and crispy dinuguan. Everything was delicious, beautifully presented, and truly went beyond our expectations.
It’s wonderful to support a Filipino family-owned and managed restaurant serving authentic Pinoy recipes with a unique twist. The young gentleman at the counter, who served our food and made the coffee we ordered, was very accommodating, polite, and professional.
I’ve also tried their sisig at the Bexley branch, and it’s one of the best sisig dishes I’ve tasted in Australia.
We will surely come back to try other menu items. Highly recommended to our Filipino friends and to other cultures who would love to experience authentic Filipino cuisine!
Every meal came out from the kitchen was absolutely delicious! Couldn’t fault any of it! Lovely chill vibes. And service is great! 100% recommend it for people to try! ❤️😊

My husband and I went around the afternoon. The resto ambiance and decor was clean and simple. So my expectations for the food was good or above. We ordered the following;
Sisig- salty and a let down. We didn’t finished it even my husband who loves sisig one taste only.
Grilled na liempo- disappointed just looking at it ketchup marination.
Pansit guisado- very oily and salty. I think the soy sauce they used is dark soy sauce.
Bulalo - i was going to order this because i only wanted to have a hot soup. Other filo resto gives out hot soup.But they told me they don’t give out sabaw so i ordered the bulalo. Looks ok but the after taste is bitter and burn. We found burn pieces of meat in the soup.
Inihaw na pusit- maybe this one is the one that we finished even though its not smoky. The squid was soft and not salty at all. That’s why i gave 2 stars on the review otherwise only 1 star overall.
Garlic rice- its ok.

Service- the lady in the cashier was cheerful and friendly. I think she’s a bit overwhelmed being all around cashier, server, table We don’t mind waiting as we know filo food takes time to cook.

Overall we went there wanted to try it and if its good we will definitely come back. But the food was a let down. I wanted to come back and support pinoy so i can recommend it to other pinoy but i find the food lacking and very salty. Will not come back.

Would have never known about this place if it wasn’t for EatClub. The atmosphere was unfortunately very loud because of the music, you could hardly talk to each other and enjoy a conversation, which made the dining experience less enjoyable.

The food was overall okay for Filipino comfort food. We ordered the sisig taco, chicken inasal rice plate, lechon kawali rice plate, and mango sago for dessert.

The sisig taco was good, though a bit heavy, but had very good flavours.

The chicken inasal rice plate was great and had adequate, well balanced flavours.

The lechon kawali, however, was a big disappointment. It was extremely dry and very oily, with more fat than meat and crispy skin. I understand lechon kawali can sometimes be dry since it’s pan or deep-fried, but this one was so dry and hard it almost felt like it could break a tooth. The amount of vinegar sauce I had to use on my rice just to eat the dish to cut out the fatty/oil taste.

For dessert, the mango sago was much thicker than I prefer. Generally I am used to a bit more runny mango sago which has more flavour but the thickness made it less appealing to eat more of a pudding based.

The servers though were welcoming and friendly though. But it took the awhile to acknowledge us and sat us to a table.

Overall, it was average Filipino comfort food, which was a bit disappointing.

We had such a great experience at this Filipino café! We ordered the Fairy Halo-Halo, Ube Latte, Lechon Kawali, fried rice, and lumpia — and everything was absolutely delicious. The Fairy Halo-Halo had a modern twist but still tasted so homey and comforting. The Ube Latte is my new favourite coffee order. The Lechon Kawali was perfectly crispy and flavorful, and the lumpia and fried rice were just as amazing.
The café itself is very neat and beautifully modern, with subtle hints of Filipino-inspired design that add so much character to the space. I loved how it felt both stylish and culturally warm at the same time. You can really tell they put thought into both the food and the atmosphere.
Highly recommend if you’re craving authentic Filipino flavors in a modern yet homey setting!

name: JAS Kan-anan
address: Shop 4/20 George St, Hornsby NSW 2077, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Love love the people here!
They cook with their hearts & souls!

Their dishes are right outta your mum’s or grandma’s kitchen - only BETTER becoz every dish is a 5* high standard!

I appreciate that they use good amounts of fats in their meats and fat=flavour.
Many times we will order takeaway after our meal coz it was sooooo good but we couldn’t eat anymore!
We have tried and love the lumpia, beef kare, pork sinigang, pork humba, lechon paksiw, inihaw baboy, cebu lechon, bbq stick!

They have also made exceptions for my dairyfree diet requirements (breastfeeding a baby with CMPI) & prepared their daily food for us🙏🏻.

Thank you and we are grateful for your kindness and gift of cooking!

Good food. Nice ambience, it may look small but very warm, welcoming staff. Price good on your pocket.

3rd review edit now. This place, every time I come here. Food just gets yummier and more fav filo food.

2nd review  edit. Sisig is awesome now after my first review. They made an effort to make their food the best. Please continue to thrive to be a good Filo restaurant.

Soup of the Pork Sinigang is the best. You can also buy takeaway and party platters. There's a pasalubong foodie for the family as well. I have tried chicharon, and it tastes very nice. Puto, very yummy in deed (forgot to take pictures).

Met the owner the other day and an awesome person and created an awesome place.

I happened to come across this gem of a restaurant, and I'm so glad I did! If you're craving authentic & affordable Filipino food in the Hornsby area, this place is a must visit! We had the sisig, bbq skewers and puto. All so good! Great service and friendly staff as well

Food was great. Specially the lechon. Super yunmy and crunchy.

You can easily spot the place coz of the signage. Very friendly staff as well. Will definitely comeback again. This time for the halo halo and the chicharon bulaklak. 😍

Best filo spag and fried chicken I've had in Sydney, full of flavour! I did not understand the appeal of this combination at other restaurants, but here I understand the appeal. Crunchy, juicy chicken and sweet, peppery, savoury spaghetti. Their silog meals are also great.

name: JJ Variety Store
address: Suite 1/450 High St, Penrith NSW 2750, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: I have been their customer for almost 7 years. This shop is one of the best Thai groceries in Sydney. The shop looks so tiny but they have almost all products you need for cooking Thai food so no more traveling to Thai town for getting specific ingredients. We even purchased our Moo Krata pan here. Price is reasonable. The owners are helpful and always welcoming. It is our to-go place for grocery shopping every weekend.

Absolutely the best customer service, the lady working there is so helpful! Heard from others they just have thai groceries but going in myself I found a variety of asian products which is great. Wonderful selection of korean, chinese, viet, Filipino, japanese, etc. Highly recommend going for all your asian product needs, will be going back soon to stock up on snack and fresh veggies for my soup
Wide assortment of Thai snacks, beverages and ingredients, including salad dressing and sauces.
Got alots of Thai food there.
Great variety of authentic Thai products, drinks, food, sauces and more. I highly recommend going shopping here

name: JT Supermarket
address: 152 Best Rd, Seven Hills NSW 2147, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Best Place to shop in Seven Hills with all Nepali, Indian, Srilankan , Philippine and Korean stuff, at best price you can think of.
Great little Asian grocery store with good selection and friendly service. Always find what I need🫶

name: JoElla Golden Delights
address: Parklea NSW 2768, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: If you're craving BBQ, this spot delivers an unforgettable experience. Their grilled BBQ chicken is a flavour bomb — smoky, succulent, and seasoned just right. The chicken is perfectly charred on the outside, yet soft and juicy on the inside, making every bite better than the last.

Got a bbq chicken skewer roll, the chicken was delicious, very tender, marinated and with a nice smoky charcoal flavour but the salad in the roll was a little underwhelming but overall would come back next for just the chicken skewers. Service was quick and staff were friendly.

Delicious Filipino BBQ skewers! The meat was well-marinated and tender. A must-try! They are located outside of one of the entrances at Parklea Markets, but you can easily locate them if you just follow the smokey bbq smell in the air. Also, get a halo halo (Filipino dessert) next to their shop called Mix Mix. It's great to support local businesses.

Poor service, no signs up to advise what they’ve run out of and this was just before midday. No chicken skewers, only pork which was fine but 1 hour wait. Could see others frustrated after queuing to be told what they could put up on signs of what’s not available. Steamed buns took 30 mins but not told this before ordering. Had to return one as it was cold in the middle and all they did was microwave it without any ownership. Customer service could definitely be better and managing stock before the lunch time rush or at least better communication. Not worth the wait.

Good value and tasty bbq.
Food was not at optimal temp perhaps prorated slightly earlier ahead of lunch crowd.

name: Johnnie's Doonside
address: 257 Doonside Cres, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Drove past Jonnie’s a couple of times and saw they had burger steak so I had to give it a go and I was not disappointed…
I ordered the Burger steak, chicken Inasal and the kalamansi iced tea.
Everything was eaten within 20mins it was that good.

Burger steak - I haven’t had a good burger steak in a while and iv had many and Jonnie’s does it the best. Burger steak cooked perfectly and the gravy is the I’ve tasted in a long time all of that paired with the garlic rice very delicious. (highly recommend)

Chicken Inasal - chicken cooked perfectly juicy and tender, paired with the garlic rice and the sauce was also very delicious
(Highly recommend)

Kalamansi iced tea - very refreshing not so watery not to sweet it was made just right and is now my new favourite drink
(Highly recommend)

Pearl was also very lovely, welcoming and fun to talk to. Place was clean and the atmosphere was great, I would’ve eaten there if it wasn’t so close to closing time.
Food was also very affordable with a huge menu. I will be returning again very soon to try some more food.

This is one of those places that hits all the marks — flavor, service, and experience.

We had the tapsilog, tocilog, spaghetti, and calamansi juice, and everything was spot on. The food was flavorful and comforting, just like home. The spaghetti especially reminded us of Jollibee-style sweetness done perfectly.

Johnnie was so personable and friendly, you can tell he genuinely cares about every guest and every plate that comes out of the kitchen. It’s the kind of place that makes you feel welcomed the moment you walk in.

Definitely a gem in Western Sydney. Can’t wait to come back!

We just arrived back from a holiday in the Philippines and was craving inasal chicken, spaghetti with fried chicken and came across this place - definitely did not disappoint! Very flavourful, cravings satisfied! 😊 Will definitely be back!

Found my new favourite place to dine.
Food is good and customer service is amazing. Reasonable pricing and quick service. 👌🥰 Would recommend 💯

We enjoyed our visit here. Owner was very nice and puts a lot of love in what he does. I enjoyed  The works beef burger and the jojos was decent. Crispy and freshly cooked fried chicken but maybe needing a little more seasoning. Took some time for the food to come out but I didn't mind it since we weren't in a rush. I Kalamansi juice was a little too sweet for me but still good. I'd come back and try one of the silogs next time.

name: Johnnie's Windsor
address: Unit 1/70 George St, Windsor NSW 2756, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: loved the inasal and garlic rice
Great food and service! Can't wait to go back and try more offerings

This place is great. It’s nice to see a different style cuisine in the area. The owner John was such a nice bloke. Had a nice chat with him. I used to eat from a lot of the restaurants/takeaways growing up in the area that have all closed down. It’s good to see a new place with heart. I recommend popping in for a bite and a chat.

Tried beef pares, palabok and chicken inasal. Chicken was so moist and tender but the baste could be more punchy. Palabok did not have the seafood hit I expected. Beef was so tender but gravy was a bit too sweet for me. It needs a bit more ginger and black pepper. But service is 12 out of 10. He catered for everything we requested.
Great taste! I miss eating pares. The pares here is really good! We'll be back!

name: Kanto Bagnet
address: 120 Crown St, Riverstone NSW 2765, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
If you’re near Riverstone and looking for great Filipino food, Kanto Bagnet is definitely worth a visit. It’s a food truck setup but they also have tables and chairs so you can dine in.

Their food tastes amazing, even the garlic rice, dips, and sauces are very flavorful. Our favorite is the Sisig Bagnet - crispy and tasty! Their crispy pork belly and pork BBQ come with a delicious Aristocrat-style sauce that makes every bite better. The chicken inasal is also very tasty, and you can drizzle their chicken oil over your rice if you want that “Mang Inasal” style experience.

They also offer weekly specials, so it’s good to ask what’s available. For snacks. we love their kwek-kwek and chicharon bulaklak, and their bulalo soup is rich and full of flavor. We are yet to try their desserts.

The owner and staff are very friendly, and they even have a filtered water machine for customers. We enjoy eating here so much!

Tip: Check their business hours on Google before visiting. To make sure your favorite dishes are still available, it’s also a good idea to give them a quick call ahead.

Tried Kanto Bagnet Sydney and really enjoyed the food. We ordered the bagnet sisig, java rice, laing, and pork belly BBQ. Everything was tasty and well cooked. The bagnet sisig was crispy and full of flavour, the laing was creamy with a nice bit of spice, and the pork belly BBQ was juicy and nicely grilled.

The java rice paired really well with everything. Overall, a solid spot if you’re craving Filipino food. Would definitely come back.

Kanto Bagnet has got to be my new favourite place. Every week I look through their specials and their “Veggie Friday”. Hindi nakakasawa. Lumpiang Shanghai, Munggo, Laing, Ampalaya with Bagnet, Palabok, BBQ..each bite takes me back home. Today they accidentally gave me the grilled liempo instead of the Laing (the liempo was good btw) but as soon as they realised their mistake, they straight away offered to deliver the Laing (and omg ang sarap ng Laing!). The best. Thank you Kanto Bagnet team for making our tummies happy. 😊
Prob the best Bagnet and Sisig i’ve had in Sydney. Must try! Truly tastes like home!
The food is fresh and delicious! They have large servings too!☺️❤️

name: Kapamilya Bakeshop & Takeaway Food
address: 1 Milgate Ln, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Great little Filipino bakery and food spot.
Tucked away around the corner but within easy access with plenty of parking.
Plenty of parking and friendly staff
The fresh baked cakes are awesome and great value. We enjoyed our banana turon and ube treats.
Customer Service was amazing. They explained all their dishes and we got to try some. We loved the flavours and the different dishes they serve. Lovely local Filipino baked goods and hot food!!
Breads are next level. Snacks ready to go in Empanadas, Siopao, Turon, and Lumpia. Dishes on go at the hot bar from Adobo, Humba, Laing, and Langka. Accessible and authentic Filipino food
Incredible food and amazing service! The woman serving us let us have tasters and everything tasted amazing. You have to try the fried banana with jackfruit sauce!

Amazing foods of the Philippines culture LOVE IT 😊 Typical friendly Filipino service and a clean shop. I RARELY leave reviews but the Kapamilya Bakeshop & Takeaway was a nice exotic treat and I recommend you try their exotic flavours 🍀

name: Kapamilya Groceries & Eatery
address: 28 Walz St, Rockdale NSW 2216, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: a good rendezvous for Filipinos especially on Sundays...

Was excited to eat filo food but their eat all you can isnt even worth it even its cheap the dishes didnt taste good it all tasted the same, the bbq are ok tho. They should hire a professional chef to cook not just anyone. And they promote this all you can eat rampantly on facebook like the food is awesome. Not. I also ordered for catering food was also bad and i just told the owner on fb it was good cuz i didnt want to be rude. Your better off watching panlasang pinoy on youtube and cooking it yourself and the dish will turn 100% delicious it wont disappoint you.

I went to the store recently, my fave saleslady does not work there anymore after 3yrs of enslavement and no pay by the owners. I was told she could not even pay a taxi to go to the airport. Maltreatment of a fellow Pinoy relative was their plan after all. #GoogleNewsThis. Stop supporting businesses like this. Bad store, bad owners!

Always nice to see a filipino eatery. Not many of them around & wished there were.  Food was tasty they had a good variety to choose from & same goes for the grocery. Good service.  Keep up the good work guys 😃

After recerching the place on FB , went for lunch with hubby worst ever food we have eaten in a philipino eatery.The man in charge was rude to the casher..?? Food was  rely terrible , poor quality and expensive for what you get   Whow how can people recommend the place????, Never again

name: Kasalo Filipino Cuisine
address: 42 Railway Parade, Granville NSW 2142, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Local Filipino food and grocery shop..
Family style cooking.

$15 rice + 1 dish
$18 rice + 2 dishes
Boodle fight food is well prepared. Staff-- they are accommodating. We held our baby shower and they even allowed us put some decor and conduct simple program. 👍👍👍

Extensive menu and friendly customer service!
We visited to try something different in the area. We were pleased to find such a comprehensive menu in comparison to most Filipino restaurants in Sydney.
The waiter was very friendly and helpful with explaining each dish to us.
Our fish and meat dishes were cooked perfectly.
We definitely recommend giving it a try as we'll be back to try more of the menu!

We usually order on uber eats and the food is consistently good. I really enjoy their spaghetti - brings back memories of my childhood. It's also not overly sweet. But today, we decided to dine in and what an experience that was! They have a karaoke machine with thousands (I think. There were a lot) of songs, which made lunch super fun. Definitely a winning combination. And the staff are nice and friendly. Love this place.

Their food taste good, ordered 3 dishes for catering which taste great and made on time.
On the same day picking up the food I didnt feel welcomed. The ladies at the front didnt greet me, I had to apporach them myself because one of them was glued to their phone. I had to ask for a certain dessert 3 times for the lady to pay attention, which ended being expired with mold on it.

I feel the place is a bit pricey too.

Honestly disappointed.

name: Kasalo Resto Grill Granville
address: 44 Railway Parade, Granville NSW 2194, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I just bought a pork curry from this place, and what I witnessed right after paying was shocking. Flies were swarming over the raw pork meat and even on the covered meals. After checking other reviews, I realized this isn’t an isolated incident—many customers have reported the same issue, and it’s absolutely true. This is unacceptable and a serious health hazard. This establishment should be reported to the health authorities immediately.

I ordered in Kasalo through Uber Eats and the food we received (sisigsilog) has a big fly insect cooked with the fried egg! I called the restaurant directly and no apologies were given. The lady on the line just told me that she will relay it to their chef and owner.

This is a food safety issue and the food safety authority should investigate this. I also reported it to Uber Eats and is waiting for their reply and refund.

This is not the first time as my daughter bought takeaway food and the rice is spoiled - so beware and do not buy food from this restaurant anymore!

I am proud to take my friends here to experience real Filipino dishes. Love the atmosphere (Native Kubo style), very casual yet clean and presentable. Food tasty and not too pricey and if you really love the food and pinoy merchandised there is a Pinoy store next or attached to it for Take away food and little groceries...

Friday and Saturday from 600pm is normally full house due ACCOUSTIC Live performances aside from daily Karaoke. Being an intimate and small dining area BOOKING is a required.

ENJOY Pinoy Food and Entertainment.

We went to eat the shop - good value for money - rice combo and 2 dishes including a drink and all for 40 bucks - servings / portions were big too thank u - will visit again ! FYI as compared to other commercial filo restos this place gives you a feel of a canderina from PH

This is not the first time i ordered from Kasalo through Uber Eats. My previous orders the food were ok. But this time, the Pancit canton and Chicharon bulaklak was very salty. My 4 years old could't eat the Pancit and its his favourite.

name: Kipling's Garage Bar
address: 2 Eastern Rd, Turramurra NSW 2074, Australia
description: Lively, industrial-chic bar in a former garage, serving inventive tapas plates and craft beer.
reviews: So happy with our choice for an anniversary dinner. The atmosphere was cozy and welcoming from the start. The share plates at truly reasonable prices was such a rare and amazing experience. All the dishes were delicious. The wine range was extensive and we both had a perfect cocktail to start. The young waitress did her best but I was super impressed by the maître de and chef who supported her doing a good job. The staff were all attentive to the needs of all the tables. Parking, after commuting hours, was easy. We will be going back, quite soon I expect.

What a fabulous restaurant! Absolutely delicious food. The menu has lots of variety and the tapas style is a great way to experience and share the tempting delights not to mention the great wines on offer. Loved the atmosphere and the service was sensational. Have dined here a few times before and will return again and again.
This place is such a gem. The atmosphere and the wine list are fantastic, Service is always good and the food is amazing. Perfect spot for a date or to catch up with friends.

It’s a lovely experience at Kipling’s today! The meals are delicious and beautifully prepared. The staff is very friendly and welcoming; the venue is so nice and cosy. We will definitely be back again to try more dishes!

Love this new find - great food and vibe!
Staff are very friendly and efficient.
The only slight negative is a lack of vegetable dishes, though this was remedied by the chef cooking us an extra serve of chargrilled broccolini - excellent! 😊

name: Kokoe
address: 551 High St, Penrith NSW 2750, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: What can I say, this little cafe offers a range of Filipino meals and traditional cafe style cafes. The food has a home cooked feel, but could be a little more refined for the price, the pork dishes were nice, but the pasta was underwhelming. The coffee was nice

Great place for authentic Filipino dishes. Place was spacious and staff was friendly. Service is fast as well. Had the sisig. It was porky and crispy. Also had their Halo Halo. Is everything you want in a typical halo halo (missing the ube ice cream).

I had their congee twice, delivered and in restaurant.  Their congee is bursting with flavour and a good amount of ginger.

I've also had their crispy pork and garlic rice delivered in the past,  also very delicious and will order again.

Service in restaurant is lovely but for congee,  there was a bit of a long wait,  around 20 minutes.

I just had brunch here, and it was wonderful. My first impression was the setting. Everything is nicely presented and super clean. The staff are so kind and helpful, the food is tasty and so was the tropical drink. I'm so glad I have found a local Filipino cafe/restaurant. I will be coming back. Thanks Kokoe.
Filipino Cafe in Penrith serving delicious classics. We had late lunch here on a Sunday. Space is nice and welcoming.

name: Kowboy Bar & Grill
address: 80 Blacktown Rd, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We hit up Kowboy Grill the other night with our friends, and we had an awesome time.  Kowboy Grill Blacktown is now under new management, and wow, there’s been such a huge improvement since we last went. The food was served fresh and was delicious, along with the live band. The M7 Band were great performers, and they got the people up and dancing to Filipino hits and classic tunes from the 80s and 90s.

Kowboy Grill also offers a great selection of drinks, beers, and cocktails.  - We got some buckets of San Mig and Red Horse - the vibe really takes you back to the Philippines.  Even my Gin & Tonic was very Pinoy, they were serving up Ginebra San Miguel Gin  - Kaya mas may Amats! LOL even the Gin Pamelo!

Best of all, the staff were all super friendly and attentive. It was a very busy night, and you could just see them all going around and making sure that customers were all happy and ok. Great Job Team!

Anyhoo, we are already planning our next visit with the family soon. - I think my cousins & titos would really enjoy it here.

Tuesday 19th August, 2025
Between 6pm - 8pm

First time trying this and I'm surprised that this is the best filo restaurant I've ever been to.
I normally don't eat Filipino food or cuisine but this place is quite delicious.
In comparison to the filo restaurant in Lidcombe near the train station on a one way road, this place is heaps better.

Service by staffs were excellent
Food had aroma and flavour
Things like Sisig, kare kare, halo halo, crispy pata
Their steam rice had this aroma coming out of it (most steam rice are bland, but this one you could literally smell the aroma from the rice. It's not like other steam rice's you have out there somewhere else).
Quite a cosy atmosphere and comfortable dining, but I think in my opinion the tables were too high for the seating on the sofa/lounge seats.
(Table 19).
Seat were kind of too low, making the table seem like it's too high.

Only biggest downfall  was that the dishes were quite exy.
Had paid $127 total
Can't believe the halo halo cup....A CUP... Was $15!!!!.
I guess maybe it's because it's the price of the ingredients in the cup.
But it was good.

Came here for lunch, food was really nice expensive when it comes to the other Filipino food I had InThe past few days.

The fish was amazing! Dipping sauces were great.

I loved the fish belly soup sour but amazing!

My friend ordered some kind of pork intestine, fried they were actually really yummy.

The pig’s intestines and crispy pata at Kowboy Grill were very yummy! The intestines were well-cleaned, flavorful, and perfectly cooked, while the crispy pata was crunchy on the outside and tender and juicy on the inside. The live band was everything I ever wanted especially if the restaurant serves delicious food.
The food is delicious, the crew are very friendly, and its great for Friday night out with friends. The singers are excellent and really add to the fun atmosphere.

name: Kuya's Kape
address: 836 Pittwater Rd, Dee Why NSW 2099, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Kuya Kape has an amazing coffee and and amazing Filipino food. Their customer service is great with all of them being so kind to every customer that walks through the door.
Filipino or not, this place has great cofffee and awesome food. Customer service is the best and the price is just right.
This place is genuinely amazing with really nice staff

name: La Crema Cusina Penrith
address: 12 The Crescent, Penrith NSW 2750, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews: Great place, customer service, food, delish & coffee is one of our faves! Best go to cafes in the riff

Such a great find! I love the food here so delicious and healthy (lots of veggie options as well as can add meat protein for those that like it) as well as some treats if you want. My fav is the Buddha bowl. Easy parking. It’s my must go place when I’m in Penrith.

I have been looking for a good Filipino cafe (modernised with a twist) in Penrith area and finally came across this cafe. They have a wide variety of menu choices that are fusion and mouth watering. I used the app and even got 15% off. I tried their Eggplant Katsu Focaccia. I wanted to add the pork belly but the app was not allowing me to do so but the staff very kindly gave this as an upgrade for free. I also got ube matcha latte which has a good hint of ube. You can possibly add sugar to your liking as it is not sweet. Definitely, good food, service and experience and will be back with my family.

I had the pleasure of eating here today. I went there totally unaware that the place had changed owners.

Well, what can I say?, I had the most beautiful Arancini dish presented perfectly on the plate!

The staff are lovely, the atmosphere is warm and friendly and the food and the coffee are outstanding!

Will definitely be back!

It was nice to pop into this calming vibes cute little cafe to celebrate mothers day with Mum. Friendly staff, amazing service was quick with coffee and the meal was delicious pork belly bao bun was chefs kiss. I would definitely be back!

name: Lazza Marrickville
address: 246 Marrickville Rd, Marrickville NSW 2204, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Over the past couple of days, my boyfriend and I had been craving good Filipino food, in particular, adobo. Being in North Sydney, quality Filipino food can be hard to come by, so we decided to head out to Marrickville to try Lazza, a place we’ve had our eye on for a while after hearing great things from friends who sing there.

From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with smiles, great vibes, and the smell of authentic Filipino food. They mentioned that adobo isn’t normally on the menu, and unfortunately they had just run out by the time we arrived but that wasn’t an issue at all. They went out of their way to cook an incredible pork adobo just for us, and wow… it genuinely brought us straight back to the Philippines.

On top of that, we had the crispy lechon kawali and the sisig. Both dishes were packed with flavour and tasted incredibly authentic.
If you’re looking for good vibes, great company, amazing food, and live singing, we highly recommend a visit to Lazza Marrickville.

My partner and I never had a bad experience here. Consistently delicious food and fantastic service. They have the best crispy sisig in Sydney! I also loved their bangus, it was cooked to perfection. Can’t wait to be back.
24 dollars，pork sisig ，take away just one tiny small box like children pack.no forks ，mad about where is the egg. It does not match with dine-in photos👀

A lovely filipino restaurant in Marrickville. Been here a few times and I have found the food to be quite good. Had the pork sisig which was good as I thought they may put more fat than pork meat but glad it was nearly all pork meat. Pork BBQ and the Lechon kuwali were tasty. The Kare Kare was presented well but was quite bland as we had to ask for more shrimp paste to add flavour. The halo halo dessert was generous in size and can feed two. My favourite was their Turon (Fried banana) with the ube ice cream. Perfectly crispy without skimping on the banana. The staff are always welcoming and friendly.

Some improvements :-
We were disappointed one evening, on a Friday night with a 6pm sitting, and they ran out of turon for dessert. Very disappointed that they did not cater for a busy evening.
I also do hope their toilet facilities have been fixed as we were surprised that the toilet plumbing wasnt completely functioning.
Please do not advertise that there is live entertainment on Fridays when there has never been any entertainment, the 3 times we have eaten there on a Friday evening. The live entertainment was a reason we chose Friday instead of a different evening.

Overall, a lovely, quaint and charming filipino restaurant.

We got the sizzling chicken plate, (chicken sisig) the deep fried pork belly and a rice. Flavour for chicken was good, but got a bit dry. The pork belly was a little oily. Overall, flavour was ok but price was fairly steep so value was just alright. Might come back occasionally.

name: Leenez Telopea Take Away Shop
address: 10/7-16 Benaud Pl, Telopea NSW 2117, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Delicious authentic Filipino food with warm and friendly customer service!
Yum! Giniling so worth it and big portion. Unable to finish everything in one go😂
Have tried a few different options now. Would happily recommend  anything on the menu. Good prices and friendly staff

Pinoy burger is AMAZING!! I order it every week with an iced mocha. The rest of their food tastes so good, they have a wide range including homemade Filipino dishes. The family working there are so lovely and always have a smile on their face. Definitely recommend!!
This Takeaway is definitely worth visiting for breakfast lunch or dinner. The food is fresh and served with the biggest smiles from the warming staff. Your tastebuds wont be let down

name: Leoncia's Filipino Desserts & Yema
address: 6, Shopping Centre, Shop 454, Level 3/14 Castle St, Castle Hill NSW 2154, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Leoncia’s – Castle Towers

Hands down the best Filipino food in Castle Towers! The dishes are always fresh, flavourful, and taste just like home. The servings are generous, and the atmosphere is warm and welcoming.

Their desserts and drinks are seriously addictive — you’ll keep coming back for more! We absolutely love the staff here too; they’re always friendly, helpful, and make the whole experience even better.

Leoncia’s is consistent, delicious, and authentically Filipino. Highly recommended! 💯🔥

We ordered from Leoncia’s and honestly, everything was amazing. The Dinuguan was so rich and flavorful and the Pork BBQ was super tender, and packed with taste. For dessert, we tried the Ube Halo-Halo and the U-Bae Gelato Tub, and wow... both were absolutely delicious! Don’t forget the calamansi juice which was the perfect balance of sweet and sour, so so refreshing. You can really tell everything was made with care and the staff were absolutely lovely and accommodating. Highly recommended!

First, the caldereta meal is delicious. I mean really super delicious. The only Filipino resto that’s generous with their servings with friendly ambiance. The gelato is Messina-grade or even better. It’s like Magnolia’s Gold Label but only better. The cassava cake goes well with the gelato. Definitely not a one-hit wonder like you have visited in the west. No Filipino restaurant in Australia offers a $2 calamansi juice.

Surely am coming back next time for their Bihon!

It is not just a filipino dessert place, you can also try filipino hot dishes.

We tried their palabok and sisig, unfortunately both dishes were so good and delicious we forgot to take a photo of the dishes. I would recommend trying both dishes.

We also bought the calamansi juice. You wont be disappointed with the perfect balance of sweetness and sourness of the drink.

The owner and the staff are so accommodating and with a funny and bubbly personality.

It was a worthwhile trip going here.

Experience a great hospitality from the owner, Leoncia, here at Castle Towers.  Authentic and homely taste from every dish - caldereta, palabok and calamari juice.
We had sampler tastings of home made ice cream flavours, jams, and spreads which was nice.
Worth coming back for more dishes.

name: Let’s Chon
address: 7 Gleeson Ave, Sydenham NSW 2044, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I brought my non Filipino friends to try Filipino food, specifically to try Lechon and they loved it!

Highly recommend their Cebu lechon pork belly. Seasoned well, ,cooked even better with a great crunch and colour on the skin. My friends absolutely loved it! Especially with the vinegar and all purpose sauce.

Recommend their garlic rice and lechon Sisig. The rice was made very well and really hit all the garlic flavour notes and textures.
