The Lehon Sisig was great as it didn't have too much mayo and really let the lechon flavour shine. There is a surcharge on the egg but it's a must have. The chillis also gave it a nice kick.

Don't recommend the chicken Inasal and palabok. While both were tasty, the chicken Inasal was dry, whereas the palabok didn't have the sauciness of most other palaboks and was definitely lacking in meat and toppings.

Service was warm and prompt with the food coming out at an acceptable wait time. Price wise, it's a little on the expensive side for Filipino restaurants, however, we will definitely be back for more lechon and tol try some of their other offerings!

For people who love Filipino Roast Pig called lechon, Let's Chon is the place to go.  They serve Cebu Lechon known as the savory roast pig of Visayas. Also serving other Filipino bbq and dishes. A quaint place near Sydenham Train Station.

Amazing flavours all the way! There was a third party app promo so decided to give them a try. The crackling skin on the lechon was delightful, however we were given a full plate of lean meat which was a bit of a let down. Would have appreciated a mix between lean and some fatty parts. Garlic fried rice and pinakbet vege dish complimented the lechon nicely and we had an enjoyable meal overall.

If you enjoy decent Philippines food then it's a good option.
I love the smell of the chargrilled rotisserie and the aroma of spice's.
The wait staff are pleasant, as are most Philipino folks.
Definitely worth a try
The milk fish I had was excellent but the portions were quite small in general. I also appreciated the variety of condiments in the sauce station and the staff's recommendation on sauce pairings.

name: Leumeah Baptist Church
address: 78 Amundsen St, Leumeah NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: Great place to be with Filipino people doing their good work

name: Li'l Darlin Crows Nest
address: 23 Willoughby Rd, Crows Nest NSW 2065, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews: Me and my sister had the best time here for my birthday. I am coeliac and they accommodated for me in the best way possible. Kira and Vit were legendary and made my day feel very special. Would definitely recommend and go again.
Amazing bottomless brunch. Calamari was the best I’ve had in Australia. Service was beyond amazing. Great vibes and music. Value can’t be beaten. Highly recommend.

Was really pleased with my experience at the Surry Hills store, so decided to visit this one. The vibe is great! However, I was a bit disappointed that there was no mushroom sauce, it was so good at the Surry Hills store.

Also, my fish burger was a little too salty, and I found around four small bones in the fillet, which was surprising as I didn’t expect that in a fish burger. Hence the four stars.

Wow - way above expectations!!!
Andre was an amazing host, Hannah kept the cocktails flowing & Rie, the head chef, served the most scrumptious food. We really enjoyed our day & to top it off it was exceptional value for money. We will return soon!!

I walked in thinking this was just a normal bar-style dining spot, but I was pleasantly surprised. The food here is much better than expected, and it honestly impressed me. The crispy Peking duck pizza was the star—it tasted exactly like a legit Peking duck wrap but in pizza form. Such a clever and delicious idea. Another standout was the cheese pizza with honey—one of the few places in Sydney where I’ve found this, and it worked perfectly with the richness of the cheese. We also tried tapas on their Wednesday special for $12.50, which is such good value. The burrata with porcini mushrooms (highly recommended by the staff) was a great balance of freshness, oil, and a hint of spice. Everything paired nicely with the cocktails, which were just as enjoyable. Overall, a fantastic spot with different promotions throughout the week—definitely worth checking out.

name: Little Angel Cafe
address: 267 Victoria Rd, Rydalmere NSW 2116, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews:
The best oat matcha latte in town!

Seriously, so delicious, my previous experiences with matcha cafes had me feeling like I've drank grass, but this one was so gooood. It wasnt overly sweet, had that actual matcha taste and was economical.

The onigiri wasnt as good, it crumbled the moment I took it out of the packaging, and the rice tasted old (hard), but the filling was yummy.

The cafe itself is small, with limited seats, and it shares a space with a guitar store (kinda cool).

Would def come back here again!
The cafe is small and cute. I tried the iced matcha ube drink and their onigiris (tuna mayo and yuzu salmon). Both were tasty and with generous fillings. The staff were also friendly and welcoming 😊.

I visit here every chance I get… which isn’t often, but each time I do, it doesn’t disappoint. Ya’ll already know the food and drink slaps, (my personal favs: ham cheese croissy, salmon onigiri, banana matcha) but lemme talk about something else.. I love all the effort that’s been put in to make it an inclusive space, from experimental, diverse ingredients (e.g honey dill / adobo) to the way customers are treated (e.g Polaroid fridge, always keen to laugh) Everything feels so intentional, even down to the origin of the cafe’s name – the meaning behind it is so thoughtful 💙 p.s never get rid of the onigiri pleaaaaase 🙏

If I could give more stars, I would!

The coffee was bold, smooth and really well executed. The ube matcha left me speechless and the Chicken Katsu Sando was filling and amazing balance with the flavours.

Not only am I super pleased with the food and drink, the most important thing happened - I was served with a smile and very efficiently!

It makes me sad that I don’t live in the area and it’s not my local but I know where my pit stop will be every time I’m out this way!

This little cafe tucked away in the north of Parra is for sure a hidden gem! The Ube latte and Banana Matcha was the best I’ve had so far in Sydney! Definitely a must try and also their katsu Sando was a good snack to go along with the drinks!

Can’t wait to come back to try their filo x Japanese fusion food in the future!

name: Lola Cocina Spanish Restaurant Narrabeen
address: 1304 Pittwater Rd, Narrabeen NSW 2101, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Every dish we tried was absolutely delicious.
I was already so full that I couldn’t try more tapas and other menu items, which honestly makes me a little sad — I’ll definitely have to come back for them.

The sangria was the best I’ve ever had. Truly outstanding.
We’re visiting on holiday, but this is one of those places I’d happily come back to just to eat here again.

Not only was the food incredible, but the service was exceptional and perfectly matched the quality of the food.
The restaurant interior and music selection created such a great atmosphere — it’s been a long time since I’ve visited a place this memorable and delightful.
It genuinely made our holiday even happier.

We loved it so much that we even got takeaway to enjoy later!

If you’re visiting the area or live nearby, definitely come and try this place!

I visited during lunch time and had a wonderful experience. The restaurant is clean and comfortable. Our waiter, Mahez, was very friendly and helpful. I also really enjoyed the drink he made for us (as shown in the photo).

We ordered the seafood Paella and the lunch special Tapas plate — both were excellent! (See the promotion in the picture.)

We will definitely come back again! The place, the service from Mahez, the food, and the price are all 👍.

Fantastic Spanish restaurant and incredibly accommodating for gluten free.

Very tasty food (we used to live in Barcelona). Slightly over spiced on the paella but nothing some lemon juice didn’t solve. Perfect pan con tomate, croquetas and pastas bravas. Gambas cooked perfectly but needed a bit more seasoning. Still took home our leftovers. Yum.

Staff were wonderful. Very busy for only
3 staff members so a little wait on service but we didn’t book ahead so completely understandable.

Thanks and we will be back!

The service was lovely as Mahez welcomed us immediately and the chatted with us while we waited for our meal. He was wonderful with his recommendations, and we ended up ordering the Paella de Marisco, Vegetarian Tapas (Lunch Special), and Sangria. Everything was incredibly tasty, so I would certainly recommend checking this place out whenever you’re in the area. I’ve been to a couple of their venues, and I have to say, this one has the best and friendliest service.
Awesome lunch today! Food and service was excellent! The pork belly, wings and paella were the standouts! Booked under the firsttable 50% off food promotion so it was real value for money! 8/10

name: Lola Remy's
address: Shop 1/56 Lakeside Parade, Jordan Springs NSW 2747, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: This place is a local favourite of ours, we visit almost every other week for takeaway- they also have some dine in seats available as well as a small selection of Asian grocery items. We love the food here, we regularly order the lechon, adobo, bbq skewers and caldereta and everything is always great. The staff here are beyond kind and always treat me and my family with kindness. Everything is truly good and I will always regularly visit.

Great Filipino food, definitely some of the best around! The flavors are amazing, though I do wish the trading hours were longer on weekends. Also, I’d love it if the pork adobo was available on the menu all the time- it’s that good!

Lovely Pilipino stop for food and shopping. The place is clean and organised. We had lunch food is so yummy, specially the sisig. Price is good 2 choice and rice $15. We did some Pilipino goodies shopping too. The owner is there. He is very nice and welcoming. Resonate of shops back home. And big THANK YOU for displaying our flyer and helping out small business. Cannot wait to be back and tell friends too!
I was pretty nervous cos I don't eat alot of fillipino  food. I had pork merchado very yummy massive portion the spring rolls fantastic . Defs going back . Thanks for making it easy
Came here to eat and lovely owners. Cozy vibes and authentic filipino food. Would recommend 10 outta 10, will be back again!

name: Lola Rizza's Filipino Eatery
address: 15 Cleeve Cl, Mount Druitt NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Friendly and warm service. We found Lola Rizza's food to be well flavoured; unlike many other Filipino places, the dishes weren't overly salty. Prices are reasonable for the generous portion sizes. We ordered from the hot bar and the food was expectedly not freshly prepared nor served hot. They were genuine in their desire to help us choose and generously explained the dishes and offered to make us try them.
We will need to return to fully experience them all!

Lola Rizza's Filipino Eatery is a amazing quality food restaurant and a great place to enjoy a delightful meal. The Service is outstanding and the food is a unique cuisine. Either at home or at the restaurant it still leaves you with a full stomach.
This restaurant has excellent expectations when you walk in, this is also a family owned business.
(My friend is the grandson of the owner "Noah")

Thank you

We ordered dinuguan, barbecue, and chicken inasal via uber eats. They were all lacking flavour. The dinuguan had good texture but lacked sourness. The barbecue lacked sweetness and was a bit thick. The chicken inasal lacked sweetness too. The only one that tasted nice was the free soup they gave.

Mum, auntie and I had lunch here, having the two choice and rice. The pork sigsig diced pork with calamansi, chilli and red onions. The kare kare had no peanut taste. The bbq pork were $5 each, but they were big portions. They served us complementary chicken soup.

The reason why I put it so low rating is because my mum found a big piece of plastic inside her Springroll but thankfully they made her meal free of charge. 🤷🏽‍♂️ $58+ in total

My 2nd time eating here. Inasal is to die for!!!! Love the marinate and the sauce- chili toyo mansi. Love the lechon kawali as well served with mang thomas. Everything tasted nice and would definitely recommend to my friends 🩷

name: Lola's Table Filipino Mexican Bistro Wentworth Point
address: Shop 1/18 Footbridge Bvd, Wentworth Point NSW 2127, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
It’s been awhile since I had a dish that surprise me. But the elote / corn was 🤯🤯

Corn: 6/5
Pig trotters: 5/5
Caramelised pork: 5/5
Halo: 5/5

The corn was really mind blowing. I’ve not tasted corn like this before and was just wowwed. It was so juicy and tasty and charred! Perfectly done.

The size of the pig trotter dish just got us laughing when it came, cause it was so big 😂 we brought home half of it…

The caramelised pork dish has a tangy flavour to it which was really nice too.

My husband rate the place 4/5 just cos we’re not very used to the flavours, and tbh, I don’t usually consume so much meat but I can’t think of how they can make the dishes any better hence, I give them 5/5 for all the dishes. One improvement is if they can have some vegetables for the caramelised pork dish cause the whole meal was super meaty without much veg.

The small halo portion was also pretty big!

We paid $78 in total, and brought home a lot of it. Seems like we’re not the only one cos most of the tables around us were also getting boxes for takeaway 😂

Only thing is, staff serving can ideally know their fridge desserts better as they didn’t really know how to explain the ingredients in there or what they were (we are not Filipinos so some food introduction would be helpful).

Also, a large group came in and sat behind us so the restaurant got really noisy after and not conducive for conversations. Perhaps some sound proofing would be helpful.

Nonetheless, I was very impressed by the meal - esp for a cuisine we don’t usually frequent. Thank you for the yummy food and mind blowing corn!! 😍

I'm always keen to try Filipino food as I have not always had a great experience. Lola's Table was also unique in that it is marketed as a Filipino / mexican fusion restaurant.

We did order from the filipino side of the menu - we'll be back to try the mexican fusion.

We had the Crispy pata - served with soft tacos :-) and guacamole as well as the usual vinegar soy sauce dip.
Spring rolls and the Daing na Bangus (boneless milkfish)

They had excellent service with staff friendly and approachable. In terms of the food the crispy pata was delish - just what I expected and it was crispy!  The spring rolls reminded me of my mums spring rolls. The boneless milkfish could have been done better. it was very well seasoned on one side but not the other and a tad overcooked.  The seasoning would have been great if it was evenly on the fish but it made half the fish super sour and salty and the other half not.

They had a lot more things on the menu we couldn't try as there was only 2 of us. Great for groups for family style sharing. They also did catering which was good to know for future events at home.

Amazing food!
Their crispy pata was definitely very crispy on the outside and the meat was tender in the inside
The fish tacos had a good kick to them
The chicken nachos were so yum as well!

Atmosphere was okay, I believe they open about 5pm. We got there at 6, outdoor setting were not yet set up. No one outside to greet to we just took our seats.
However, staff were lovely. Very kind.
My partner loves this place...gets the pork knuckle EVERY TIME...
Also had another traditional Philippino dish and was delish.
We even tried a couple desserts. Very different but sooo good.
BYO also.

This was our first time to eat in Lolas Table.
Reservation was done ahead of time. They were very accommodating when we adjusted the time due to unexpected activities to attend.

The food was great, we were so full!
We choose boodle fight B and ordered nilagang baka for our soup since there are 3 kids.
The crispy pata was so crunchy and yummy.
The kaldereta was good too.
My Mom only complained on the sisig because it was kinda spicy 🥵 chilly. Hahaha.

They assist us well and attended on our needs.
I encourage everyone to visit their restaurant.

name: Lugaw Queen Restaurant
address: 207 Liverpool Rd, Ashfield NSW 2131, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Of all the Filipino food I’ve been able to find outside of western Sydney this place is the most accurate to the flavours I grew up with. Pales in comparison to what you’d get further out west but the food actually tasted like home without trying to pretentiously re-invent Filipino food to be on trend.
Had the loaded beef pares which came with lechon kawali, chicharon bulaklak and a side of soup (the nice server asked if I wanted the soup later so I said yes but they should just go ahead and give the soup regardless because it’s my favourite part of the meal). The flavours of the beef pares were more subtle than other ones I’ve had but the soup was deeply beefy and flavourful so balanced the meal.

Place was decorated exactly how my mum would do a place up if she had free rein over interior design. The smell of scented candles they had on the tables was a little dizzying but the ambience was nice. Maybe non scented candles next time?

Other reviews complain of service and whilst it wasn’t rude, I did have to call out to pay the bill but that’s probably another bonus for the place’s authenticity (kids running around the back included)

So-so experience. Ordered the chicken inasal and it tasted more like java chicken timpla rather than legit inasal. Disappointed though that the initial meal and drink I wanted wasn’t available. Service and friendliness can be improved as well.

wonderful ambience and great authentic filipino food, our big fambam enjoyed all theie food; unlike soup, kare kare and hipon siniganggang was such a big hit! Husband and i also ordered their Lomi overload and mami overload perfect for them winter chilli nights! Only request please add sweet and sour spicey sauce for fish balls and kikiam, soy/ vinegar for crispy pata & more Ice tea please!
Can’t wait to go back and tey your halo halo
Lovely place. Make sure you come in early because they run out of food pretty quickly especially Lugaw! Lomi is superb and given in generous servings too.
Food tastes great. Home Filipino food. Not bad at all.

name: Lutong Pinoy atbp.
address: 28 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Its not Lutong Pinoy now Its Cucina de Manila, and they sell good ole pinoy comfort food at reasonable prices. Good service

name: MRT Pilipino Foods and Products
address: Shop C10, 5/9 Freedom plaza, Cabramatta NSW 2166, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
This is a first for Cabramatta, a Filipino store walked past it and noticed it has all the products that a Filipino person would need.

They also have a hot bar outside servicing hot delicacies as well from their motherland.

Can't complain the more variety of choices the better.
Heaps of yummy dishes available at affordable price. Most of Philippine products are there. Very friendly staff and good customer service. 100 % satisfied!
I really like shopping here as last time I was looking for Reno liver paste and I can't find it anywhere except here in MRT. They have cooked foods that you can eat as well.
Glad to have U MRT pinoy shop at cabramatta...Only one n first Pinoy shop @ cabramatta. Affordable prices n most of all humble and friendly owners ❤️❤️ MUST try to shop in mga kababayan!!!

wonderful/beautiful owners, unbeatable service , great/yummy/fulfilling Filipino dish!
a must visit store for ALL!
especially homesick(ed) pinoys

name: Mabuhey App - Online Filipino Groceries Food and Products
address: 710 George St, Haymarket NSW 2000, Australia
hours: {"mon": "Open 24 hours", "tue": "Open 24 hours", "wed": "Open 24 hours", "thu": "Open 24 hours", "fri": "Open 24 hours", "sat": "Open 24 hours", "sun": "Open 24 hours"}
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.

name: Macheon Korean Restaurant
address: 4/38 President Ave, Caringbah NSW 2229, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Been wanting to visit and try since I found out about this restaurant. Service was attentive and intimate as the restaurant was quite small and more formal than ones I visited. Food menu not a long list but sufficient for those who craves for Korean food and did well. We’re happy with our order and also took the dessert home. Worth a visit!

Small but mighty! Unassuming set back from the road, this restaurant delivers! The tteboki had great flavour, the japche noodles were chewy, and the spicy soup was rich and good level spicy. The Korean take on tiramisu was interesting but worth a try. I recommend booking. I will be returning!

As a kdrama loving Filipino, this place hit all the right spots! The food tasted like it came straight out of a kdrama sceen so good lol. I almost looked around for a dramatic plot twist. Cozy vibes, friendly staff, and you can really tell there’s heart in every dish. I’m definitely coming back and bringing friends with me next time.

Me and my family came here for the first time as we are from Canberra. The food was the best Korean food we've ever had. The lady who served us and the cook himself were very kind to us, and the atmosphere was very calming. We will definitely be coming back to this restaurant on our next visit to Cronulla👍

I appreciate good food. I appreciate Macheon. I feel like I got teleported back to the streets of Tanjong Pagar in Singapore!

The rice melts off the spoon, chicken batter succulent and crispy — full of flavour, and temperature perfect the moment it was served. Ingredients were high quality and felt soul-filling. Even the sides tasted super fresh.

Everything was amazing from scratch to end, service impeccable, staff were very friendly and constantly checked in to see how we were doing.

New favorite in the Shire!

name: Maison Mornings
address: 2/72 Tennyson Rd, Mortlake NSW 2137, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
A bright, warm and welcoming cafe that serve amazing food ! Was definitely surprised and excited about the Filipino dishes on the menu and had to try it. The pork adobo was so incredibly tender and delicious that I could have more of it! It was cooked amazingly well and the vinegar to soy sauce ratio was perfect. The dish was cooked with hearty intention ! And of course, the honey glazed pork was to die for. Portion and presentation was nailed down, and kudos to the chef because the meat was incredibly tender and delicious.  Coffee was great too, and the customer service was friendly and welcoming ! :) The place is also dog-friendly, and has small outdoor seating.

Overall, a beautiful gem in Mortlake, and would definitely come back :) Maison Mornings was definitely a great start to my morning !

Very nice and relaxing vibe. Beautifully presented dishes with a generous volume. However, the syrup was fully poured in the pancake plate making the lower part too sweet and a bit soggy. The mushroom pasta was also too heavy on the cream and light on taste. The service was quick enough. However, the two ladies in the shop were spending more time in the back or talking together rather than being around.

Great little place. Had lots of good options for food. The coffee we got was excellent. The milk is hot and silky. The pasta we ordered had truffle but not so much truffle. You couldn't taste anything else and was absolutely delicious. The service there was excellent. Staff are friendly to us and our children who were perfectly behaved. The table that we sat at had a nook for the pram which is excellent. A nook!?!  The whole place felt inviting and comfortable - and the staff helped us by taking our order at the table, I'm sure it's meant to be that I go up to the counter, but they saw we were a bit disorganised and helped us out.
Would go again for sure.

Maison Mornings is truly a gem in the neighbourhood. My wife and I go there every morning for coffee, and we always feel so welcomed — Ivy, Jason and the team greet us (and even our dog Poppy!) by name, which is such a lovely personal touch.

The coffee is hands down the best in the area, and the breakfast menu never disappoints — I’m especially a fan of the eggs Benny, it’s a go-to favourite. The café itself is beautifully designed with a calm, inviting atmosphere and always the perfect background music to start the day right.

All the staff are friendly, thoughtful, and seem to genuinely enjoy getting to know their regulars. Maison Mornings has become a part of our daily routine, and we feel lucky to have it as our local.

Highly recommend!

Went here for a spontaneous brunch date and was super happy with the dishes we ordered. Reasonably priced, super tasty and the owners were lovely.

Definitely recommend visiting. Will be back!!!

name: Mama Lor Restaurant & Bakery
address: Shop 5/39-45 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Fresh Lumpia… the filling is just ORANGE and PURPLE SWEET POTATOES. I was expecting vegetables inside… where are the beans carrots sprout beans?
Crispy Palabok… it was dry hardly any sauce in it and have to pay for the extra sauce.
Tapsilog… garlic rice had a plastic taste
We used to enjoy food in this restaurant but now its food standard has gone down to ZERO. What happened ?
We will NEVER come back to this place again.

If you're craving for fried pork belly.. this is the place! It's crunchy and yet melts in your mouth. The adobo is tender and melting.
Their bakery range is a must try too! Ube cheese pandesal!!!

Came for lunch,  I ordered the chicken adobo it was nice, I also ordered the pork soup, this was quite strong and sound once I adapted it was amazing!

My friend ordered the fish and beef, the fish was yummy needs a nicer dip though.

Ordered Beef Pares.  It was disgusting.  Thin fatty bits of what they called beef- it could have been anything.  It did not resemble the photo at all and it was mainly sauce with a few bits of meat in it.  Unfortunately I didn't take a photo.  This was $25 plus another $4 for rice.  And my son feels sick now after the meal. Will never go there again.

I ordered food yesterday for work through Uber Eats and scheduled it for afternoon delivery. As a long-time customer of Mama Lor, I’ve always enjoyed their food and had high expectations.

Unfortunately, this recent experience was a huge letdown—and enough to lose me as a loyal customer.

I ordered the fried chicken because it looked appetizing in the Uber Eats menu photo. But when it arrived, I was shocked. The chicken looked overcooked and very dark. The skin was hard, the taste was bitter, and it had a greasy aftertaste that made it inedible. A friend of mine who is a chef said it likely tasted that way because the oil had been reused too many times. That was clear.

The pancit was also disappointing—extremely oily, to the point where it looked like it could spoil quickly. There was no balance in flavor or presentation. It felt rushed and careless.
To make things worse, I brought the food to share at work, and everyone commented on how unappetizing it was. It was embarrassing. And for the price I paid, it felt like a complete waste.

I spent $46 for the pancit with an additional $4 for the chicken add-on, and $48 for a 10-piece fried chicken. Nearly $100 in total—and not a single dish was worth it. Expensive, but with no substance or quality to justify the cost.

Just because I ordered via Uber Eats doesn’t mean the food should be treated with less care. The standard should be consistent with what’s served in-store, especially for a restaurant I’ve supported for years. This experience didn’t reflect that at all.

I won’t be ordering from Mama Lor again, and I’ll be warning others as well. Very disappointing.

name: Manila Food
address: 20 Main St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I went here recently at lunch hour as I was hungry looking for an affordable meal.

When I enquired as to what food choices I could have from the food in the food warmer, a woman stated that I could get 2 servings of Filipino food and rice for $16. When I clarified if I heard her correctly, she replied that 2 servings of food and rice in a typical takeaway container was $16. I was surprised.

The shop is not a restaurant and those prices I'd expect at a cafe or restaurant. I ordered pork buns instead but the pastry was slightly stale, the pork was okay.

Would I come here again? If prices were more reasonable, especially in an area such as Blacktown then maybe. I wouldn't order the pork buns ever again.

I wanted Sisig, I paid $18.50 for it and it is DISGUSTINGLY FILTHY. It is so hard to chew and I am so disappointed. I want my money back. THIS. IS. NOT. SISIG. Filo Lasa’s Sisig (inside Westpoint Blacktown) is the legitimate one. Do better, Manila Food. I’m embarrassed to call this Filipino food. I will never buy sisig from here ever again. If I can give it 0 stars, I would.

Great staff. Very accomodating, and very good service. Great filipino authentic street food. They also have Ria Money Transfer service.
Take-away and dine-in filipino food stall. 👍
Street parking for half-hour only.
1 min walk from train station.

My husband and I have tried many things from here and I an so impressed! I really enjoyed the spring roll thing with the sweet potato inside. I also got a pork soup and the flavours are amazing! We will keep coming back!

First time trying this Filo store  .
One who were serving us has attitude . Ask a question gave a plain answer . What a great service isn’t it. Seems like she’s not interested at all.  Pls don’t have a business if you have NO proper customer service ettiqute.
Just close your doors .  ON top of that Food has no taste and Overpriced.

name: Manila Grind Co.
address: 19A Evans Ave, Eastlakes NSW 2018, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews: Food are of great value (full chicken is literally a whole chicken), staff are very friendly.

We had trouble finding the shop, so we had to ring them in order to find them. They were very helpful in giving us direction. They're across Pattinson's bakery.

The food was delicious. The coffee was brewed to perfection, and the service was relatively quick and friendly. We definitely will be coming back!
Warm. Cosy. Friendly. Good filipino dish.
Very good coffee. Lovely,  friendly staff,  thank you.

name: Manila Mart West Ryde
address: 23 Ryedale Rd, West Ryde NSW 2114, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
This is the best Filipino shop ever.
Located close to the train station, access is so easy, you don’t need to drive to get there.

This store has so many of the popular food and snacks, you’ll feel like you’re in a regular Tindahan.

They also have their own kitchen inside the store where you can buy some signature dishes.
Including the famour Halo Halo.

The very friendly owner always makes you feel right at home.
Don’t miss out. Go to this store and take a “teleport trip” to the Philipines.

My partner needed a handful of ingredients for filo cooking and found everything she needed. Aunty at the counter even gave her some tips for preparing one of the foods. If youre filo or ethnic haha you must visit this store.

Really appreciated to be near a Filipino shop, as I’m used to them being in the far west of Sydney. They have quite a number of goodies in store, and it took everything in me not to buy every single craving.

There were a few options that I couldn’t find in there that were either unavailable everywhere or I’ve seen in other stores. Definitely a potential to expand or add more items, but it alone is enough to help with my homesickness.
my mom is friends with the owner, she is a is great owner, and always brightens up my day! Then, there’s the other worker, he is a really funny person once you get to know him!

Happy to know there’s a local Filipino Store that’s able to accommodate all needs. The hours they provide is good especially when you need to grab something last minute that you can’t get at a local supermarket. Prices are reasonable for them being the only Filipino store within the area of West Ryde.

name: Manila Sari-Sari Store
address: 2/427-441 Victoria Ave, Chatswood NSW 2067, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: What a great shop and customer service! My mate and I were passing by in the mall and came across this place. We were curious and asked the woman (apologies, I didn't get her name) for some of her favorite recommendations. Will definitely return to try some more new things. Thank you

Super expensive! Walked in expecting a casual browse, walked out feeling like I’d accidentally taken out a small loan. None of the items are tagged with prices, so you’re basically shopping blind until you reach the counter…and that’s where the bill shock hits hard.
By far the most overpriced shop with the rudest staff. Simply terrible customer service. Total disrespect and disregard.

This shop doesnt even deserve a star, not accommodating, rude staff , no price tag! 🙄🙄 PLEASE LEARN HOW TO SAY THANKYOU AND HI HELLO ???????  ITS EVEN PRICEY AND NOT EVEN WORTH TO VISIT UR SHOP!!!!!! ALWAYS USING PHONE WHILE THERE’s a customer needs to pay!!! LOL

This store is unbeliveable not professional, price is expensive, the item is not sometimes available. In addtition, they always open not ON-TIME, says 10.30 am on the website but the staff comes around 11 or whatever they like.

name: Manila Sunset Convenience Store
address: 3/43 Broadarrow Rd, Narwee NSW 2209, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: Manila sunset is a place where you can buy pilipino food,and you can send money via western union, to your family. And they are very friendly.
Below average store & service not very friendly. Not much variety.
Convenient place for loca recycle
mosy complete filo store in the area
Saw the place

name: Manuel's Kitchen
address: 126 Hampden Rd, Artarmon NSW 2064, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We found this Filipino café through a Google search, and the food was amazing! We ordered the chicken inasal, beef tapa, and pork BBQ skewers—everything was delicious.

Kuya Manuel was incredibly welcoming, which gave the café such a warm, homey vibe. We’ll definitely be coming back!

We stumbled upon this gem by accident while searching Google Maps for Filipino food near Chatswood.

Manuel is such a warm and hospitable host—he even asked us to try the 'puto' before our meal arrived. I recommend saving it for dessert (or taking it home), as the portion is huge!

We ordered the BBQ and Chicken Inasal—both were excellent, and the BBQ was a standout; my kids said it was one of the best they've ever tried.

We finished with 'halo-halo' that didn't skimp on the toppings at all. The portion is generous enough to easily share between two people.

All in all, highly recommended—we'll definitely be back!

Finally got the chance to visit Manuel’s Kitchen yesterday, and it was well worth the wait! (We would’ve come earlier during the holiday break, but they were closed from Dec 24–26.)

Manuel greeted us warmly and took great care of us throughout our visit, which made the experience even more special.

My family and I loved everything we ordered — the longsilog, bangsilog, pork BBQ, and inihaw na baboy were all spot on.

I was tempted to order the pata tim too, especially after hearing friends rave about it, but I didn’t have anyone to share it with. At least that gives me a good excuse to come back!

Loved the relaxed, chill vibe. Perfect for a late Sunday morning — or honestly, any day.

Food was very good and service super friendly. The star of the show for me was the chicken inasal, super tender and juicy and the adobo rice was delicious. Pork bbq skewers were good too. Will come back.

MANUEL’S KITCHEN — Artarmon, Sydney

Stepping into Manuel’s Kitchen feels like walking into the heart of Filipino hospitality — where food is more than a meal; it’s a story of love, comfort, and celebration.

Every dish served here reflects Chef Manuel’s passion, craftsmanship, and deep respect for Filipino flavours. It’s a culinary experience that speaks to both heart and heritage.

Pata Tim — A masterpiece. The pork was fall-off-the-bone tender, luxuriously glazed, and beautifully seasoned. The portion was generous — a true feast to share. Each bite melts effortlessly, reminding you why this classic remains a Filipino favourite.

Kare-Kare — A comforting beef stew immersed in rich, nutty peanut sauce. The flavours were balanced and soulful — perfectly paired with bagoong (shrimp paste) that brings out that savoury depth we all crave.

Chicken Inasal — Juicy, chargrilled to perfection, and marinated with that signature Visayan tang. Dip it in vinegar and you’ll understand what home tastes like — smoky, zesty, and deeply satisfyin

Pork BBQ — Simply perfect. Tender, caramelised, and bursting with flavour — every skewer evokes the joy of Filipino street food, elevated with finesse.

Halo-Halo — A delightful finale. Not overly sweet — just right. A refreshing medley of textures and colours that cools the soul and crowns the meal with happiness.

Every dish at Manuel’s Kitchen tells a story — of tradition, resilience, and pride. Chef Manuel has truly created a space where Filipino food takes its rightful place on Sydney’s culinary map.

For those who long for the taste of home — or anyone ready to discover the heart of Filipino cuisine — Manuel’s Kitchen in Artarmon is a must-visit.

name: Mary Immaculate Parish
address: 125 Barnier Dr, Quakers Hill NSW 2763, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews:
What are lovely Parish - remember I Catholic church is not a building it is a community it is people.  Very prayerful and welcoming; the Filipino community is very strong here and the priest is Filipino as well (I'm sorry I don't remember his name) but the spirituality of the place comes from the top and it is very strong here.
I felt very welcome there are plenty of seats and the church is very beautiful and air conditioned. Great sound system, heaps of parking, plenty of toilets. As Jesus said: "come and see".

Friendly Catholic community and location is very convenient. Close to residential area with lots of parking in the compound. Staff and volunteers are very welcoming and warm to people. Keep up the good work and spread love and kindness.

New to the parish and everyone has been so nice. The people in the parish office was very courteous and helped me in joining the parish and getting the parish priest to come over to bless our house.

Would definitely look forward to helping out here more often.

God bless.
I have been attending mass here for over 20 years. I feel welcome and at peace here. The priest is always supportive and available to us. I’m very blessed to be part of this parish.

The church service we attended was not the best. We couldn't fully understand the priest as his English was not good. And at the beginning of the service, the sound system speakers that were set up began squealing throughout the hall, causing most of the congregation headaches (literally).

name: Masagana Mini Mart & Filipino Groceries
address: 3/4-6 Nardoo St, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: We went to the store around 10:45 or 11 am last Saturday MARCH 28TH 2025 to get some pinoy kakanin and stuff. I was so excited the fresh lumpia we bought I've been craving since. Tapos kinain ko afternoon at 4 something the same day. Yung sauce nya medyo maasim na! pag check ko ng exp date. Nak ka ng pating!!!! Exp date: MARCH 6TH 2025!👿my goodness! We did not go back to the shop as it was like quarter to 5pm and shop is closing. And yeah we'll never go back in that shop again😤 we just did proper complain about it. I mean look at  the picture!! Not appetizing at all 😤 I AM SO DISAPPOINTED.
My cousin ordered an ube cake from here, it was so good! Friendly staff too! Salamat!
Very kind and patient staff that can manage and advise customers on items and their freshness. I went here with my wife and it felt so refreshing to talk to someone in our language. Thank you

I don’t recommend this shop, especially to our fellow Filipinos who have just arrived here in Sydney. The new management is completely rude. My brother bought something here, and one of the staff members (an older lady) told another staff member, “Wag mong Englishin baka hindi maintindihan,” referring to my brother, who has been studying at Ingleburn High School for two years.

The next day, my mother went to buy something from the shop, and the same staff member asked her, “Anak mo ba yun? Yung maraming pimples? Trying hard siya mag-English, pansin ko.” When my mother told me about this, I was completely shocked. What’s wrong with trying hard to speak English? We’re in Australia, and it’s important for us to improve our English since it’s the main language used to communicate with people of different nationalities.

I’m really disappointed with your staff’s attitude. You should know how to treat and communicate with your customers better!

So the owner turned off the comment section because they couldn’t come up with a better reason to respond.

Don’t try to fool me—I know your shop. When Mang Jigs was managing it, there were no issues like this because he knew how to treat customers well.
And what do you mean you don’t have an older lady staff member? She herself claimed to own the relyeno dish you’re selling.

love this store, lovely family business and very very helpful with my Ria transactions.  They have beautiful homestyle cooked food for sale on Saturday mornings and the place smells amazing...lot's of people coming in and out to buy the food and have a chat; a truly authentic experience! 5+ STARS!

name: Masagana Oriental Variety Store
address: 6 Charlotte St, Ashfield NSW 2131, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Always a pleasure dropping by for my regular top-up of Filo products. Prices are decent, and the selection of fresh foods, bread, and pastries is more extensive than other Filo stores. Love the ulam section and the fresh bread tucked away at the back of the store - definitely worth checking out.👌

I absolutely love this place! Especially with their new food menu. I had the bangus and panedesal which immediately transported me back to when I lived in the Philippines. 🇵🇭 I also got to meet the owners who are lovely, humble and very generous. I look forward to my next visit and I highly recommend to all locals not just Filipinos. Thanks team Masagana 🫶
Lovely staff, clean and organized shelves. Will always come back here for our pinoy groceries!

Had a fantastic experience at Masagana Filipino Store in Ashfield! The store is clean, well-organized, and all the products were fully stocked — I found everything I needed and more. A special shoutout to Josh at the checkout, who delivered the best service — friendly, efficient, and genuinely helpful. It’s a homie feeling  shopping here. Highly recommend to anyone looking for quality Filipino products and excellent customer service!
Beautiful shops everything's you need is all here clean and tidy shops easily accessible with parking available around the corner.  Highly recommended to all Asian lots of everything's

name: Masagana Oriental Variety Store - Campsie
address: shop 6/20-22 Anglo Rd, Campsie NSW 2194, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: The service is always so friendly. Definitely my favourite Filipino shop to go to because they have everything I need. The food is always amazing especially their empanada. I tried their mango float yesterday and it was top tier 🤌🏼

The lady at the counter is quite rude and snob. There's not much to choose from at this shop and over priced. Very untidy store and some of the cakes are moldy and still selling them. Do not buy from this shop. It is not worth my trip to go to this store. Worst of all Filipino Store. You should update or check what ever you are selling and replace that lady in the counter- she's got an attitude problem that drives customers away!!!

Lady was too slow and can’t multitask .
Plus not friendly at all .
Made customers wait too long to serve people who came after them .
No wonder other reviews here got the same vibes from her .
I love going to this place! Finally get to munch on my favorite snacks from home salamat po talaga!😃
Worst customer service! The lady at the counter was unwelcoming and has an attitude. Greeted with a smile but no response at all! First time buying and not coming back! 😏

name: Masagana Oriental Variety Store - Rockdale
address: 10 Walz St, Rockdale NSW 2216, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We are a regular customers, unfortunately we were sold expired halo-halo and chips on separate occasions. Even accused me of buying from another store and called me "malas" / unlucky.

The "kuya" is very respectful. However we did not have a good experience with the lady who is talking on the loud speaker while with a customer.

Please double check your items before leaving the store. Thanks
Good variety, fair prices, well organised and fantastic staff who are accomodating and helpful. Thank you team Masagana.
The shop is new and the owner was very helpful, lots of Philippine flavors and food.
Great little variety store, good selection of snacks, friendly staff
Great variety of food, Philippine food, fresh and great help by staff

name: Melchrisel Bakery
address: 23 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: My wife ordered a cake and discovered the establishment was under new ownership. The new owner was Jeremiah, who was very accommodating and very courteous. He had carried his people skills from his previous profession in the hotel industry to this business venture that he has engaged in. The cake we normally order is a traditional mocha cake that reminds us of the good old days of Goldilocks Bakery in the Philippines. We’ve been a patron of Melchrishel’s Bakery from the time of its very first owners and the flavour of our favorite cake has not changed. Keep up the great standard Jeremiah. Your brand is truly an icon to the Filipino community here at OZ.

Breakfast and meryenda sorted! We’ve visited Melchrishel Bakery three times in the past few weeks because my wife and I genuinely enjoy their baked goods.

Our favourites so far are the pandesal, Spanish bread, ensaymada with cheese, and mamon. It's always fresh, soft, and full of flavour. We’re looking forward to trying more of their selection next time.

I also learned that the bakery is now under new management, and you can really see the improvements each time we visit. It’s great to see the effort being put into making the place better.

If you may have felt disappointed during a visit a month or so ago, I’d suggest giving them another try. Based on our recent experiences, there’s been noticeable improvement and we’ve been very happy each time.

Big thanks to Jeremiah and the team for bringing the authentic taste of a Filipino bakery to Sydney!

If you’re craving something that tastes like home, this Filipino bakery in Sydney is a must-visit 🇵🇭✨
The breads and pastries instantly gave me a wave of nostalgia — every bite brought me straight back to childhood merienda afternoons. Soft, freshly baked, and full of that authentic flavor you can’t fake.
The new owners are incredibly warm and welcoming, and you can really feel the care they put into everything they make. They’re also working on expanding their menu with more traditional and creative recipes, which makes me even more excited to keep coming back.
Overall, such a gem. If you love Filipino baked goods (or want to try them for the first time), this spot is 100% worth checking out. 💛🍞🥐

I’ve been a customer for years. The quality of their bread, cakes and snacks is always the same. Always fresh and at a reasonable price. Another best thing is that you are greeted with a smile. I would buy my pandesal, hopia, and ensaymada in the afternoon after work, and they'll greet me with a pleasant smile. Thank you for making yummy bread and my favourite leche flan cake. Always a treat going to your shop. More power to the new owners.

I super love their yema cake. Since the day I tasted it, I make sure to include this yema cake on every occassion. I even recommended it to my friends and they love it as well.
Very friendly staff also😊Thank you for always bringing smile to your customers..😃

name: MerKado Filipino / Asian Grocery
address: Shop 1 274/276 Queen St, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We had such a great experience at MerKado Filipino / Asian Grocery! We went online looking for a capiz parol, and Karl was incredibly friendly and helpful from start (through Messenger) to finish (in-store pickup). The transaction was smooth, and he even made sure the parol was carefully packed for us to take home safely.

The store itself is neat, well-stocked, and has a wide range of Filipino products that are hard to find elsewhere; from pantry staples to festive items that remind you of home. You can tell they genuinely care about their customers because of the warm service and attention to detail.

Highly recommend MerKado if you’re after authentic Filipino goods and a pleasant shopping experience. We’ll definitely be back! 🇵🇭✨
Love our new Parol! Amazing quality at a very reasonable price. My son was in awe. Thank you for helping us keep the Filipino Christmas tradition alive here in Australia. Mabuhay!
My wish of getting a parol has finally come true! Thank you Karl for supplying a pinoy Christmas classic and the great customer service you never fail to provide.

Honestly speaking, their service for NBI renewal is highly recommended. Sulit ang bayad and very accommodating sila online.

They made my NBI renewal easy as 1-2-3! Thank u so much Merkado Filipino!♥️♥️♥️♥️

Fast and reliable service. I was having issue getting my NBI clearance on gov’t website but MerKado Fil/Asian helped me to get my NBI clearance. They are so courteous and helpful. All I can say is I have a great experience and I’ll definitely use their service again.

name: Meryenda Cafe
address: 5/18-32 Oxford St, Darlinghurst NSW 2010, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Such a great gem in the area! There’s a really nice balance between savoury, sweet, and coffee, which makes it easy to keep coming back. There are classics like Adobo alongside breakfast favourites, all done so well!

The sweets are a highlight, especially the ube cake and cupcakes… honestly some of the best ube treats around 👀 Would be awesome to see even more ube goodies on the menu one day.

The atmosphere is relaxed, casual, and welcoming, and it’s genuinely affordable compared to a lot of places nearby. I don’t really agree with reviews comparing prices to Asia… it’s Sydney, and it’s not a fair comparison.

It’s a popular spot and can be busy, but the owner and staff are always so friendly and warm, which really makes the place feel special. A great neighbourhood café and definitely worth supporting! 🙌

The foods was delicious and well-prepared
The staff were very approachable and friendly.
They always ask if you need anything and make sure you are comfortable. Excellent service.

Initially a cafe and now branching out into resto. Still plenty of improvement. Ate here twice, Saturday brekkie and Sunday, had the topsilogs. Both times the food has made differently. Waiting times on Sunday was a bit  of a stretch due to the fact that there was only 2 staff on a peak time.

Still, we love having a pinoy food available in Surry Hills.
Food : Ok. Still needs improvement
Service : staff are polite but short during peak hours.
Atmosphere : you get a little feel of pinoy karinderya vibe. Location is prime.

Goodluck Allen!

Just arrived to Sydney and found this amazing brand new coffee shop. Everything is fresh and has that special home made taste.
Super kind staff and great coffee!
Thank you and all the best guys!!!
Great little new cafe on Oxford St. good coffee, cakes and food. Friendly staff, looks like being a new local.

name: Miguelitos Diner Campbeltown
address: 200 Gilchrist Dr, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
When you're missing Jollibee and you want to fill that craving.
The prices for a 1 piece chicken meals are a bit high, but that makes the family meal worth it. The size of the pieces are roughly 1.5x the size of KFC. They're very moist and juicy. And the skin is exceptionally crispy. The chicken is seasoned well.
The gravy is very buttery and well seasoned.
You can miss this spot as it in the corner and the nameplate isn't facing a thoroughfare.
They also had taho and other Filipino style desserts.
Food is good but didn’t like the gravy. Could make the shop easy to find tho. Paid $107 for 3 meals and 2 halo halo , and this is in the food court. Bit expensive I reckon

Total rip off! The chicken and chips weren’t even nice and it’s so overpriced. I paid $20 for 2 x chicken drumsticks, a small serving of chips, and a can of drink 😧There are so many other nicer places close by so I would avoid this place.

If you’re missing the famous Chickenjoy and gravy from Jollibee, this place is definitely worth visiting. As a Filipino, sometimes we really crave those familiar flavors from home, and it’s nice to find something that can satisfy that craving.

The chicken was very decent, crispy, and juicy, and the gravy really reminded me of the classic taste that we love. It may not be exactly the same as Jollibee back in the Philippines, but it definitely hits the spot when you’re missing it. The meal was very satisfying and comforting.

It’s always a great feeling when you find a place that can bring back a little taste of home. Perfect for Filipinos who are craving Chickenjoy vibes, and even for anyone who just wants to enjoy some good fried chicken and gravy.

Overall, a very enjoyable experience and I would happily come back again whenever I’m craving those familiar Filipino fast-food flavors. Highly recommended! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
The chicken is nice and similar to Jollibee’s, but overall it’s very expensive, and a bit overpriced.

name: Mix Mix Co. Express
address: Macquarie St, Liverpool NSW 2170, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: My husband and I have our own favorites. I love the keso fruit salad — it’s creamy, refreshing, and not too sweet. My husband loves the Avo Smash, which is always fresh, flavorful, and satisfying. We also both love the Taho - warm, silky, and just the right amount of sweetness. Our kids love the soft serve in a cone ☺️☺️

The most unique and delicious selection of Filipino sweet treats. Always comes with the most amazing service every time we visit. Our personal favorite is the taho. Takes me back to my childhood every time. Creamy and silky tofu, soft pearls with just the right bite and the perfect amount of brown sugar syrup that ties everything together beautifully.

Been coming here on and off for the past few months on a Friday afternoon towards closing time after work. Their drinks/desserts are eye catching and really good. I have tried a few of the flavours but I think the mais con yelo is my favourite. The owner is friendly and have accommodated my reasonable requests to modify my drink (albeit its not busy). I will continue to come back again. Thank you!

Our family usually buy ourselves some Filipino sweet treats at Mix Mix Co after finishing our shopping at Parklea. The "Halo Halo" dessert is usually a go to for my husband and my in-laws (I personally dont like them as much as they are a bit too sweet for my taste). There are other lighter options (e.g. Sago) if you prefer.

The staff is always so helpful. The quality control is also high standard. One time, I ordered some peach pies and the staff ended up telling me that they didnt feel comfortable serving me the pies (they did give me an option to wait while they can cook some more pies). Honestly I wouldnt have been able to tell had they not told me.

If you have a sweet tooth and want the best tasting dessert in Sydney then Head Over to Mix Mix Co. Their treats are beautifully colourful and very tasty. If you have any questions just ask the very friendly ppl in the shop for clarification. They have a dessert for you for every climate. Hot, warm or Cold.

name: Mix Mix Co. Rooty Hill
address: 5/52 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: We ordered the taho set for our party and it was a hit! Everyone absolutely enjoyed it, and there was plenty left over. Such generous servings and perfectly made taho, complete with delicious condiments.
Solid place for Traditional Filipino desserts. Ordered their Halo Halo. It was creamy, right amount of ice and many toppings.

Superb cold desserts! Visually and gastronomically appetising. Amongst all the things I have ordered, what stood out was the melon coolers. The halo halo was so filling and so was the mango mess. I didn't try that taho as it was my friend who ordered it. She claimed that it tasted so perfect and was served warm, so was perfect for the cold weather.

Great tasting halo halo which is a rarity in Sydney. Lots of options other than it too. Taho is quite alright as well.

Would just like to have a bit more filling for the price, but otherwise it is good enough.

I ordered the taho rental for a night and it was great! Kim was very easy to communicate with to organise the rental; they gave a lot of ingredients and everyone at the event was very happy with the quality. I would definitely recommend to others

name: Mix N Match Bakehouse Doonside
address: 4 Hill End Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We ordered CUTE Cakes for my niece’s birthday! Big thanks to Jenny the baker for accommodating us and putting so much effort into our requested design. We love it and my niece can't stop eating it! Haha.

Special thanks as well to the lovely lady at the counter for recommending the bibingka and puto cheese, we’ve now found some Filipino pastries we absolutely love! Such wonderful Filipino girls. Definitely a must-try bakeshop in Western Sydney!

Contrary to what others have said, I had a pleasant experience with the staff this Monday, 13/10/25. Ate was friendly and attentive, she also offered other products. We had the cheese bread, ensaymada, spanish bread, and the empanada(could be warmer).

My husband and I visited Mix & Match today and were disappointed with the service. Even though the Christmas music created a festive atmosphere, the staff member at the counter did not smile, greet us, or acknowledge us. She seemed uninterested and distracted.
We purchased balut, lumpia sariwa, and mamon, but the lady who served us was rude and didn’t smile the entire time. This kind of attitude is not acceptable in customer service and really affects the store’s image.

Hi, this is my first time buying your puto queso, and I found this metal piece in one of them. I really love puto, but seeing this made me very concerned about safety it could be quite dangerous, especially for kids. Please be more careful when preparing your products.

I visited this Filipino shop a few days ago and had a wonderful time chatting with the lady at the counter while she explained the different breads, sweets, and cakes to me. I tried the turon and ube cake, they were delicious! I’ll definitely be coming back.

name: Mix n Match Bakehouse Rooty Hill
address: 63 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: This is the first time I got disappointed with the service, not sure if it was an honest mistake or if it was deliberate. I bought about 12 types of small breads and 2 desserts. I’m a regular customer so I know around how much I get to spend which is usually between AU 30-45. I was surprised when the cashier was charging me around AU70. She did not question the amount what with the small amount of items I bought. Only when I asked for the receipt did she backtrack and said that she had charged me a full box of bread instead of just 1 piece of a particular one. I told her that I was a regular customer and I know how much I usually spend there. I was the only customer at that time so I know it was not busy to make such a mistake. Please be careful when charging or else we’ll be suspicious every time we buy bread there.

My mum in law is Filipino so I got her the Ube macapuno cake for her birthday from Mix and Match - Rooty Hill.

The cake was so good! It was really moist and the icing had the perfect ube flavour.

Would definitely recommend! The staff here are always so helpful and friendly too.
They have a nice icing cake, i had an ube flavour. Around 85% is icing. They might have changed chef. Icing cake lovers, you’ll going to like this.

It is very disappointing and truly very alarming that you are selling old stock with moulds already. It is not right. My husband came back this morning and showed your staff and the excuse they gave him the green color being " food coloring" in an ube hopia? instead of apoligising and taking accountability of your wrong processes your staff had to lie.The green colours on your hopia are not merely "food coloring", they are in fact MOULDS. People are buying your products and you should be selling fresh ones that will not harm their health. I hope the Mother business of your franchise can see this review and take actions.

I bought 4 pcs of ube buchi, but when I checked the receipt, I noticed I was charged for 5 pcs. Unfortunately, I only noticed it when I was already in the car. I hope staff can double-check orders in the future to avoid this kind of situation. Even if it’s just a dollar, it’s still hard-earned money.

name: Mr Wong Oriental (Filipino) Store
address: Shop 1/34 Hercules St, Ashfield NSW 2131, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Really nice Tito that let us put up our sign. Great range of Filipino groceries! Always helpful, kind and lovely. Please shop here rather than the big guys and bigger stores, help support local! 🇵🇭

Great range of Filipino groceries and lovely family run business.
Support local and small businesses like this and not bigger stores and brands!

Never ever will go to this shop again .. Rude ,Racist ,aggressive and arrogant. Learn how to run a business before you open one .. A business sole purpose is to  satisfy and communicate with your customer not abise them .
a nice shop with a friendly team. I like the skyflake crackers and the puto and the mayonnaise. they sell good products to buy. keep up the good work.

Tried to call the number just now to inquire on some Asian stuff I wanna buy when we drive to Ashfield and some old man shouted at me why I bother asking, said to see the shop instead of calling and dropped the phone! Rude and arrogant! Don't go here.

name: Mummy Inday's Catering
address: 8 Ambrosia St, Macquarie Fields NSW 2564, Australia
hours: {"mon": "Open 24 hours", "tue": "Open 24 hours", "wed": "Open 24 hours", "thu": "Open 24 hours", "fri": "Open 24 hours", "sat": "Open 24 hours", "sun": "Open 24 hours"}
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Great filipino food and service! We had Mummy Inday do our catering for my mum's 50th with about 70 guests and they were all very satisfied with the food, especially the lechon. They did a fantastic job setting up and made sure everything went smoothly for the party in terms of food and drinks. Highly recommend!

Very yummy food - we had so many compliments how tasty their food was, especially their juicy lechon belly and spring rolls! Will definitely order again. Easy to communicate with and very helpful in helping us out with portions. Thank you!

You can never go wrong with Mummy Inday’s catering. They serve the best Filipino cuisine especially their pork belly and of course everyone’s all time favourite Lechon(whole roasted pig). They cook these with perfection!
Their service has always been the best. They are very friendly, well mannered and very professional. Aside from that, the food has always been excellent! Made fresh as always. A budget friendly catering services here in Sydney. Very highly recommended.

We had around 70 guests and the food, customer service and overall experience satisfied not only myself but all of the guests that had attended. I really loved and appreciated the care that was put in from start to finish despite booking the business in very short notice. As we booked Mummy Inday's for my husbands 60th birthday party, it was an honour to have them celebrate with us as they truly made the experience the best. Highly recommended!!
Amazing food and wonderful service! Thank you to Mummy Inday’s Catering and the staff for making everything so smooth and enjoyable.

name: Noemis Cafe
address: 89 Gibson Ave, Padstow NSW 2211, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Big serving. Value for money. Pleasant service. Pang Pinoy. =)
Great food at really great prices. Noemi  always has smile and great coffee.
Always nice coffee and service
Great food best bacon/egg rolls great service with a smile
Great food and great price!!!

name: Oi Manila Food Hall
address: 5/22 Hill End Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: If you are looking for chicken inasal, this place is highly recommended and if you are craving for filipino street food, must visit here. They are also serving a good filo foods like sinigang, sisig and paksiw.

Amazing Filipino restaurant serving delicious homey food.
They have a wide variety of dishes. You can order choices on display or from the menu. Wide selection of charcoal grilled skewers.
Cozy atmosphere with enough seats for small groups. Friendly service. Decent pricing.
They also sell Asian groceries and snacks.
Great place to enjoy a Filipino meal. Highly recommended.

Oi Manila Food Hall – Doonside

We dropped by this little Filipino spot in Doonside, and even though I didn’t eat myself, the group I was with couldn’t stop recommending it—especially if you’re a red meat lover.

It’s got that real street food vibe, just like you’d find back in the Philippines. The guy doing the BBQ out front had such a great personality—friendly, welcoming, and clearly knows his way around the grill! They’ve also got a small seating area inside if you want to dine in.

Overall, a proper foodie joint. Nothing fancy—just good, honest flavours and a feel of home for anyone missing Filipino street eats.

This is my go to spot whenever I’m working nearby. The food is incredibly homely and hits all the right notes of nostalgia. They have all the dishes I loved growing up, plus a great selection of Filipino snacks! It feels like a little piece of home. Definitely recommend anyone to try it out!

VALUE FOR MONEY: 8/10 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
I ordered their pork barbecue meal with 4 skewers, rice, soup and drink ($21), 2 chicken intestines ($3.5 each), and liempo ($13). Portions are a bit small, except for the liempo.

FLAVOUR & QUALITY: 10/10 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Very good 💯. One of the best Filipino barbecues in Sydney. Food is served fresh and hot.

SERVICE: 10/10
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
The family is very friendly, hospitable and the service is quick.

ATMOSPHERE: 9/10
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
The space is relatively small because of assorted groceries, so it feels like eating at a carinderia in the Philippines which I enjoy.

name: Okasyon Catering Services
address: Callow Pl, Woodcroft NSW 2761, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: We recently ordered from Okasyon catering service and everything was absolutely spot on! The sisig tastes like home — full of flavour and perfectly seasoned. The roast beef was incredibly tender, paired with a rich, savoury sauce. The seafood Cajun was another standout, cooked just right and bursting with flavour. Everything else tasted great as well. The quality, consistency, and attention to detail really showed in every dish. Highly recommended!

We had the absolute pleasure of working with Okasyon Catering Services for our wedding on October 12, 2024, at Quibaree Park, Lavender Bay, and we are beyond thrilled to share our experience!
From the initial consultation to the final toast, the team at Okasyon Catering Services went above and beyond to ensure that our special day would be unforgettable. Their passion for culinary excellence was evident, and they took the time to craft a personalized menu that perfectly matched our tastes and preferences.
On the day of the wedding, the setup was nothing short of magical. The presentation of the food was stunning, transforming our outdoor venue into a feast for the eyes as well as the palate. Each dish was beautifully arranged, and the aroma wafting through the park created an inviting atmosphere for our guests.
And the food? Simply spectacular! From the first bite to the last, every course was a delightful experience. Our guests raved about the flavorful appetizers, mouthwatering entrees, and decadent desserts. It was a culinary journey that truly enhanced our celebration.
What impressed us most was the professionalism and attentiveness of the staff. They were friendly, efficient, and always one step ahead, ensuring that everything ran smoothly. Our guests were consistently attended to, and the service was impeccable without being intrusive.
We can’t thank Okasyon Catering Services enough for turning our wedding into an extraordinary event. Their dedication to quality and service made our special day even more memorable. If you’re looking for catering that will elevate your celebration to the next level, look no further than Okasyon Catering Services. We wholeheartedly recommend them for any event

All our guests were impressed with the scrumptious selection of food Okasyon served on my daughter’s 1st. We were impressed with the exemplary customer service, responsiveness, courteousness and professionalism of all staff of Okasyon  Catering Services. Despite the short notice, they were able to cook more than 10 delectable dishes that we requested. We are really grateful and fortunate to have found a trusted and excellent caterer for our events who assures to deliver an exceptional and stress free experience to their customers. We highly recommend Okasyon Catering Services and we will definitely book them again for our next big event.

Thank you Okasyon catering for the delicious food and service well done today at WSCC Christmas party.. Dennis, Eureka,Tita Bambi and your team were highly organised and effectively delivered the food on timeline and executed beyond our expectations. We look forward to get your service again in the future. You are highly recommended.
God bless your business more and more.
Merry Christmas!

Hi Dennis and Eureka,  thank you so much again for all the food you guys had cooked for us. amazingly delicious-was devoured instantly at my daughter's birthday party. Highly recommended Okasyon Catering Services- good people, good service and most especially good food 😀

Jenifer & John Calingao

name: Our Lady of Fatima Catholic Church
address: 89 Shaw St, Kingsgrove NSW 2208, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews:
Very nice church with spacious areas and seating.

It is a Catholic Church with a warm family like congregation. A good mixture of nationalities attend mass there. Very culturally diverse parishioners.

Lovely quaint atmosphere and in good spiritual settings for worship.

Fr Casey is the reason why we love attending mass here each Sunday, he is a Filipino priest which gives us most comfort being Filipinos, we enjoy hearing his words. Fr Emmanuel is wonderful and so friendly 🙏🏽
There is a carpark out the back of the church ⛪️

A great Christian Catholic church within easily accessible parking and transport hubs (bus stop and train station are literally around the corner, with parking across the road from the church) suitable for all ages and people of faith.

A great church with an excellent community, everyone is so welcoming and they hold great activities throughout the year, we attend the feast of Fatima it had excellent community engagement.

Palm Sunday in the courtyard was special.

If you're Filipino the priest is also Filipino and very friendly.

The church is connected to schools and you have a younger crowd for some mass
Love this church priest wonderful person and lovely community.

name: Our Lady of the Rosary Catholic Cathedral
address: 23 Yardley Ave, Waitara NSW 2077, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews:
Nestled amidst the lush greenery of Waitara, you'll find a truly magnificent Church that's sure to take your breath away.

The serene surroundings offer ample parking space, making it convenient for visitors to access the Church.

Additionally, there is a dedicated children's or toddler room within the Church premises, specially designed for our little ones who can sometimes get a little too noisy.

Attended Filipino mass and enjoyed the free snacks after the mass 😍😍😍

Thanks to those who prepared it
Great church. Thank you for allowing our videographers early access to set up for live streaming.
Fantastic school we as well as our daughter love it. Teachers and staff are great. Highly recommend to anyone
Great catholic community

name: P'Nut Rouse Hill
address: GR/112A Main St, Rouse Hill NSW 2155, Australia
description: Asian hawker-style fare, including noodle dishes and soups, in a vibrant restaurant chain.
reviews:
The food was mostly average, I mean there can be more flavours. Even though it looked good at first, it kinda tasted a bit bland. As I was hungry from not having lunch, I ate it but I have had better fried rice than this.

As this restaurant is close to my home, I was really hoping it to be good enough so that I can be a regular, but I guess not. Maybe good for occasionally trying it out, but not on a regular basis.

Well, the good thing is that their meat is halal and that is a good news for me!

Ordered the Cashew Nut Noodles with Chicken... Haven't had flavour like this in a long time

Extremely friendly service, short wait time for food and the food was top tier!! 100% recommend, good price and portions too
Will def come back later
Still as good as when I first ate here when I was a kid. Glad to see it’s thriving. Sheila is a great asset to the team too. Winning smile.

Had a great experience at P’Nut Rouse Hill! The food was delicious and consistently well prepared, but what really stood out was the service. The staff are incredibly accommodating and always smiling, which makes such a difference.

Special mention to the lovely Filipino lady at the counter, Ate Sheila — she’s so warm, welcoming, and genuinely kind. Her positive energy really makes you feel looked after and keeps us coming back.

Highly recommend this place for both great food and exceptional customer service!

Great food.  Staff need training.  Preferred my takeaway.  Advised to suit and wait till they call my name.  Kept waiting until it was long enough and asked where's my food. Lady looks on the pick up shelf and its there.  Waiting around like a knob for no reason ! Just call out the names !!! Geez
