name: Pabico Club 55
address: 1 Mount Druitt Rd, Mount Druitt NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Familiar place for Filipinos on event meetings,  place has descent area to hold events.
Food is ok. Staff are friendly but decoration abd place needs updating

Nice place, food is okay not bad. The area is big but if you’re sat in front of the stage, it’s a cooler place but at the further sides, it’s hot pretty warm. They should have more air conditions around the place so it will be bearable to spend the whole night 🤩

I really enjoyed the Christian service and catching up with my friends!😄🙏❤ The Filipino food was 😋 and this is a happy place and great for celebrating God's word and teaching's. You can feel his presence and love😇🙏❤
Friendly, very accommodating, food is delicious, atmosphere is a whole vibe! The singer Lily is beautiful and is so entertaining. Thank you for a great night!

name: Pabico Fast Food And Asian Groceries
address: Carlisle Ave, Mount Druitt NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Highly recomended for Filipino food and very affordable.

I highly recommend this Filipino food eatery. They cook simple yet nice Filipino cuisine. Must try is their Pork Sinigang, Monggo, and Beef Nilaga. Eating here feels like being back in my hometown, eating my mom's home cooked food. Thank you for providing outstanding dining experience. The price is competitive. You can also shop for nice drinks like Soursop "Guyabano" Juice, Calamansi Juice, and Sarsi.
Hot food wise a little disappointed. The pork dish was barely any meat on it. The beans option wasn’t bad
Pabico is a well-known Filipino fast food eatery in Mt Druitt area. Am one of its long-time customers. Cheers
Native Filipino food, very delicious at affordable price, you won't be disappointed plus 110% customer service satisfaction very friendly staff and superb chef double thumbs up!!

name: Paddy's Night Food Markets
address: Carpark V, 250/318 Parramatta Rd, Homebush West NSW 2129, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
𝗣𝗔𝗗𝗗𝗬𝗦 𝗙𝗢𝗢𝗗 𝗠𝗔𝗥𝗞𝗘𝗧𝗦 - 𝗔 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗦𝗧 𝗙𝗢𝗥 𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗦𝗘𝗡𝗦𝗘𝗦 𝗪𝗜𝗧𝗛 𝗩𝗜𝗡𝗧𝗔𝗚𝗘 & 𝗘𝗫𝗢𝗧𝗜𝗖 𝗥𝗜𝗗𝗘𝗦!

If you’re looking for an experience that hits all the right notes—food, culture, and some of the dopest cars and bikes you’ll ever see—Paddy’s Food Markets is where it’s at!

This place is pure vibes.

From the moment you step in, you’re hit with the mouthwatering aroma of international flavors. Food trucks line the market, serving up everything from juicy burgers to authentic street tacos, aromatic Asian delicacies, and rich, decadent desserts. Every bite was chef’s kiss!

And then, the real show-stoppers—the vintage and exotic cars and motorcycles. Imagine classic muscle cars, sleek supercars, and rare bikes from different eras, all gleaming under the lights. Whether you’re a car enthusiast or just love seeing a lineup of pure automotive art, this exhibition is next level.

But what truly makes Paddy’s special is the people. The energy here is electric—families, car lovers, foodies, and people from all cultures coming together to enjoy a night of great eats and even greater company. It’s a melting pot of flavors, sounds, and styles, making every visit an unforgettable experience.

If you haven’t been yet, do yourself a favor and check it out. Come hungry, bring your camera, and soak in the good vibes!

Love the night market vibe here at Paddy's Night Food Market. Very vibrant and alive with lots of food choices and events happening around.

We went on a Filipino food night event, which coincided with a car show which was awesome.

First off, it was easy to get to via M4. Then there are lots of parking above, with very organized traffic control as you enter the area.
Great car show every month.Good food,great vibe awesome cars

Love the paddy market here! Especially when there’s a food festival been held here. Couple of month ago it was philippine Food Festival,and just yesterday it was Malaysian Food festival.
The food there is always amazing with cars meet.
The line can be really long at ever food stalls but it was worth the wait!
It was great. There are a lot of choices for food. Kids jumping castle and live band. A car show as well. It was a good night out.

name: Pagasa Asian Food Imports
address: 32 Binney Rd, Kings Park NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
I was scammed. Be careful.

I called Susan the owner a month before to advise her of a remittance in October.  I  wanted to remit in instalment but she wanted lump sum. I called and texted the figure. She sent me a form and both information were the same amount. I deposited the money. Her quote was in writing.

She remitted money to me in instalment . After 11 days, she sent me an amount different from what she quoted and gave several strange excuses.

When I sent all the above evidence to job her memory, she became rude and told me to do what I need to do. The tone was very different now that she owes me money.
It was take it or leave it.

I called her a crook.
She owes me money and refuses to pay.

Everything here is documented.

If your a Filipino I highly recommend here for all your food needs from home.
Just the smell brings back memories of the mother land.
Great customer service

name: PanaDarna
address: 55 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews:
What a fantastic find! 🌟 Visited PanaDarna located at the entrance of The Kamayan and had such a wonderful experience with the family during Mother’s Day. You can really feel the warmth of a Filipino family-run business, and everything we tried was fresh, comforting, and full of flavour.

I brought my family along and everyone really enjoyed the experience. My family enjoyed the iced lattes while I went for the iced chocolate, which was rich, refreshing, and delicious. Their famous buko pie absolutely lived up to the hype and is definitely a must-try.

It’s always special seeing Filipino-owned businesses bringing people together through culture, hospitality, and great food here in Western Sydney. Wishing the team at PanaDarna all the best as they continue to grow. Definitely looking forward to coming back again!

name: Panlasang Pinoy
address: 26 Regent St, Kogarah NSW 2217, Australia
description: Straightforward restaurant spotlighting roasted fish dishes, plus classic rice meals.
reviews:
From the moment we walked in, we were greeted with warm smiles and familiar aromas that instantly transported us back to the Philippines. The welcoming atmosphere and friendly service definitely set a positive tone.

There was a good variety on offer, and many of the dishes were enjoyable, especially the classic Filipino comfort foods. However, even as Filipinos, we felt some dishes were lacking a bit of depth and balance. A few items could have benefited from stronger seasoning, more freshness, and better consistency, as some dishes leaned slightly dry or underwhelming compared to traditional home-style flavours. The buffet setup also meant some dishes had been sitting for a while, which affected temperature and texture.

Overall, it’s a decent introduction to Filipino cuisine and a convenient option if you’re craving familiar flavours, but it didn’t quite hit the mark in terms of authenticity and wow factor for us. Still worth trying, especially for those new to Filipino food, but with room for improvement.

A must visit for your Filo cravings. Also best place to replenish your Filo pantry. Available almost everything. Tasty food and friendly staff. I love their dinuguan and barbecue, very Pinoy taste! A little pricey on the Filo goods. I hope they can just put price tags to easy accounting. Street car parking available.
There is no soap, leftover that cost $30, not worth the hype and $35 each per person, no veggie dish, and also limited seating. Overall, not recommended! Go to another Filipino Buffet!
food was fine but service and experience misleading. went to eat on a thursday, but was charged weekend rates & our whole group was overcharged $30 for no reason.

Wonderful family style Pinoy buffet serving home cooked style that your grandma used to make. The people who run it are lovely also and very friendly. $30 on weekdays running from lunch time all the way to dinner. And $35 on weekends where Lechon (suckling pig) is added. Will definitely be back here again.

name: Parklea Markets
address: 601 Sunnyholt Rd, Glenwood NSW 2768, Australia
description: Buzzing market hall featuring over 100 fresh produce, clothing, fast food & assorted goods vendors.
reviews:
I visited this market today and found it quite good overall. The experience was great, with so many shops available under one roof. The wide range of fresh vegetables and fruits is definitely a big plus. Apart from that, you can purchase almost everything a household needs, which makes it very convenient.

If you live nearby, it’s a good place to visit. However, I did find the prices of fruits and vegetables a bit more expensive compared to some other places in Sydney. On the positive side, the kids’ zone is a bonus. The fish and meat shops are another advantage, and there are plenty of fresh juice shops—which I absolutely loved.

Open from Friday to Sunday. We spent a productive morning shopping for fresh fruits and veggies. The prices were really good. We also got some gardening stuff. The Filipino food at Gate 1 was delicious. Get the chicken bbq and siopao.

Located at 601 Sunnyholt Road, Glenwood, New South Wales, Australia 🇦🇺
This is a pretty good market open Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 8.30am until 4.30pm. It has a fantastic nursery section selling all kinds of plants and outdoor decorations. There’s also fresh produce ie fruit and vegetables along with fresh seafood 🦞 and meat 🥩 products. I don’t, however, think the prices are any cheaper than if you were going to a supermarket. There’s plenty of clothing available, but don’t expect anything but basic everyday things, nothing really designer. There is always Australian 🇦🇺 souvenirs and toys, beauty products, sweets 🍭 and nuts, luggage and bags, jewellery, novelty items and more. There’s plenty of parking 🅿️ and good toilet facilities. There is also ATM 🏧 facilities. Worth a visit. 👍🙂

I visited Parklea Market for grocery shopping, and it’s truly a wide and well-organized market. You can find everything under one roof, which makes shopping very convenient. The market is open on Friday, Saturday, and Sunday, and if you go on Sunday, you can grab some really good deals on almost everything. A great spot to explore for groceries and more!
Was my first time visit and loved the green vegetables. I found Fiji apples. Best place to buy veggies from. Had breakfast at the dosa place and loved it. Highly recommend

name: Parlea Markets Leather Store
address: 601 Sunnyholt Rd, Parklea NSW 2768, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: As the name implies it’s a market where you can find wet goods and dry goods. They also have electronics such as cellphones. There are also food stalls right at the front entrance and some bakeries inside. There is one stall outside that caters the Filipino taste!
A large market you can browse on weekends with family. We pickupedn some delicious bbq pork grills and bought some vegetables here.  Not all foods and vegetables here are in good price.

Nice and close to home only 15 minute drive.
Not too big a market has everything that everyone can find.
However, prices not that cheap compared to local stores.
A lot good thing's to buy

name: Pasalubong Oriental Foods
address: 1073 Botany Rd, Mascot NSW 2020, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Owners are friendly n honest. Products from the Philippines are bottled properly.  Sausages are tasty especially the ones that are from Cebu. Jasmine rice is clean, no insects.  Myron pork crackling is very good. I won't go to shop Filipino products aside from Pasalubong in Mascot, they are very good.
the prices are pretty jacked up...Nevertheless, one yearns for Filipino stuffs

I had such a great experience at this store! Very helpful and friendly couple who own the store that made it extra special ontop of getting all your fav Filipino products. Made me feel like I was in the Phillipines… Will be regularly shopping here. Thanks guys!
Excellent  ! My wife is a big fan. It suits all her needs whether it be Filipino food, snacks, western union money transfers, thoughly recommended a visit.
They've got everything. Filo (Asian) products at reasonable prices. Door to door package delivery to the Philippines. Money transfer. Friendly store owners.

name: Phil-Asian Supermarket - St Marys
address: 8/66 Queen St, St Marys NSW 2760, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Conveniently  located in the arcade really close to plenty of parking, the train station and bus stops.
Really well stocked, staff is super friendly and reasonable priced. I will definitely  be back.

I had an absolutely fantastic experience at Phil Asian store. Jovie, the owner, goes above and beyond. She's not only the friendliest business owner I've ever met, but also incredibly helpful and accommodating. From the moment I walked in, Jovie made sure I felt welcome and comfortable, ensuring my visit was a positive one. The prices at Phil Asian are unbeatable. I've visited many asian stores and none compare; their prices are the lowest I've come across. This, combined with the outstanding service, makes the Phil Asian store my top choice for groceries and specialty items. I will be definitely returning and will highly recommend Phil Asian store to all my friends. Thank you Jovie for such a wonderful experience and making shopping such a pleasure!

Best customer service, convenient location, a good variety of Filipino foods to choose from, friendly staff, quick and reliable service. Not surprising that this store almost got a perfect 5 star reviews. Only 1 review seemed to have a negative experience 6 years ago. I moved to St. Marys in 2019 and this store was still owned by a different person that time. Kudos to the new owner. I used to go to different Filipino stores but Phil-Asian is the best filo store for me. Not only due to all I mentioned but there's a lot of parking available a few meters away and their business hours is so convenient.

Ate Jovie was absolutely amazing! She was incredibly friendly, approachable, and made us feel welcome right away. Her warm personality and genuine care really stood out, and she went above and beyond to make sure we had a great experience. Highly recommended – thank you, Ate Jovie!
The best filipino shop around st Mary's with a lot of products and great price. The shop owners are friendly. I've been shopping there for months and I highly recommend shopping there. 👍❤️

name: Philippine Airlines
address: Level 4/49-51 York St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews: I have a connecting return flight from Davao to Manila to Brisbane. I called the pal customer service to advice that I will forfeit my local flight and said I will start my journey from Manila to Brisbane 3 days before my flight date. The guy I was talking to said there is no problem with that and I can just check in at Manila international airport. I did ask him if there anything I  have to do and he said no and that I am all set. When I got to Manila international airport I was told that they could not check me in because I did not follow my flight sequence and I am in danger of losing my flight as a consequence unless I have to pay the reissuance fee of $150 so I can fly home with my wife.I did explain that I have done all my diligence to call and inform the Airlines about my plan and I was promise of no dramas. I submitted a complaint to the complaint department and all they offer me is sorry for their mistake that cost me money . After flying with them for so many years this is what I've got. So disappointing

I am about to travel to Manila from Sydney with PAL with my family of 6. Yesterday they changed the aircraft type and seating layout. I spent a lot of time organising the seats over 2 booking to ensure we had 5 seats in a row and none of my children were next to a stranger. Also paid extra for this. When they reallocated the seats, the system allocated my 9 year old to a non existent seat. So now he has no seat and there is a stranger sitting between my partner and my 6 year old son.

Over 8 hours of chatting online as you cannot call them. All of the solutions offered were ridiculous. Been escalated several times. They can’t move the random person to another seat without their agreement, but had no problem moving my children without mine. Rather than relaxing getting ready for a holiday, I am up all night stressing about this.

We got quite a good deal fly from Taipei to Sydney via Manila. The business class both for short and long haul are comfortable and good service. The seat for short haul has a foot rest which is my favorite. The long haul has lay flat seat (with massage function), excellent space, touch screen TV, plenty of entertainments. The teams deliver wonderful service. They are very thoughtful, prepare a Lunar New Year Red Pocket with a chocolate while serving the lunch. If there was anything I would ask for more, probably is the food-they are good and could be better. One more thing, they are not affiliated with any of the three major airline alliances currently. So you wouldn’t be able to accumulate your mileage except their own Mabuhay Miles frequent flyer program.

A Disappointing and Irresponsible Experience

I deeply regret choosing Philippine Airlines, as my experience with them has been nothing short of frustrating and disappointing. They demonstrated an appalling level of irresponsibility and lack of accountability when it came to handling our luggage, and their customer service was completely ineffective.

During our trip, they damaged and lost parts of my wife's walking frame, an essential mobility aid. I immediately reported the issue and provided all necessary documentation, including tickets and photos of the damaged frame. Despite this, Philippine Airlines failed to follow up or take any meaningful action.

Over the past several months, I have contacted them more than 12 times via phone, email, and social media—each time being reassured that I would receive a follow-up call. That call never came. Instead, they repeatedly ignored our case, gave false promises, and played dumb whenever I followed up.

This airline has proven itself to be extremely negligent in handling customer complaints and utterly disinterested in resolving issues that directly impact their passengers. A responsible airline should acknowledge its mistakes and compensate customers for damages they have caused. Philippine Airlines did neither.

In the end, they lost us as customers, and I strongly advise others to avoid flying with them. If they treat a case involving a medical necessity with such disregard, I can only imagine how they handle other issues. Fly with an airline that values its passengers—Philippine Airlines is not one of them

I’m surprised with the quality, punctuality and kindness from all staff.
I got a very good deal, but wasn’t expecting too much. The airplanes are amazing and very comfortable. I will definitely flight with Philippine Airlines again 💙

reviews:
I have ordered Pinoy Chef’s Catering Menu 3 times since March 2025. Their food is scrumptious, service is excellent and delivery has always been on time.

They are also flexible with my requests, such as separating the chilli (not a fan of spicy food but our some of our guests are) or changing the delivery time at an earlier timeslot. I truly appreciate their exceptional customer service and recommend them to all of you.

Even my non-Fillo family & friends truly enjoyed their food as well. Salamat po! ☺️
Nice little nook in Kings Park industrial area. The crispy pata dish I ordered was tasty and a good size for my quick lunch. The service was swift and warm. I won’t hesitate coming back here :)

Great value meals and fantastic coffee.
A little cafe with your standard cafe menu but with extra offerings from the Philippines.
We always enjoy visiting for a casual meal. The Filipino breakfast combos are great value at $15 with the garlic rice.
I couldn't say no to the biscoff lattes when I visited for a coffee either!
We'll be back to try the dessert menu as there is quite a selection of fancy cakes to choose from. It's a shame they aren't open late as access is great with easy parking out the front and located in an industrial area. A little hidden gem in the outskirts of Blacktown.
Great Cafe. Relaxed. Good food.  Very generous portions. Excellent coffee. Not bitter. Creamy. Perfect place while waiting for my car to be serviced next door.

We ordered catering package for my son's birthday party.
Food was delicious, good value for money.
They were aware regarding food allergies and able to communicate with us well.
We will order again.
Highly recommended. 👍👍👍

name: Pinoy Food Trip and Cakes
address: 28 Walz St, Rockdale NSW 2216, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Called them and they had extra big ube cake. First time in the store and so happy to see lots of cakes and bakery! Staff lady was so sweet who gave me extra leche flan, candles, a happy birthday greeting for my ube bday cake and extra bakery to take home. Definitely my new Filo bakery to go to! Maraming salamat po!

For Great Food & Service: "Incredible dining experience! The food was delicious and perfectly presented, and our server made excellent recommendations. Can't wait to come back!"
For Ambience/Date Night: "This place exceeded expectations. The warm ambiance and friendly staff made us feel at home. Perfect for a cozy evening".
For Families/Casual: "Hidden gem! The staff are friendly, and the food is excellent and well-priced

So appreciate you taking my late request for catering. This meant so much as it was for the 7th day Novena for my beloved Dad who passed away. My dad would have enjoyed eating this just as much as we all did honouring him. It was great value for money and the quantity was very generous and as compared to other nearby restaurants. The pork adobo sitaw and beef caldereta was absolutely delicious. Always love your Ube Macapuno cake - I’ve lost count how may I have bought from you guys including your delicious Suman! Thankyou as well for letting me try your homemade longanisa- wow what a flavour bombe!
Definitely coming back for more Pinoy Food Trips & Cake 🇵🇭❤️🙏
Very generous and helpful. They accepted our late order via Uber and even adding free “Malabon” noodles and “Suman” pastries. Thank you

This is our go to Filipino food spot. The food is absolutely amazing and very reasonably priced. My husband always says the flavors and ambiance remind him so much of the Philippines, which makes it even more special. The staff are incredibly friendly and accommodating. Highly recommended for a quick, satisfying meal!

name: Pinoy Grill N Chill
address: 3a/3 Oxford Rd, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I was in the area and have heard good reviews from friends. The food did not disappoint.  I went for lunch but came back to takeaway some pork skewers, crispy pata and dinuguan which were muy delicioso. Happy tummy!

Ordered a sinigang, lumpia and pancit for my birthday lunch with my partner. The food was absolutely spot on from how my family makes it, every single meal was delicious from what we ordered. The atmosphere is also great. It was a hot weather today which is around 35 but the place counters the heat with its own air-conditioning and music. Really nice place, thank you guys !!!

First time dining here, and the food is fantastic. Made the mistake of ordering 2 x crispy pata. Only 1 is required between 2 people. Went on a Sunday afternoon and there were already customers eating there. Great Filipino food at decent prices.

These lovely people have catered for my Daughters 1st birthday & my sons 15th birthday. On both occasions the staff & the food have been absolutely amazing !!  The staff have been very understanding as my mum who is currently overseas pays for food and I cant thank the staff in being very understanding while serving me while they are busy. Thank you so much !

It was my first time to dine in at Pinoy Grill N Chill, atmosphere is amazing, soft music, I feel at home, service is very good, I feel like I’m eating at home with my family, highly recommend the Crispy Pata, reminded me on how my mother used to cook the Crispy Pata, I love the desserts too, I even had some take aways haha.. I met Ate Cynthia for the first time and I must admit she made me feel like family. The background music brings back some good memories of my childhood. I will certainly go back again. Oh I forgot, they have the best boiled rice too… ube cake is one of the best I ever had too. I could go on and on but I’m afraid that I might ran out of space. I just wanted to take this opportunity as well to say thank you for the lovely and friendly service as well. Job well done!!! I’m proud of you Ate Cynthia!!!!! Keep up the good work. I’m a proud Filipina and it’s so uplifting to see my own kind doing a good job. All the best Ate Cynthia and her team. Compliments to the chef.

name: Pinoy Grocer
address: 8 Butler Rd, Hurstville NSW 2220, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Friendly staff, slow service, no price tags on items which does not help in this economy
Very accomodating staff. The store offers variety of Filipini products

Good shop..
Friendly and Helpful
The adobo here is better than the Pinoy Panlasong. Sometimes there are even Dunkin Donuts

name: Pinoy Lounge
address: 1 Hill End Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Very good family restaurant! We ended up sharing a platter I wanted to teach my very western daughter how to eat like Filipino and so we washed our hands and ate with our hands. We also had leftovers, (because of course). The food was good, but honestly? The atmosphere is what sold it.

The karaoke setup was solid and the vibe was warm, loud, and exactly what you want on a rainy night. Romi was singing when we were there and she absolutely understood the assignment 😍her voice was on point, energy up, and the whole place felt better because of her. Even if you’re not singing, it’s great just to sit back and enjoy.

Service was friendly and relaxed, not rushed, not awkward. By the end we were overstuffed, happy, and very aware that unbuttoning pants was the correct choice.

If you want Filipino food with proper karaoke energy and a fun, welcoming vibe, this place delivers. Would 100% go back, especially if Romi’s singing again.

Excellent food and awesome service..
Trisha is nice person and very accomodating.
Our first time to dine in here in Pinoy Lounge.  We're coming back to try other food.

The Filipino food was absolutely delicious — the crispy rolls were golden and crunchy on the outside with a soft, flavorful filling, the grilled stuffed squid was smoky, juicy, and perfectly seasoned with onions and peppers, and the stir-fried vermicelli noodles were light, tasty, and full of fresh vegetables and seafood. Every dish had an authentic taste and generous portion, making the whole meal so satisfying and worth coming back for! ✨ will come again

Came here by Googling "filipino restaurant near me", and I wish it was actually nearer to me cause the food is amazing.
Staff were attentive and gave excellent recommendations. Variety of food selection with generous servings, all very fresh. Masarap 🫶

My photos do not do the food justice.
The Sisig was crispy, zesty,  rich, with a slight kick, and  morish.

A great Filipino eatery for dining in, drinking or just taking in the kareeoke singing by the patrons, lol.
Traditional dishes & even the Ube Lager available here! ) AAA+++

Southern highlands FILIPINO SHOP

name: Pinoy Station - Filipino Restaurant
address: Unit 7/24 Main St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I’ve ordered from Pinoy station a few times and their food are true to the Fillo taste! I’ve tried both catering size & take away serving and they are generous with the quantity provided. My husband and in-laws (who aren’t Filipinos) like their dish too, especially the Beef Caldereta, Chicken Spring rolls, Pancit Palabok, Turon (Banana spring rolls), Tortang talong (Fried eggplant with egg), Embutido, Fried Tilapia, BBQ Pork and Leche Flan. I highly recommend them.

What I also appreciate is that when I requested for BBQ Pork, which was not on their menu, Jerome provided it for me without any hesitation. That is a mark of a true excellent customer service! I will defo order again! Thank you Jerome, Marissa and Team for always giving us an A+ service and products! :)

Smelt really good, decided to dine in.
$15 for 2 dishes, $20 for 3 dishes.
Portion was small, they were served on palm sized plates.
We had peanut sauce beef, pork belly and pork with white rice.
Pork was too salty to finish.
Place was also pretty filthy with food scraps, cockroaches on the floor.
There was extended seating area behind the store itself to accommodate more customers but it seemed like they hardly require that space.
2 stars for the fragrance and home cooked family business, perhaps the other dishes were great but would not dine here again.

Absolutely beautiful.  Really loved the lechon...the res coloured potk and something else dish  ..the mince with veggies..oh and the banana and jack fruit fritter was worth driving to Blacktown from Manly for!   Hubby ate a whole different range which he also loved.  Owner was lovely also.

Their Pansit Malabon was absolutely delicious! I will definitely order again in the future and also try other dishes. My order was ready on time, and the staff gave excellent customer service. Maraming salamat po.
Whenever I come here I get the giant spring roll (can’t remember the name!) and the pansit. Never disappoints. Kind servers and reasonable prices.

name: Pinoy Stop (Doonside)
address: 18 Hill End Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Sun-22-Feb-2026, 11:30am-12.15pm. Casual dinning Festive Filipino food at the rear of a grocery store. Fast and friendly service, recommend coming before lunch rush. The pork bbq sticks had a good balance of sweet and salty and the pork sisig  was super crunchy and had just the right amount of chilli hit. Got the medium combo $16 plus bbq sticks. Good portion size for the cost. Small dinning area, ok for 2-4 people but suggest takeaway. Street parking available, you can be lucky to get a spot infront as most locals tend to come and go. Plenty of parking along the streets adjacent to the Main Street. Also 2 mins walk from Doonside Station.

Our first time at Pinot Stop and we thoroughly enjoyed it. Only just started in the Filipino food arena and tried some new and amazing dishes here.
The sisig is incredible.
Worth the visit if you are in the area.
Amazing home cooked phillipino food, excellent service and very well priced. Highly recommended.

Pinoy Stop will surprise you. As you enter the shop, it seems like an ordinary Asian store, but as you go in further, at the end of the shop, there is a small street like eatery restaurant with 6 small tables. Either you pick from the readily available pre-cooked food from the stand or order from the menu posted on the wall. Their groceries have different asian items varieties. Only 1 hour street parking is available.

We dine in and choose the $14 and the $16  2 dishes and rice. Their portion of food is so little for the price that you paid for.

Not coming back anymore as this is the second time I tries eating and not worth the money you are spending for.

I hope you guys can improved on your serving portion.

name: Pinoy Stop Ashfield
address: 2 Hercules St, Ashfield NSW 2131, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Found an amazing store in Ashfield. Can’t still believe that we have something like this is sydney

They really have an amazing food. The store is pretty clean and staff is so careful and knowledgable in handling food for customers. I’m not sure if she’s the owner but she knows what she’s doing.

The shop is well stored with so many asian products. The labels are on point, what you see is actually what the price is (not unlike other stores), this is just way too organized. The girl in the register was pretty nice and funny as well.

The only downside i can see is that they have a pretty small space for the customers to walk in.

I love the sisig n also the woman on the register😳 is she single?

This place offers an excellent range of food choices. The quality and taste of their food far exceed the unbelievably low prices 😳😳 Coupled with the nice and friendly staff at the register, the overall experience is wonderful and just brings me back from home. Definitely will come again!
Pretty & Friendly staff, organized and clean shop. Cheap price goods and loaded shelves! Recommended place to go👍👍👍

The food at Pinoy Stop Ashfield is delicious and truly tastes like home. Everything was freshly cooked and flavorful, and the staff were friendly and welcoming, making the whole experience even better.

Good looking, super friendly and approachable staff at the register! 🥰 The shop is packed with all kinds of Asian goodies and the prices are wayyy cheaper than anywhere else. If you haven’t been here yet, you’re seriously missing out, highly recommended!! 👌🏻

name: Pinoy Stop Campbelltown
address: 218 Dumaresq St, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: My always go to Filipino Store with bunch to choose from dried fish, canned and frozen foods, biscuits, snacks, desserts at iba pa. Staffs are so nice and helpful.

Your one-stop shop for Asian and Filipino groceries! Great range of fresh produce, pantry staples, snacks, and hard-to-find Filipino favourites all in one place. The store is well stocked, prices are reasonable, and the staff are always helpful and welcoming. Whether you’re doing a full grocery run or just chasing a specific ingredient, this place makes it easy. Definitely worth a visit if you love cooking or exploring Asian cuisine.

Pinoy Stop in Campbelltown has become my favourite place where I can buy any food filos. Friendly and very helpful staff. Clean place. Products are well organised on the shelves. Highly recommendable filo shop.

Very friendly and helpful staff 😊
Also so many varieties of products to choose. Love going here for my pinoy goodies
Spacious store and clean, unlike other Asian grocers. Stock is very good. Staff are friendly.

name: Pinoy Stop Ingleburn
address: 44 Carlisle St, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: All you need for filipino stores. Many varieties and brands to choose from. But aisle spaces are too narrow to fit two-way traffic. Staff are friendly and kind.

A good filo grocery in the south west where there is a lot of options specially when one is looking for bangus, longganisa, hot dogs etc. and price is affordable for a filo grocery that is 40+km away from city centre.
There for the lunch menu. Plenty of customers on a Saturday so it must be good. We weren't disappointed, good food at a great price. Will be back again for sure
Good variety of stock which we could only usually get from the Blacktown area before, so glad we can it locally now

Good range of products.
Easy to move around the shop.
Pricing can be abit more bigger.
Neat and tidy and checkout lady friendly.

I love how they carry not only the usual favorites but also hard-to-find items that remind me of home. The store is clean and well-organized, and the staff are incredibly friendly and hospitable. This is a very affordable Filipino store that I always go to in Sydney. All my cravings are satisfied here, and the staff are very welcoming. This store really makes me feel like I’m back in the Philippines—like in a sari-sari store, mini mart, or tindahan. Thumbs up to the owner and the staff—I hope they continue to do well!

I always go there to buy food consumption so good. That currently unfair staff today not worth to be appreciated but corrupt. I always buy in that over 100 but now that the current staff over charge me with that bag then starting now I would never go to that shop. So ridiculous and unfair.

Such a wonderful shopping experience.
The store is orderly and incredibly pleasant. Staff were friendly and genuinely helpful. There are heaps of products available that makes me feel Im home. Definitely a place to recommend.

name: Pinoy Street Au
address: 601 Sunnyholt Rd, Parklea NSW 2768, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I recently visited Pinoy Street AU Parklea and had one of the most upsetting experiences I've ever had. I used to look forward to buying Pork Bbq and Isaw here, but during my last visit, I felt completely disgusted. While eating, I found a cockroach on my takeaway food. The sight of it made me feel sick immediately and I honestly wanted to vomit what I had already eaten!
This incident left me extremely concerned about the cleanliness and overall food handling of their takeaway shop. Because of this experience, I will never order takeaway or dine in at Pinoy Street AU Parklea again. I hope they will take significant steps to improve their sanitation and food safety handling. Unfortunately, based on this visit, I cannot recommend Pinoy Street AU to anyone.

Filipino food at Parklea Markets. From classic BBQ dishes, they now have a store right inside the markets. All the favourite ingredients for your Filipino home cooking! Also snacks and other Asian food products!

The BBQ kiosk is outside and ample seating is provided. It gets busy as the whole market is buzzing during the weekend!

the place brought my memories back. food is authentic delish, customer service is top notch and atmosphere is the best. chicken inasal is a must try and highly recommended! definitely will be a regular here.
10/10 experience here at pinot street au @parklea. the staff are approachable and even the owner itself! highly recommended 🥰👌🏼
Filipino breakfast, that was pretty good

name: Pinoy ‘To Atbp Cafe
address: 109 Oxford St, Bondi Junction NSW 2022, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
A hidden gem serving up wonderful Filipino food, right in Bondi Junction! This time we tried Lechon Kawali (crispy and tender pork belly) as well as sizzling Sisig (pork with chicken liver) with a side of garlic rice. All were delicious. Service also friendly, and food appeared on our table within minutes.

Advice for the undecided: Walk in past the unassuming storefront, through to the end and give these guys a go. Their menu is in Filipino, so expect to Google, but there is such a wide range on offer, suited to anyone from the faint to brave hearted.

Bad service! Asked for a menu and couldn’t provide me one, I had to open Uber Eats to see their menu and when I was ready to order, been told to wait until they are finished cooking the other patron’s food. Ambiance is none existent, place is hot and humid, not enough seats to accommodate more than 10. Food may be good but, couldn’t say/judge if they can’t even provide service. Pity!

This is one the best filo restaurants I have been to in Sydney! Their food is delicious, I have been back multiple times with my partner. We have tried quite a few filo restaurants around Sydney and this is by far the best one! Definitely try the sisig and Ginataang Kalabasa

Extremely bad service. Waited almost an hour for food to arrive (3 dishes), and even then, it was not worth the wait. The store at the front has a good variety of Asian grocery items, but the service, like the cafe, is poor.
What a great little cafe. Very casual in the back of a Filipino supermarket. Extremely tasty, affordable, authentic food. What a treat! Great for something quick and delish.

name: Potato Station Rooty Hill
address: 52 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I bought a piece of bbq for my son then when we went home I saw the bbq it was toasted/ burned, so hard to bite 😔

Firstly, the name doesn't fit to the type of main food that this place is offering.  Though it's a knock-off of the much-known brand Potato Corner,  their potato fries offering is hardly the reason why people come to this place.  People come to eat the silogs!

Last Sunday, me and wife went to Rooty Hill to have brunch.  We usually go to Kamayan to have breakfast for a legit lutong bahay dish but we decided to check out the place called Potato Station to check out their offerings. Since they have silogs, we decided to go in and dine for a change.

I ordered Longsilog and my wife ordered Lekasilog. My order was served first and minutes later, my wife's.  They served Hotsilog instead.  My wife turned it down as she ordered a Lekasilog.  On the other table, an Aussie guy ordered Corsilog (corned beef) but was served a bistek. Instead of apologising, the owner joked around to the guy to say it nicely in tagalog accent so he will be clearly understood.  For me that's rude and not welcoming.

On my wife's case, instead of apologising, she told us that the kitchen staff is half-asleep! You don't tell your customer that. Let alone trying to joke around to your customers that you just met.  Take your business seriously as well as your customers. If you want to know what customer service really is, go to Takam in Darlinghurst or Lencia's in Castle Hill.
The food is great tasting and the atmosphere along with the staff make it a great place to eat in or takeaway.

What a lucky and wonderful discovery here in Rooty Hill! 🌟 Visited the newly opened Potato Station with the family during Mother’s Day and honestly came away very impressed. From the moment we entered, there was such a warm and welcoming atmosphere that made the experience feel both comfortable and memorable.

The space itself is beautifully set up with a modern yet authentic feel. Plenty of natural sunlight comes through the venue, the ventilation is excellent, and the overall ambience feels cosy while still being fresh and contemporary. It’s the kind of place where you can comfortably enjoy a meal, catch up with family and friends, spend time working quietly, or even host a larger gathering. The venue feels very versatile and community-oriented, with seating arrangements that work well for small groups while also having the capacity for bigger events and celebrations.

Accessibility is also excellent, especially being located right next to a major car park which makes visiting very convenient.

Food and drinks were fantastic. We tried the loaded cheesy bacon fries which were generous, flavourful, and perfect for sharing. I also had the iced white chocolate while another family member enjoyed the iced matcha, both of which were refreshing and well made. What stood out was the variety across the menu, with breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks, breads, and drinks all available, making it a place you could easily return to multiple times and still have something new to try.

You can really feel the heart behind the business too. It’s always inspiring seeing Filipino-owned and woman-led businesses creating welcoming community spaces here in Western Sydney. The hospitality shown throughout the visit was genuinely appreciated.

I give Potato Station my highest recommendation and can absolutely see why it’s quickly becoming one of the hottest new spots across Rooty Hill, the Blacktown LGA, and broader Western Sydney. Looking forward to coming back again and wishing the team all the very best for the upcoming grand launch on 23 May 2026!

We went to habe a breakfast with my 2 driends and son today the staff was not that aporachable and seem not  to be aporachable my friend ordered tapa siloh the other  friend bangsilong my son beef steak and i ordered longsilong my 2 friends oder came on time mine and my son took forevwr and my 2 driends was almost finish having there breakfast and my order  and my son hasnt came yet and when it was time the stafg gici g me a difderent one that i did not oder will not reccommed vwry slow servixe ans dont have staff who knows wjat there doing not even busy food was ok bit was not happy of the experience we had with this restaurant
