name: SP Originals
address: 141a Liverpool Rd, Burwood NSW 2134, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Everytime I drive past SP Originals when they are open I have to pop in. I order some cooked to go as well as some frozen croquettes. I love the prawn and the chorizo croquettes. The cod fritters are a hit with the kids. Small family business with lovely owners.

If you like Portuguese croquetas de bacalao (cod croquettes) then you should not miss croquettes from @sp_originals .   I have had so many cod croquettes in the past and this one is a must!  I ordered Seafood Sample box yesterday evening and Sofia delivered to me this morning.  They are well packed and still hot and crispy outside but so soft and tasty inside.  I reheat them in the oven for few minutes.  The croquettes are not oily at all.   The amount of cod fish and potato just perfect!!  The flavour is just right which is not too salty.    I can’t stop eating them! 😄 The prawn rissols are so delicious too! I like the falvour of garlic in the prawn white sauce.     Sofia is also kindly gave me a lunch box which is so cute! ❤️. Thank you!!

I've ordered from SP Originals a few times and even sample boxes to gift people. Each time they are delivered fresh and still hot. The prawn is a family favourite, but you can't go wrong with any of their flavours! The leftovers heat up great in the air fryer!

SP Originals opened today on Liverpool Road in Burwood. This Portuguese bakery cafe sees Sofia Pestana working from her grandmother’s recipe to produce Portuguese croquettes and pastries. This bricks and mortar store started out as a home-based business during COVID-19 and proved successful enough to open a commercial kitchen, with a public-facing front of house. Today it’s packed with members of the Australian-Portuguese community. “There’s a new Portuguese place that’s opened up, so I came to support my community,” one woman explains.

Pão de Deus ($5) is the star of the sweets. This Portuguese ‘bread of god’ is made with coconut, and eggy and soft under a dusting of icing sugar. It beats the Portuguese tart ($5) that looked and tasted the part with crisp pastry and a vanilla-heavy custard, but wasn’t quite set enough. A small sample box ($30/24) allows you to eat your way through the range of croquettes, and it’s hard to pick a favourite. Minced mild chorizo scrubs up well on a crescent shaped croquette with scalloped edges. The green-flecked cod or bacalao has a nice balance between pungent fish and potato, while prawn has a good creamy garlic sauce base. Even the vegetarian version is exciting, dressed up with turmeric, carrot, green peas and mild spices.

Yummy Portuguese croquettes.

Outwardly they look similar to some finger food in other cuisines (e.g. empanadas from the Philippines, or pastel from Indonesia) but it’s a bit different. The outward skin reminds me of Dutch croquettes, but thinner.

name: Saint Paul's Catholic Church (St Paul the Apostle, Parish)
address: 40 Buckleys Rd, Winston Hills NSW 2153, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews:
This is the only church I'd attended so far who blessed anniversary and birthdays and only one who offered lunch food to share after mass.

Just had a Christmas party today, it was really fun. Lovely community 😊❤️

Beautiful Church, wonderful Priests with traditional values that truly care, great place of devotion with many groups and ministries to choose from. Wonderful place just to chill out and meditate with, beautiful gardens, spectacular district views and peaceful surroundings. Highly recommended.

To be honest, I’ve never attended the church. I’m leaving a review because of the view of the city, mountains, valley and sky that you can get next to the presbytery. I’ve been it there a couple times to take some photos and it is amazing. I’ve done some research and got turns out that if there isn’t any haze in the air, you can see all the way to Wollongong.
It’s amazing if there is a storm over the valley and you can just watch the lightning. There is a bench to sit on and it’s all grass. There is a car park that you can park in and look at the view too.
Overall, it’s a great little secret for any admirers and photographers alike.

⛪Holy Mass at St Paul the Apostle Parish, Winston Hills  😇🙏
We enjoyed listening to the Filipino Parish Priest, Fr. JP Escarlan. 😇
It's also our first time to see an electronic Tap and Go donation for the Parish. It's so cool! ⭐
There's also a Marriage Encounter postcard announcement here and we're interested to attend. 🥰🙏
#thankfulgratefulblessed
September 15, 2024 24th Sunday in Ordinary Time, Year B

St.Paul The Apostle up on The Hill at Winston Hills. A very happy friendly Parish, two wonderful Priests for our Spiritual guidance , both very humble men , terrific organist & singer at the 9.30am Sunday Mass, lovely Morning teas every other Sunday. I always feel happy there.🤗

name: Sans Rival Food Service Fairfield
address: 52 The Crescent, Fairfield NSW 2165, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I did my grocery shopping here and stayed within a strict budget, so I was tracking the price of every item up to the total. Unfortunately, I noticed the cashier added extra amounts to some of the prices. Please double-check the prices of your items carefully. Kapag binigay na ang resibo iba na ang total. It’s disappointing to experience this, especially from fellow Filipinos.

Everyone gets a warm welcome upon entering the shop. All the staff members are friendly. They sell a variety of Philippine products from snacks, cooking ingredients, frozen foods, cool drinks, cooked viands and native snacks. They cater for grab n go meals and big tray party food made to order. You can also order your Noche Buena cooked food in trays here, too.  Remit money or send package to the Philippines through them. San ka pa?

I visit most of the time,most of pilipinos shop,eat lunch,and sending money overseas available,they charged $12 dollars for i small cup of boiled rice and 1small serve of pilipino dish.few feedback of customers told me its a bit pricey.

Meals consistently taste good here, but the serving (or pricing) can be improved. For a lower price on other nearby vietnamese and chinese restaurants, you can get much more meat and vegetable, with rice as well. Came on a hot day and it was so hot to eat inside too.
It was the best food I ate from blue sky shawarma I really like it best services thank you guys

name: Shunli Supermarket
address: Shop 9-10, 36/44 John St, Lidcombe NSW 2141, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews: Very cheap and convenience
Cheap and affordable prices from vegetables to meat and Asian snacks. They are open till late.
Love Sun flower seeds Roasted and Plain flavor
Cheap and staff friendly,
Has huge varieties of filipino goodies

name: Sir Manong Chatswood Chase
address: Chatswood Chase Shopping Centre, g031/345 Victoria Ave, Chatswood NSW 2067, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
First time going on a public holiday as well so have a surcharge. Open at 12, we went in at 12. Very well aircon abit cold for my liking.
We ordered sisig small plate, chicken adobo, garlic fried rice and crispy pata(pork knuckle).

Sisig was served in hotplate but doesnt really stay hot, I had better sisig before to be honest. Chicken Adobo as main , portion is small.
Garlic fried rice good portion.
Crispy pata would be the highlight of the meal, good portion and so Crispy, u dip it with vinegar and they gave a small pickled veg.
Meal not very extensive.
Total just over $100 for 3 people.

Sir Manong in Chatswood is a real gem. The food is absolutely delicious — full of authentic Filipino flavour with a modern twist. Every dish feels hearty and comforting, especially the crispy pork belly and adobo, which are perfectly cooked and beautifully presented. The staff are warm, welcoming, and genuinely attentive, making you feel right at home. The atmosphere is relaxed yet vibrant, great for both casual meals and family gatherings. It’s wonderful to see Filipino cuisine represented so well on the North Shore. Highly recommended for anyone wanting a satisfying and memorable dining experience!

Great place to hit your filo cravings.
The pork knuckle and beef kare kare are delicious! Great for sharing or if you can solo adventure.

Do note it's located in the food court at B level not ground as we were walking around in circles looking for it.

Sir Manong Restaurant  Signature Pinoy food was absolutely divine—fresh, tender, and perfectly dressed. Every bite felt like a little slice of summer. The service was equally impressive; our server,  was attentive and had great menu knowledge. A truly wonderful experience from start to finish. Highly recommend for a special occasion!" "From the moment we walked in, we were greeted warmly and seated right away, even without a reservation. The restaurant has such a vibrant, fun energy. Our waiter was a star—super friendly, checked on us just the right amount. The small plates were great, too, but the service truly made the day."
"The atmosphere and decor here are absolutely stunning—it's a great place to meet friends.
Kudos to sir Manong.

We visited Sir Manong’s in Chatswood and ordered the same dishes we usually get at their other branch — kare kare, binagoongan, and a few of our regular favourites. Unfortunately, the quality was completely different this time. The meat was much harder to chew, and the flavours just weren’t the same as what we’re used to.

It was quite disappointing, especially because we really love their other branch and always make the effort to visit whenever we’re in Sydney. The difference in taste and overall quality between the two locations was very noticeable, and not in a good way.

Looking back, we probably should have played it safe and gone back to the branch we know and trust instead of trying the Chatswood one. We were hoping for the same great experience, but sadly it just didn’t deliver this time.

name: Sir Manong Eastern Creek
address: Shop 3501/159 Rooty Hill Rd S, Eastern Creek NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We ordered crispy pata and the torta,  have to say both dishes were amazing!!! The pata was crispy enough but the meat wasn't dry but moist.

The torta you need to experience, full of flavour, the dried kale on top added a nice dynamic which changed the flavour profile Im used to with my wife's version.

Highly recommend!!!!

Sir Manong’s is always a must-visit for us whenever we’re in Sydney. We genuinely go out of our way to eat here because the food has never failed to impress. We’ve been in Australia for over two years now, and I’ve already celebrated two of my birthdays here — and both times, this restaurant has been part of those special memories. That alone says a lot about how much we love this place.

Our usual orders include classics like kare kare and binagoongan, and they are consistently outstanding. The meat is always tender, perfectly cooked, and full of flavour, while the sauces are rich, balanced, and comforting — exactly how Filipino food should be. Every dish feels thoughtfully prepared, and you can really taste the care that goes into the cooking.

Another thing we really appreciate is how family-friendly this branch is. While we wait for our food, our son always enjoys playing with other kids in the small play area just outside the restaurant. It makes the wait feel shorter and more relaxed for everyone, especially for families dining with young children.

The service is just as good as the food. Staff are always friendly, welcoming, and happy to help with recommendations, which makes the whole dining experience even more enjoyable.

Overall, Sir Manong’s holds a special place for us. It’s not just about the food — it’s about the memories we’ve made there. If you’re looking for consistently great Filipino food in Sydney, this is the branch we’d recommend every time.

If you’re missing Filipino food, this place is a must-visit. The food is comforting, flavourful, and really hits that home-style Filipino taste. I also loved the ambiance—warm, relaxed, and welcoming.

The only downside is the service, which could be improved, as the staff sometimes forget to accommodate requests for utensils and plates.

Overall, still a great spot for Filipino food, especially when you’re craving something familiar and hearty. 🇵🇭🍽️

Sir Manong Filipino Cuisine brings a true taste of the Philippines to the table, with food that is undeniably tasty and full of flavor. The dishes capture the richness and heartiness of Filipino cooking—savory meats, comforting soups, and well-seasoned classics that make the restaurant a worthwhile stop for anyone craving authentic flavors.

🍴 Food

The highlight here is definitely the food. Each dish is prepared with care, offering that satisfying balance of sweet, sour, and savory that defines Filipino cuisine. From grilled specialties to stews and rice plates, the menu is generous, delicious, and sure to leave you feeling full and happy.

🪑 Seating & Atmosphere

That said, the seating isn’t the most comfortable, and the overall atmosphere can feel a bit cramped. Add to that the fact that the space often gets noisy, and it may not be the best place for those looking for a calm or cozy dining experience.

👩‍🍳 Service

The staff are friendly, greeting you warmly and creating a welcoming vibe. However, they are not always very helpful, and service can sometimes feel inattentive or rushed. It’s not unfriendly, but it doesn’t add much to the dining experience either.

⸻

✅ In short: Sir Manong Filipino Cuisine delivers where it matters most—the food is tasty and authentic—but the comfort, noise level, and service could be improved. It’s a place best enjoyed if you’re going mainly for the flavors rather than the setting.

A wise Filipino once told me that if you feel a little bit dizzy after a massive Filipino meal, you'll know that food hit good! Something about high blood pressure from too much crispy pork 😅. That was my colleague from work who told me that, and I put her theory out to the test!
Many Filipinos have told me they love the food at Sir Manong in Eastern Creek. If there's two must order dishes here for me, they'd be the crispy pata and the BBQ chicken. Crispy pata is a twice-cooked pork knuckle, which is first stewed in seasoning and spices before being deep-fried to make the skin as crispy as possible. A mountain of crackling gets served to your table, enclosing layers of soft, flavourful meat underneath. The BBQ chicken is marinated and brined before being cooked over charcoal on a rotating spit. The meat turns out incredibly juicy, but I'd say the best part of the chicken is the tangy, savoury crispy skin. Both dishes get served with pickled red cabbage and a soy vinegar dipping sauce. These were just a joy to eat, and the sweet-and-sour fireworks in your mouth from both are definitely worth the temporary pressure rise 😂
Some dishes are done traditionally here, while others have a modernised touch. Take the sisig for example - this was my first time seeing sisig with a soft-cooked egg and extra crackling on top. I probably do prefer it on the usual big oval sizzling plate though. The bottom layer would get nice and crispy as the meat cooks further in its own fat. I loved the pork binagoongan fried rice! This was my first time trying it, and wow, the flavours on this one were intense. It's fried in the wok with fermented krill and, I think shrimp paste. The fried rice has smokey shellfish flavour throughout, almost like there was crab and prawn meat in it. It's served with eggplant and crackling skin twice-cooked pork belly on top. Another +1 on the pressure train, but again so very delicious it's forgiveable.
P.S. The calamansi juice here is a must. It's uber refreshing after all of that fried pork, you'll need it!
📍 Sir Manong, Eastern Creek

name: Sizzling Fillo Lidcombe
address: 36 Railway St, Lidcombe NSW 2141, Australia
description: Traditional Filipino eats are served in a quaint & colorful space with a karaoke bar.
reviews: The food at the restaurant is quite decent. I particularly enjoy the presentation of the turon - the way it's plated makes it very appealing, and it's also delicious to eat. The palabok is also quite tasty. The pork in the pork sinigang is tender and flavorful. I really hope that sisig can serve super sizzling and really crispy sisig.

Overall 7/7
A diverse menu with authentic dishes approved by some filo friends.
Also fantastic value for money.
So many of the dishes go fantastic with rice so recommend getting a big crowd and ordering a ton to share.
Highlight is the fried plate of meat (photo 4) and the pork knuckle.

Overall the food is good but not excellent. I haven't tried Phillipines cuisine before so I am not sure if the food here is authentic. We tried chicken aboro, fried milkfish, tamarind soup (with prawns) and all of them were okay. The Thai red tea was below average unfortunately. It was not hot enough so when I poured the milk in it quickly became cold, and it tasted so ordinary. (Milk and tea like you dip lipton black tea tea bag in boiled water and add some full cream milk, it tasted like that)

I haven't been here in a little while and last time I was here I had a boodle. Returned to just have a simple meal and it was a great decision.

The food was all good. Sizzling sisig and crispy pata were all really done well. I really have to come here more often especially to try the halo halo and flan.

The garlic rice is a must everytime you come here. I could eat it with nothing and it would still be great.
Good food but not the best. Crispy pata and turon and sago were the top ones. Serving is small especially kare-kare.

name: Smoky Cravings Campsie
address: 325 Beamish St, Campsie NSW 2194, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Amazing staff, Beautiful food
Never had Filipino BBQ before but it was great for a first time would definitely recommend giving it a go
I had the M3 - BBQ Skewers
Your choice of 3 Skewers, Adobe Rice, Little chilli dipping sauce, Soup and a drink for $22 after trying the different Skewers I loved thw pork and got another 2 more 😆 🤣 😂
Highly recommend 👌 👍

The food is delicious, fresh, and prepared with all hygiene protocols. The flavors are amazing, the restaurant is spacious with good ventilation, and the customer service is excellent. Simply delicious.

So far, this is the best Filipino restaurant I have tried. (I still need to visit Rooty Hill to try other Filipino restaurants.) The food took a bit longer to arrive, but they were busy with in-house orders and takeaway deliveries. I wish they would add more yam ice cream and use finer shaved ice in the Special Halo-Halo dessert 😊

Absolutely love this place! The food is always delicious. The BBQ is packed with flavor and cooked perfectly every time. The staff are friendly and welcoming, and the atmosphere is warm and inviting. I’ve even been to their kitchen, it’s spotless clean and very well maintained, which really shows in the quality of the food. Definitely one of the best Filipino BBQ spots around. Highly recommended!

Bubbles and I had been wanting to eat here, and we're glad we finally did. The chicken was tender and well-flavored, and the adobo rice was a great addition. We also tried some skewers (pork and beef) and were not disappointed. The spring rolls were fresh and crispy. We will definitely be back to try more!

name: Smoky Cravings Kings Cross
address: 82/94 Darlinghurst Rd, Potts Point NSW 2011, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Half the food they don’t even have on a Friday lunch?? No chicken, no adobo rice, no sisig, no selected skewers. Food and flavour was good, but we came from Perth and wanted to try a local filo place in Sydney. Followed the reviews but left disappointed

Filipino Food is very delicious and dessert halo halo is very tasty 😋
Service staffs was very friendly and helpful.

Very glad that they have the city branch. Their BBQs are an absolute must try as they are delicious and authentic Filipino flavours. The halo halo of course is a must to finish off a great meal. Friendly staff, neat place and great location.
I always come back here for the grilled pork liempo—so good with that vinegar! The adobo rice and soup on the side hit the spot too. My go-to whenever I miss Filipino food. ❤️

Ate here on a Sunday for lunch.

The food is 5/5. The sauce on the skewers was amazing and like crack. I want more.

However the service was poor. When ordering food at the counter the women taking our order, she stopped serving us and answered the phone which was pretty rude. Also we asked where the closest bathroom was and she didn't know. When she gave us the food, the chicken adobe was missing.

6 x skewers and chicken Adobe = $30

name: Smoky Cravings Parramatta
address: Shop 1/20 Victoria Rd, Parramatta NSW 2150, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We went here weekday lunch just before lunch hour rush.wr got our order fast ordered crispy pork kare2x  comes with soup and drink. It's ok
Tried their pork BBQ stick. It's average and into sweeter marinade. I bought crispy pasta for take away its big bone  but looks like I only got half the meat.

Definitely the extraordinary for Filipino food, it's a small eatery located above Cole's on Victoria road, plenty of spots to park especially after hours inside or street parking. Great variety of BBQ flavors, enough to feed your family. The food tower is $150 included with garlic rice and soup which arrived within 10mins of ordering. Pork crackling here is bomb 💣do not forget the Halo Halo.

I'll return for sure bringing family and friends for food when I'm not bothered feeding them all. One disclaimer is there is no toilet on the premise but you'll have a sink to wash your hands.

Recommended to you all Parramatta folks. 🎉🍲🥂💥🇵🇭

First time dining and we tried M1,M2 and M3 combos. We also ordered kwek-kwek, chicken isaw, sisig and halohalo. Loved their food but not really with the sisig cos its missing the wow factor we are looking for. The quantity was generous and they served the food quickly. We also bought some sweets for take away, we loved the ube cake, letche flan and chicken macaroni salad. Staffs were very friendly and accomodating. Highly recommend to try their food especially the sweets🤩

The filo flavours here are incredible—so rich and satisfying. The prices are on the higher side but it was worth it, the staff are lovely. Be prepared to wait as the place can get busy. Highly recommend !!

The food is outstanding, bursting with flavor and expertly prepared. Each dish showcases a great balance of spices and ingredients, making every bite a satisfying experience.

Staffs are friendly too!

The ambiance is warm and inviting. Overall, it is a fantastic choice for anyone looking to satisfy their filo food cravings with delicious food and great service. Highly recommended!

Masarap po!

name: Smoky Cravings Tempe
address: 884 Princes Hwy, Tempe NSW 2044, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Filipino BBQ Vibes!
The food is absolutely masarap!
Service was brillaint!
Chef and staff were welcoming and friendly!
The place looks really fresh and clean!
10/10 would recommend and will be back for more!

All in all, it was a pleasant dinner experience. The Filo food was excellent and very remarkable. If you've been craving Filipino street cuisine, Tempe Smoky Cravings is a great choice for a classy evening out or a special occasion. I highly recommend it.

The pig ears were deli, bbq pork skewers were well flavored and not overly salty. Yum! Located beside Harry’s on wheels, convenient spot with parking available. Dine in and takeaway friendly! Lovely service too.

Was looking for a place to eat randomly in Tempe and found this Filipino restaurant which did skewers, right next to Harry's! The soup of the day had generous portion of beef innards, addictive but a bit oily. Probably best to eat with rice. The skewers were cooked well overall and the chicken was tender. Wasn't really a fan of the dessert as it didn't use fresh fruit. However, it was very popular. It gets quite busy. Remember to throw your own rubbish and sort the tray and red baskets on the table next to the bin. Overall, not too bad!

Filipino street food right next to Harry's in Tempe! We ordered a couple of skewers and fish balls and enjoyed both. The skewers are grilled fresh to order so be prepared for a little wait. We will definitely be back to try other dishes! Highly recommended!

name: St Luke the Evangelist Catholic Church
address: 1 Beaconsfield St, Revesby NSW 2212, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: This parish embodies the best of Australian Multiculturalism and the universal Church. I’m not Filipino but I went for the epic featival of Santo Nino de Cebu - where parishioners went ALL OUT. At lunch my table had one lady from Mauritius, one from Indonesia, one with Irish heritage and 2 Filipinos born in Australia. The performances had children from Tonga, Indian, Lebanese. It was generously inclusive, fun and welcoming. Inside the Church, there very clear signs detailing their devotional Saints and their connection with the Parish. A very edifying parish of the Catholic faith community.

St Luke’s Revesby is an amazing church with a warm community making all feel welcome. There are many services including Tuesday nights etc. Confession is available before most masses, with adoration and/or rosary, depending on season.
Beautiful Church with massive statues and convenient for anyone hoping to experience adoration!
Great spiritual community experience.Highly recommend.

Fr. Dariusz was kind, shaking hands with his people. He will clearly gentle ,enthusiastic sharing  all about the bible, your fully awake if you sleep, After reading the Gospel, he came out and there explained to you the meaning of the God Words, giving us examples of how to understand, i liked  it when he links it to families, connecting into our daily life,respect, our moral and values  I  will love to bring my children as much as i could .

name: St Therese Catholic Church
address: 45 Sutherland St, Rosebery NSW 2018, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: Nice Catholic Church, went for a Mass on Sunday morning.
wow St Therese Catholic Parish looks the very similar to the Saint Christopher's Cathedral in Canberra
My favourite wall. Mother Mary from all over the country

Attended a funeral here on Friday and the church was beautiful. Being of an Italian background myself, it was so nice to listen to a mass partly in Italian and some in English. The priest seemed lovely and the funeral was so respectful and done beautifully.

What can I say? This is my parish; my place of spiritual refuge since I came to Sydney, and settled in Mascot for five and a half years now.
There's always a sense of belongingness for me in this place.
I miss the fellowship lunch every fourth Sunday; after the Filipino Mass.

name: Starlight Bakery
address: 17 Hill End Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Such a cute space in Doonside! Came here for drinks and ended up getting some bites to eat! The guy who served us was lovely and we thank him for his hospitality. Definitely recommended coming here for the sweet and savory options!

The best Basque Cheesecake is sold here!!! ) AAA+++
The texture is just right & the consistency of the cheese is sublime!

5-Star Review – Starlight Bakery
Visited: 12 April 2025

We visited Starlight Bakery with a group of 18, and everyone had a great time! The staff were warm, welcoming, and served us with genuine smiles, which made the experience even better.

This Filipino bakery has been part of the Western Sydney community for over 25 years and is clearly loved by both the Filipino community and many others.

The food and drinks were fresh and delicious. The ube buns were a standout — soft, tasty, and served quickly. Everyone in our group enjoyed what they ordered, from egg pie to mocha roll and refreshing drinks.

There’s a cozy seating area for about 20 people, and even with our large group, the service was smooth and friendly.

Blacktown. September 2025. I've ordered a Cappuccino but it's not a Cappuccino.... it's Flat White   and just chocolate power on the top. Very disappointing Coffee. I didn't finish the coffee as the taste is bad. It made my day bad in the morning.

Such a wonderful cosy place. I originally went in to grab a coffee as i was just passing through and had no intention of grabbing any food. However, when i laid my eyes on their cakes, I couldnt help myself. I bought the most decadent chocolate cake slice which my husband and I fought over the last bite. It wasnt too sweet, it was soft and a big slice. Great customer service aswell.

name: Sto Nino Oriental Mini Mart
address: 10 Queen St, St Marys NSW 2760, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: good store best banh mi you'll ever find in the shop next door
I could always ring if they have what I need prior to coming or if they're not busy I'll give the list for them to gather of what I need to buy so I can come and ready to pay and pick up👍
It is my wife's favourite Filipino store in St Marys.
Filo shops,  easy to access.
Good little shop for Asia foods

name: Suki Kart
address: Shop 3, Ground Floor/25-31 Florence St, Hornsby NSW 2077, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Order received! It was well-packaged and arrived just in time for the weekend—placed the order on Tuesday, and it was delivered by Friday. Excellent customer service, too! They were very responsive when I called with special instructions. Overall, a great experience!

2nd time ordering my cravings here! I am a Suki for sure. I hope you get more variety.
Even though there’s some out of stock items in my order they refunded me straight away.
I hope they get more stocks. Thank you so much! Maraming salamat po! 🧡❤️❤️ Cravings satisfied

Suki Kart has a great variety of items for sale and the prices were reasonable. I purchased an assortment of snacks and drinks. The customer service team provided good prompt communication notifying me that some items were out of stock and asked if I wanted to proceed (with a refund for the out of stock items). Postage and delivery was very fast and I received my items one or two business days after placing my order with express postage, to Brisbane. Overall very happy with my purchase and their service. Will order online again. Thank you! 😊

First time to order online from Suki Kart.
Found the Frozen And Dry goods my family is craving for.
Friendly, fast and easy transaction.
Posted  from NSW to GC (Express mail). Items arrived overnight with ice pack halfway melted and nicely pack.
Thank You ☺️ Mabuhay

I missed Filipino goodies and found Suki Kart online. I am very satisfied with my order and the staffs were really nice. It was easy to navigate on their website and they have a lot of items to choose from. The delivery was fast too. I will order again next time.

name: Suki Kart & Linalyn's Rooty Hill
address: 10 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Had the best customer service experience with Mercy, Julius, and Casandra. They were very warm and welcoming, genuinely interested in helping and understanding what I needed. I also appreciated their honesty when giving recommendations. The store offers great value for money, and it truly feels like a one-stop shop for Filipino finds. The food is fresh every day, which makes it even better. I will definitely be coming back and highly recommend this shop to others.

I've been shopping at this place many times and it's always a great experience. The store is clean, well-organized, and the staff are super friendly and helpful. Their fresh produce section is always stocked with good quality food. Prices are reasonable and they often have great specials. I also love that they support local products. Highly recommend this place if you're looking for a reliable local supermarket!

Nice supermarket with good range of products
Parking outside available
Great product range, nice friendly service.
The best Pinoy store

name: Summer Vibe
address: Rouse Hill NSW 2155, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Great juices and bubble teas - perfect for a summer day. Big fan of watermelon and lychee.
Try the halo halo - a refreshing & unique filipino boba drink.

Great service from an older gentleman. Made my drink perfectly. Then proceeds to check if I needed a bag as well. Great refreshing drink with elements of fresh fruit incorporated. I had their signature mango coconut sago today
Summer vibe is your go to for Taho!

name: Sunny Corner Cafe
address: Shop 1/2 Hill End Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Don’t be fooled by its small size — Eskinita Café, right across from Doonside Station, is big in its offerings and flavours! We came as a party of seven (two families with three kids) and enjoyed a truly satisfying lunch.

The place felt cozy and welcoming, and our table was soon filled with delicious Filipino favourites. The kids devoured the barbecue, garlic rice, crispy pata, and daing na bangus — every dish cooked beautifully. My husband and I especially loved the sisig, pancit, and perfectly cooked chopsuey with fresh, crunchy vegetables.

And of course, no meal is complete without dessert — the kids were thrilled with their generously sized halo-halo, which was as refreshing as it was delicious! Service is topnotch, too - highly recommended.

A level up from X-Eskinita. If you are craving for authentic Lomi and Lugaw, this is the perfect place for you. Such a humble place, and I never felt away from home. Top-notch flavours, servings, and service are impeccable. Kudos to the new owner for bringing these real flavors here. It is a dog-friendly 🐶 and coffee is very good too. ☕️

X-Eskinita, now called Sunny Corner Cafe under new owner/manager. Food is mouth watering and how it looks is  also how it taste - superbly yummilicious! Serving is generous! Its big which I thought I can't finish but I did! "Simot-Sarap"!! Very friendly new owner/manager too!
We will come back for the silog & coffee, we heard a lot of good reviews on this too!
Their tapsilog and tocilog taste good. Some spiced vinegar would have made it perfect. Their halo halo was yummy and a really huge serving even though the sago is missing.

Amazing little place right beside the Train station in Doonside, I ordered a Tomato and cheese roll with caramelised onion in a roll and it blew me away the simplicity of it but the flavours were beautiful and it was filling, great place to get a snack if your walking round the area!

name: Super Pinoy Sari-Sari Store
address: 228 Queen St, St Marys NSW 2760, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Disappointed on my first visit.
picked up kutsinta without checking before paying.

the kutsinta it has mold i was suppose to eat it in the car. So i drove back to return it what a waste of time.
I’ve been coming to this store for a while now and it’s great. Staff are always friendly and welcoming. Great range and it’s always growing in variety. Great prices too.

Highly recommended Filipino store in St Marys! Our favorite pinoy store in Sydney. You can find items here that are not available in other stores. Also, items are much cheaper and the owner is very kind and accommodating. Great shop!
Friendly staff/owners but limited product range.
Great shop, Lovely people, highly recommend

name: Superior Bakeshop & More
address: Carlisle Ave, Mount Druitt NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Came on a Sunday arvo and randomly selected breads including suggestions by the staff. I realise now we should've tried the pork buns but didn't get the chance. The ones we tried weren't particularly good. There was a strong taste of margarine in most of the filling. Many of the breads also had a shade of margarine yellow. But not the pandesal: theirs is made of white flour and on the sweet side, only lightly dipped in breadcrumbs with barely a noticeable "sal" texture. What we will remember and consider returning for is the energy and eagerness of the staff.
Great Filipino bakeshop with some decent bread. Price is fair and the pandesal was very soft. The ensaymada taste good as well.

I just walked past and smelled the nice bread and beautiful music. Decided to walk in. The price of each piece of bread is 1-3$. So I just tried some of them. Everything is so good. I like Butchi (sesame ball), caramel cake, and egg pie. Despite the shop looking old and unattractive, the quality of the food is amazing. Highly recommend.

Absolutely love this bakery I decided to try it because I moved schools and it was at the station I had to wait at for the bus and it just smelt so good so I decided to try it the pork buns are my personal favourite everyday they’re out I get one and I’ve slowly been trying the other stuff as well and all of it I’ve liked I go here multiple times a week when I have money 😻
Best pan-de-sal (bread with salt, but don't let the name fool you - it's a deliciously sweet, yeast risen bread) in Sydney. I bought 5 dozen for 2 adults and 2 kids, and they didn't last us a week!

name: TNB Cafe
address: 1 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews: This Cafe is absolutely delightful! The food, the ambience and the service are fantastic. Would highly recommend their Tnb Breakfast Burger and their Lockdown Chicken Burger. Their "It's Benny Long Day" is also a fresh and delicious twist on a regular eggs benny. Coffees and drinks are great too!  Can't recommend enough

This is such a hidden little gem, walking through feels like you're teleported into a hip Surry Hills café. Service and vibes was high up there, and the food was so good we demolished 4 plates among 3. Coffee orders came out nicely, iced Koji latte was a unique and creative drink - well worth it.

Our order:
Stacked Pancakes
Beef tacos
Sisig pasta
Pork belly scramble

Iced cold brew
Iced Koji latte
Iced hazelnut Oat

disappointing to say that the only good thing about this place is the atmosphere, price, and convenience. i do like the filipino fusion in their cuisine, however for the amount of staff they have on their service is not that great.

i counted and they have 4-5 people on at the front of house, with only one coffee machine, barely assisting customers with their enquiries and instead directing them to the qr code on the table.

additionally, i ordered the avocado toast and it was extremely hard to eat. i was served avocado with a side of toast. please put less avocado next time.

Love this for Rooty Hill. Such a cool cafe that not only serves great coffee but tasty food as well. My partner had the fried chicken Benedict and he is still raving about it 5 days after. It was crispy and salted just right. I broke off a crumb to have a taste and wow. I got the pancake stack and first off, perfect portions - some places do pancakes that are too big so they're doughy and/or don't absorb the butter well. The pancakes here were the right size. But the standout was the bacon I added on. Cut just right so the lean bits were crispy and the fat was just the perfect juicy buttery goodness! It was packed and you can see why. Love.

Oh mate i do miss drinking coffee on the evening!🤣 Thanks Tnb, it was a really good espresso one. At the heart of Rooty hill station. Equip with Slayer Steam, 2 izaga markibar, and mahlkonig ek43. Brew method available espresso, batch brew and handrip.

name: Taguan Cafe
address: 274 Abercrombie St, Darlington NSW 2008, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I got up early to find this place because I was on a hunt for pandesal. Thankfully, they had just baked some fresh ones! I was welcomed by my fellow kabayan mafias and bless their hearts for serving me a fresh brewed coffee from PH. Their cafe is perfect for slow mornings! Come all and come through!

P.S: they’ve got stickers from Magenta house 🥹
Very good Filipino dishes served in this cafe. Albeit we had to wait a while since they run out of beef tapa, chicken tocino and rice! But when they were served, we enjoyed them - tasty.

Literally my favourite café
There are a lot of really great cafes in our lovely Sydney, but nothing quite gets close to this Taguan.

The staff are the most lovely people you could possibly imagine meeting in a cafe that feels more like your family home than a business, filled with so much warmth thanks to all their smiling faces.

Sometimes when you show up they’ve got a new fun drink or food item that you’ve never heard of before, you should definitely give it a go if it sounds even remotely appealing to you. Sometimes it’ll be good, sometimes it’ll be absolutely mind-bogglingly amazing and delicious.

You can’t go wrong with any of their coffees, seriously like just order anything on the menu and you will have a good coffee

I’m a huge fan of the indulgent Ube grilled cheese, but honestly, I haven’t had any food at this place that isn’t handsdown great. Presentation, and taste.

I wish I had time to go here more often, and any time I have the chance to invite someone to come here with me I do.

Everything about this cafe is wonderful! The food is delicious, and the atmosphere is inviting. The service is second to none, regardless of whether you are quickly stopping to grab a coffee or dining alone, they are attentive to your needs.

I won’t pay for over priced or overhyped food, so the fact that I eat here regularly says a lot. They do justice to Filipino food, and I really do get have my cravings satisfied whenever I miss my Mums food.
Our fave cafe in Sydney! Filipino food done CORRECT. Great atmosphere, great food, great service. Highly recommend coming here to get a taste of Filipino food and hospitality

name: Takam, Filipino Food Forward
address: 324 Victoria St, Darlinghurst NSW 2010, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We tried the $98 per person set menu at Takam and it was absolutely worth it. The whole dining experience felt like a well-thought-out journey, with each course flowing nicely into the next. Every dish was beautifully presented and full of flavour, showing a lot of creativity and attention to detail.

The atmosphere was warm and inviting, making it perfect for a relaxed dinner or a special occasion. The staff were friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable, and they made us feel genuinely welcomed throughout the night.

Overall, great food, great service, and great value for money. Highly recommended and definitely keen to come back.

Came to this Filipino restaurant for lunch and it did not disappoint. Staff were very friendly and gave us detailed information on their menu. Calamansi soda and sarsaparilla were both very nice. The next table at a ube thick shake which was very tempting. Started off with the calamari which had nice sour notes to awaken the palate. This was followed by the swordfish which was excellent. Mains of beef, Murray cod with their version of sambal and the chicken were all very good. The crab fat fried rice had the expected umami hit. Leche flan was nice but we were quite full so could not order more dessert. A nice restaurant overall.

An Unforgettable Birthday Celebration at Takam!
We recently celebrated my daughter's birthday at Takam, from the moment we walked in, the warmth of the hospitality was incredible. Takam's friendly and attentive staff made us feel like family from start to finish.
A massive thank you to Patrick and the rest of the floor team; they were so professional and went above and beyond to make the celebration extra special. The service was impeccable-attentive without ever being intrusive.
The food was absolutely delicious! The chefs at Takam are masters of their craft, taking traditional Filipino flavours and giving them a refined, modern twist. The lemongrass chicken inasal was perfectly charred, and the Byron Bay_pork cutlet was a total standout. We loved the Palabok it had the perfect balance of rich shrimp sauce& to finish it off the Leche Flan was delightful.
If you are looking for a place with superb service and top-tier Filipino food for any occasion, look no further than Takam. We can't wait to come back. Maraming Salamat Po

Absolutely 5 stars! Love seeing Filipino food being presented the right way — beautifully cooked, thoughtful, and full of authentic flavour. The service was warm and attentive, and the whole experience felt so welcoming. Takam Sydney truly showcases Filipino cuisine with pride. Highly recommend!

I came to Takam for my birthday lunch with family. I had such a lovely experience & couldn’t recommend them enough. The service was amazing & the food even better. Patrick and the lovely team of talented chefs took great care of us. 5 stars ⭐️

name: Tambayan Pinoy Variety Store
address: 513 Burwood Rd, Belmore NSW 2192, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.

name: Tarro Foods
address: Liberty Servo Doonside, 13/15 Doonside Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Lovely, home-style food that really reminded me of home. We visited on Australia Day 2026 and they were open with normal trading hours.

I ordered the Chicken Siomai, Chicken Feet, and an iced tea, while my partner ordered the Overload Silog. Unfortunately, they had run out of the pork and shrimp siomai, so I opted for the chicken instead.

They were extremely busy, which is understandable given how popular they are. Even though the drive was only about two minutes, we waited close to an hour for our food. Next time, I’ll try calling ahead and possibly pre-ordering if that’s allowed, which might help with the wait.

We ordered takeaway, and the siomai came in a paper bowl without a lid. By the time we got home, the paper had stuck to the siomai, and some of it had also stuck to the paper bag, so I had to peel part of it off before eating. The siomai came with soy sauce or vinegar; it would be great to also have an option like lemon or lime to add a bit of citrus.

That said, the food itself was great, and we would definitely come back. The service was friendly and welcoming, and the man who answered the phone was very helpful. They were clearly busy because they’re popular, and I really hope they continue to grow, improve their packaging, and streamline service as they get bigger.

Overall, a great experience with a few small areas for improvement.

We drove over an hour craving Vign Empanadas after watching so many TikTok reviews and came across Tarro, and I knew I had to try them. I’m so glad we made the drive because it absolutely did not disappoint. Everything we ordered was exceptional, the prices were super reasonable for the quality, and the service was honestly 10/10. Worth every minute of the drive,  we’ll definitely be back!

Decided to give these guys a try after seeing a Instagram review

we order a few things on the menu and we're a little disappointed

I have eaten Filipino food many times usually home cooked at friends places but I found that the food at this place was not to my liking.

The bbq meats were over marinated (salty) and the fried stuff just a little to bland for my taste but usually filo food relies heavily on their dipping sauces which were nice.

The ube and Pandan drink was okay I wasn't a fan of the balls in the drink but the liquid was tasty

The only dish that I enjoyed was the empondas they were tasty and crispy but a little oily but still enjoyable

They told us the chicken inasal was small and needed a skewer to pair with it, but when it arrived, it was massive. We got scammed in the best way. 🤣

Definitely coming back. Best comfort food and the best Filipino food stall on the west side! 👌🏼

Try the chicken inasal, it was so goooood! 🤤

The filo flavours here are incredible—so rich and satisfying, I find myself craving it all the time. The prices are fair, the staff are lovely, and the outdoor seating adds a great vibe to the whole experience. Highly recommend!!!!

name: Tatak Pinoy Land Down Under
address: Level 2B/45/51 Main St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Had all the Filipino items you could think of, when it comes to common necessities. They even Baro’t saya and Barong.

Honesty the best filo goods store in Sydney, and the staff are so welcoming and happy to describe each product.

10/10+ recommend if looking to try or see unique and authentic filo goods.
The produce here is quite good, and well priced, the customer service is good as well. If you are looking for a wide variety of filo foods, and products. Including a Filo ice cream cart you can hire.
Friendly staff....reasonable prices....sells beautiful native handbags too ! 👍💖
prices very cheap and good value, friendly manager and overall a great customer experience

name: The Bangketa Grocer - Filipino shop
address: shop 1/35 Lawson St, Penrith NSW 2750, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Fantastic neighborhood grocery store! The shop is always clean, well-organized, and stocked with fresh produce. The staff are incredibly friendly and always willing to help you find what you need. Prices are fair, and I love the variety of local and specialty items they carry. It’s become my go-to place for weekly shopping. Highly recommended!

Quick delivery
Lots of supplies

I keep coming back for the delmondo corned beef.
I also love spicy garlic, they have 3 versions to choose from.
I’ve also discovered new spicy vinegar that tops pinakurat.
Best place to order quality goods!
Our go-to for Filipino favorites! Amazing selection of authentic Pinoy products, from classic snacks to essential frozen goods, and has a reasonable price. Absolutely highly recommended!
Very friendly staff will bend over backwards just to help you! Pricing also very competitive and low comparing with bigger Asian stores. Thanks Bangketa Grocer 🙏🏼
Easy access, multiple parking. It's a one stop store,from buying your Filipino ingredients or delicacies to sending balikbayan boxes and at the same time It's an easy padala to your loveones in Pinas

name: The Baristas' Shed
address: 20/9 Hollinsworth Rd, Marsden Park NSW 2765, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We visited The Baristas’ Shed (20/9 Hollinsworth Rd, Marsden Park) in the evening and had such a great experience. Even though it was quite late, the service was still warm and welcoming, and the atmosphere felt calm yet lively—just perfect for an evening catch-up with friends.

The barista on duty made us some of the best coffee we’ve had in a long time. You could tell right away that he takes real pride in what he does—the coffee was rich, smooth, and perfectly balanced with a creamy texture on top. It’s not easy to find a place that serves such consistently good coffee even at the end of the day, but this café absolutely delivered.

The interior of the café is modern and stylish, with warm lighting and a cozy layout that makes you feel comfortable the moment you walk in. We decided to sit outside, and it turned out to be a great choice—the outdoor seating area is spacious, peaceful, and has a nice evening breeze that added to the whole experience. It’s a spot where you can relax, chat, and enjoy your drink without feeling rushed.

What really stood out was how friendly and accommodating the staff were. Despite the late hour, they served us with genuine smiles and made sure everything was just right. The overall vibe of the café is welcoming and relaxed, making it a great place whether you’re grabbing a quick coffee or spending time with friends.

All in all, The Baristas’ Shed is a gem in Marsden Park—fantastic coffee, great ambience, and staff who truly care about what they do. We left feeling refreshed and happy, and we’ll definitely be returning soon for another round of their amazing coffee and good vibes.

From the moment I walked in, I was greeted by Simon at the counter. Undecided on what drink to get he recommended his favourite was Mango Drop. Great choice Simon! So refreshing especially for such a hot day like today.

The beautiful lady Dani arrived with the dishes.  She was so sweet & friendly.
Now let’s talk about the food. Talk about delicious...
I got the Filipino Longanisa and the Fairy Floss Waffle. Absolutely Amazing !! Loved both dishes.

The staff is what really has me coming back. The atmosphere is very welcoming. Shoutout to Simon & Dani thank you for the amazing service.

Outstanding Coffee, Creative Menu, and Warm Service Every Time

I visited The Baristas’ Shed (20/9 Hollinsworth Rd, Marsden Park) yesterday and left very impressed. From the moment I stepped inside, I felt the welcoming and positive vibe that this café is clearly known for. The staff are genuinely warm and friendly, making it a place where you feel like more than just a customer.

A special thank you goes to Heema, the lovely barista who made my cappuccino. She greeted me with a smile and prepared one of the smoothest, most perfectly balanced cappuccinos I’ve had in a long time. Rich, full-bodied coffee with just the right texture of foam—it was the kind of cup that makes you stop and appreciate how much care went into it. That personal touch made my visit stand out.

The menu deserves its own spotlight. What I really liked is how it goes beyond the basics—yes, they do the café classics, but each dish is given a fresh, modern twist. From avo on toast loaded with green peas, golden beets, wakame seaweed, and chia lemon, to the hearty breaky bruschetta layered with mushroom ragout, kale, Persian feta, and a perfectly poached egg, it’s clear that thought and creativity go into every plate. And for anyone with a sweet tooth, the mango & pineapple toast with berries, lemon balm, coconut sorbet, and macadamia soil is both refreshing and indulgent. It’s the kind of menu where you know you’ll want to come back just to try something new each time.

The atmosphere is equally appealing. Indoors, there’s a warm and cozy feel with background music and a lively buzz, while the outdoor seating makes it a great spot to relax and enjoy the fresh air. It feels versatile—you could comfortably bring a laptop and do some work, meet friends for a casual brunch, or simply sit quietly with a good coffee.

Service is efficient without ever feeling rushed. Drinks and meals come out quickly, but the staff still take the time to engage and make you feel looked after. That balance of speed and genuine hospitality is something I really value in a café.

I’ve uploaded some photos from my visit, so be sure to check them out if you’re curious about the presentation—it’s as inviting on the plate as it is on the menu.

All in all, The Baristas’ Shed has everything I look for in a café: fantastic coffee, a fresh and inventive menu, friendly and attentive staff, and a welcoming environment that makes you want to return. I’ll definitely be back, and next time I’ll be trying more of those creative dishes alongside another cappuccino from Heema—highly recommended.
Absolutely loved my experience! The atmosphere was welcoming, and Jobert was so attentive. Perfect spot for a quick break! The service was speedy, and the food was delicious.

Tried the Veg Peri Peri Burger and Dubai Chocolate Pancakes, and honestly, both were amazing! The burger had just the right amount of spice and was super satisfying. And those pancakes… so rich and chocolatey, they felt like a real treat. Definitely coming back for more!😋

name: The Gospel Church of Jesus
address: 42 Kurrajong Ave, Mount Druitt NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: Filipino church. Lively worship and good worship music.
Vibrant church with great singing to enjoy friendly

The Gospel Church of Jesus has been a wonderful partner to work alongside, and it has been our privilege at Your Funeral Director to conduct funeral services within this caring and faith-filled community.

From the moment families arrive, they are welcomed with warmth, compassion, and genuine Christian fellowship. The church provides a peaceful and respectful environment where loved ones can gather to honour, remember, and celebrate a life well lived.

The pastoral care, support, and professionalism shown by the church leadership and congregation help create meaningful services that bring comfort and reassurance during one of life's most difficult times. Their willingness to work collaboratively with families and funeral professionals ensures every service is conducted with dignity, reverence, and care.

We are grateful for the trust, kindness, and hospitality consistently extended to the families we serve and to the YFD Cadre.

Thank you to The Gospel Church of Jesus for helping create a space where faith, love, and community come together to support families when they need it most.

— Nicky Myers & the YFD Cadre
Your Funeral Director

name: The Greener Grocer
address: 14/832 Anzac Parade, Maroubra NSW 2035, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: Cheaper fresh produce. International goods available.

Great place for decent priced produce. The wide selection of popular asian snacks & brazillian products are my fav. Lee from the store Also , introduce me to some philippine snacks which has been my favourite in my cookings.

Nice, fresh fruits and veggies with lots of variety. Also, a huge range of Asian (Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Southeast Asian), European and Mediterranean foods and products. Before COVID, they used to do food samples on weekends which was fun.

Best International Indepentend Shop in the Eastern Suburbs
Everything is very fresh at very affordabel prices.
The Staff is super helpful and frendly
Go at support this Grocer
You wont regret it.
My absolute favourite place to get fruits and vegies. Also great at supplying international goods. Best!

name: The Kamayan
address: 55 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Best traditional Filo food I’ve had in Sydney. It has carenderia feels and the food tastes exactly how they are back home.

My go to dishes are dinuguan, pakbet, pork soft bones bbq, pork bbq, lechon paksiw and daing na bangus.

Probably the best Filo food in the Rooty Hill area. I say that because it reminds me of my Mum’s home cooked meals, the flavours are just right, not too sweet or salty. When it comes to the quality they don’t hold back on the meat, often times Filo restaurants uses too much fat and not enough meat. It’s definitely worth trying if you’re around this area.

We only went here today, and our Filipino friend told us that the food was delicious. I was surprised by the pork binagoongan because it had pineapple and wasn’t oily at all—it was very dry. As for the kare-kare, you could barely taste the peanut butter and the sauce wasn’t thick.

The chicken inasal we got for takeaway was flavorful, and the vinegar was nicely seasoned. The service was good, the staff were kind, and the place was clean.

In the heart of Rooty Hill there lies a Filipino restaurant. I thought I’d give it a go. Delicious flavours and friendly customer service. Even the locals were friendly in terms of recommending dishes from the local restaurant. It’s a shame that the dishes aren’t all displayed so it’s difficult to know what’s on offer. Lines move so fast to the front that you’re not given enough time to decide what you want. Staff were patient though.
In terms of food, I’d highly recommend the Pork Skewers, Halo Halo and the Tocilog.
It’s a shame that, for dine in, our dishes came out fairly lukewarm so it ruined our dining experience. Got staff to reheat it though. Tables and chairs need a bit of a clean.
It’s worth checking out if you’re ever nearby.

Food was great. I loved the fresh choices and the char grilled chicken and leimpo.

The staff were very friendly as well.

Glad I got to experience this place and the food. Will definitely come back when I'm around the area.

name: Tindahan Filipino-Asian Grocery
address: 8a/103 Junction St, Nowra NSW 2541, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
Amazing selection!!
The family that owns it is so lovely 😍
We loved the little boy who greeted us Koby 😇🥰
Great selection of snacks and flavours. Especially love the fudgee bars.
Very happy and thankful to know you guys are around!☺️ The shop is clean and has a wide selection of products.

name: Tita's Cakes
address: 6/2 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: My family and I personally met Tita and her family, and they are truly wonderful people. They made us feel so welcome and comfortable. The food was absolutely heavenly—every dish was full of flavor and clearly made with love. We will definitely be coming back and highly recommend them to everyone! We ordered a few. Price is reasonable.

Ordered the Christmas bundle for New Year’s Eve - affordable, well thought out, and everything tasted great. There was a small mix-up with my cupcake choice, but when I messaged them they offered a replacement straight away, no questions asked. Just waiting for them to return from the holidays to collect my flan cupcakes 😊

Tita’s Cakes is hands down one of my favourite dessert spots. The cakes are incredibly fluffy and light, never too sweet, and you can really taste the quality of the ingredients. Everything feels authentic and thoughtfully made, using real Filipino flavours without artificial enhancers.
My absolute favourites are the calamansi cheesecake, which has the perfect balance of tangy and creamy, their halo-halo which is the best I've ever had and honestly anything ube on the menu. Everything tastes truly homemade, made from scratch with so much care, yet still feels premium and special. Highly recommend if you love Filipino desserts

The cupcakes were all delicious! My Christmas puto bumbong and bibingka cravings were satisfied.

And Hayden was so warm and gracious.

Drove more than an hour just for their bibingka! Worth it!!

I bought this box- 2 bibingka, 2 nilupak, 2 brazo de mercedes. Everything's good! Taro latte was wow too- must try!

name: Tong Li Supermarket Cabramatta
address: shop 16/193 Railway Pde, Cabramatta NSW 2166, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: the store is quiet and easy to navigate but the cashiers make me feel rushed and just watch you when you dont know how to use the machine to pay. even if they do help they do it in a tone that makes me feel like i need to rush and that they are annoyed to do their job. even using the machine can take awhile but they move on to the next costumer, yes to improve efficiency but im still paying and it just makes me not want to come back:(

Very poor customer service at the cashier. If I were the manager, I would seriously review the performance of the two ladies staffs members working there. I will replace them. 😱😱😱
I am a regular customer and I live nearby. When I tried to pay for an additional product they put outside of the shop,, the first cashier asked me to wait. I then went to the second cashier, who sent me back to the first one. The experience was very frustrating, so I decided to leave.!!!
Has a lot of variety of Asian groceries. I go there to get snacks that I used to eat as a kid growing up in cabra

best place to look for some yummy ice cream. this shop sells Pinoy Sorbetes or Filipino ice cream.. i comes in avocado, ube (purple yam), ube macapuno, cheese aka keso and so on.

one of the best ice cream ever at 13.50.

thank you tongli Cabramatta for importing such wonderful product.. this is one of the reason on why I go to Cabramatta..
Very good big selection good store but hidden location

name: Tong Li Supermarket Liverpool
address: Shop 1008/1009 Westfield Liverpool, Macquarie St, Liverpool NSW 2170, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Bought this coconut water second time and can’t get them open. Very frustrated

Great place, competitive prices and large selection of asian foods and this branch not only contains the usual Chinese/Vietnamese/Thai groceries. This branch also stocks Filipino and indonesian groceries as well. They have fresh vegetables as well and frozen food.

They take eftpos/cash and also accept Amex as well.

Very unprofessional owner(?) or employee. Guy with glasses and Grey hair swearing at Indian customer all because he wanted a new drink. He even paid for the new one. I get he was making a scene from saying the drink was spoiled but he didn't need to start swearing in front of children
Has self heating food....very cool
My choice for a huge variety of Chinese, Japanese, Vietnamese and Korean products, groceries, fresh vegetables and frozen goods. Highly recommended.

name: Tong Li Supermarket Rockdale
address: Rockdale Plaza, Shop 37/1 Rockdale Plaza Dr, Rockdale NSW 2216, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: Have been here a couple times and cashier has always been lovely. I am able to find most of the products I need here. However the other day I saw one of the staff chuck in date tofu on the floor and then put it back in the fridge. I know they're all still wrapped in packages but wish they would be more careful with the food. If you get food from this shop make sure you wipe things down when you get home.
New Asian supermarkets offer a wide range of products, including Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Vietnamese, and Filipino items, to name a few. The store is new and spacious. I would definitely come back.

Premium Lamb was off! It didn’t smell right when it hit the heat and sure enough it tasted off too. Ruined dinner.
Tong Li has no online contact for reporting defective products, so the one star should help.
Very clean and well organised store. Great selection, more than reasonable prices, and great service.

The store is always clean, well organised, and fully stocked with fresh produce and a great selection of everyday essentials. The staff are friendly and helpful. I really appreciate how easy it is to find what I need, and the checkout process is always quick and efficient. Prices are reasonable, and they often have great specials and discounts. Definitely my go to supermarket.

name: Triple H 100.1 FM
address: 25 Edgeworth David Ave, Hornsby NSW 2077, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews: I was a guest on SHOWBIZ BUZZ WITH PEARL GARDINER on 28th July 2015. Along with musician Josie Critter from Mad Cowboy Disease band, we introduced our collaborative song SITUATION NOT NORMAL to the world. The song was written by Warren Rodwell ; Composed and performed by Mad Cowboy Disease. It tells of kidnapping by terrorists in the southern Philippines. Thanks to Pearl for being such a great host and interviewer. I look forward to our next scheduled radio interview.
Quiz  Winner of Monday Kick Off with Shamal, Zachariah Fenn receiving the Event Cinama movie pases from Hugh Mulgrey a Director of Triple H FM. Awesome effort Zac. #ShamalTennkoon
Pumping out tunes to the Hornsby area since we were kids!
Very well run Community radio station
Support community run radio. Thanks Ben!

name: Vietnamese Delicious Cafe and Rolls
address: 2 Gymea Bay Rd, Gymea NSW 2227, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Chicken vietnamese roll. I was so much looking forward to try it. Yes, it was good, but could not taste the pate, other than that it tasted very fresh, delicious. Didn't have time to take picture, we were so hungry :))
Best noodle salad Ive had in a while!! Really filling and fresh. Lovely service too.

Great location to sit and eat a pork roll, pho etc

Tried vermicelli pork with spring roll. It was OK the iceberg lettuce was unusual in the mix instead of viet herbs like Parilla. Lots of mint which i like but pork had no character/texture as if it had been soaked in MSG?

Perfectly fine pork roll but not crunchy roll as I like it. All the food was good but just not remarkable in any way other than sitting in sun to consume. Easy parking also.

My go-to Vietnamese lunch in the Shire. Good indoor and outdoor sitting area, and amazing crackling pork belly. Good rice paper rolls, vermicelli salad and pork rolls. Just wished there's chilli oil for the pho.

Ordered 2 phos and 2 vermicelli salads, which were both absolutely delicious! Quantitatively, it filled me up and are both satisfyingly healthy options for lunch. Loved the sriracha sauce and the very spicy chilies! Would honestly have this everyday if I could

name: Wow Filipino Food
address: Rowe St, Eastwood NSW 2122, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Friendly and smiley customer service at their Burwood Chinatown branch. The barbecue skewers and the pork belly have that perfect sweet and tangy flavours: definitely a must try! Be careful as you'll need to order extra rice. 😅
First time trying Wow Filipino food in Burwood.  Tried their sisig , it taste very authentic and well packaged, excellent service too. Will definitely come back! :)
Absolutely the best Filipino barbecue in Sydney. My kids love it too.  Highly recommended!!!
The best gulaman, pig’s ear and grilled pork. Great service and super friendly staff. Will definitely come back again
You MUST check them out! Their service is absolute a star. Special thanks to Kian, from Burwood Chinatown. You have to try their signature pork sisig, pork belly and chicken Maryland Is my favourite.

name: Woy woy Sari sari Filipino store
address: 32 Blackwall Rd, Woy Woy NSW 2256, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews: Feels like home I love the name of the store, Sari sari Store, famous in the Philippines. Happy  I got my ube icecream.Will be back again  and again  and also happy refer to my friends.
The new owners Thess and Greg are so friendly! I love that they stock fresh pandesal. Definitely coming back to stock up on Filipino goods.
Amazing selection! Such a well presented store. The owner was super welcoming and informative.
great filipino grocery selection and friendly people :)

The lady at the store barely gets off of her phone but she later entertained us as my partner is Filipina and had some chit chats anyways they have most common goods essential to Filipinos here. Over all, wishing for better customer service

name: Yummylicious Cafe
address: Shop 14/179 Coogan Pl, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I’ve ordered two cakes (ube and ube leche flan) so far and both were amazing. I ended up ordering another one for my niece’s birthday which is the Mango one, looking forward to try it. I’ve been on the hunt for a really good sylvanna, and this is exactly the one I was looking for. I haven’t tried their breakfast options yet, but I’m definitely looking forward to it. The owner, Aileen, is very accommodating too. A great spot if you’re craving Filipino food and desserts.

Stumbled upon this filo restaurant and we keep coming back. Meals are cheaply priced but tastes and looks good!
Iced flavoured coffees were too sweet for my liking, but pairs great with the lunch menu.

Such a relaxing place to wind down, and the tastiest food. I had the Tapsilog, and my goodness, what a treat! The garlic rice and fried eggs are the perfect addition to the sweetness of the beef marinade, and the freshness of cucumber and tomatoes washes it down perfectly. A taste bud explosion! The service is fantastic too, friendly people that genuinely care about the presentation of their food, the quality of the ingredients and the care of their customers and staff. I feel very fortunate to have come across such a hidden gem in Campbelltown. I also love that they are open at 7:30am, it means I can come and relax before my busy day begins. Highly recommend this place to anyone who wants a unique tasty foodie experience in Campbelltown ❤️

The team at yummylicious are amazing. From the chef who cooks incredible food to the family feel welcome from the service staff. The food options are endless but I can never pass up their traditional Filipino breakfast and ice coffee. 100% recommend this hidden gem in Campbelltown.

We waited patiently for our order to come.  Alas, after 45 minutes, we have our tapsilog and bangussilog. The tapa was rubbery, eggs overcooked,sinangag cold and the cucumber not fresh :( Bangus was undercooked :(. They also forgot our coffee which we followed up after 30 minutes when we saw others who went after us served first:( They have warnings about mistakes , second chances and grace written inside the cafe, however, we do expect to have decent breakfast at least.  Not coming back.
